Still wine around the world

721 editor-picked still producers across 16 regions.

A guide to still wine worth seeking out, by region.

Still wine in Alsace

Maison Trimbach ★ 5.0

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Geisberg, Osterberg, Rosacker)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot Blanc, Pinot NoirFounded 1626Trimbach familyWinemaker Pierre Trimbach (cellar) with Anne and Julien TrimbachRibeauville-Hunawihr-Bergheim

Thirteen-generation Ribeauville house founded in 1626, source of Clos Sainte-Hune Riesling from the Rosacker Grand Cru and a long-standing reference for bone-dry Alsace Riesling.

Tasting: Cellar visits and seated tastings by appointment at the Ribeauville maison; reservations through the family website.

Signature wines: clos-sainte-hune, frederic-emile, reserve-personnelle

Tip: Tastings are by appointment; allow time to walk up to the Rosacker hill above Hunawihr where Clos Sainte-Hune sits.

15 Route de Bergheim, 68150 Ribeauville, France → Book →

Famille Hugel ★ 4.9

Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Schoenenbourg, Sporen) and Vendanges Tardives / Selection de Grains NoblesRiesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir30 haFounded 1639Hugel family (Marc, Marc-Andre and Jean-Frederic Hugel)Riquewihr-Zellenberg

Founded in 1639, the 13th-generation Riquewihr house that led the 1984 reform giving Alsace its Vendanges Tardives and Selection de Grains Nobles laws.

Tasting: Cellar tours and seated tastings at the Riquewihr house; appointments preferred.

Signature wines: schoenenbourg, vendanges-tardives-gewurztraminer, gentil

Tip: The Hugel Schoenenbourg Riesling sits below the family's cellars; the village walking route passes the foot of the Grand Cru.

3 Rue de la Premiere Armee, 68340 Riquewihr, France → Book →

Leon Beyer ★ 4.7

Alsace AOC (dry-style focus)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot BlancFounded 1580Beyer family (Marc and Yann Beyer)Eguisheim-Husseren

Eguisheim winegrowers since 1580, the house that built the dry-style reputation of Alsace Gewurztraminer and Riesling and supplies many French three-star tables.

Tasting: Tastings by appointment at the Eguisheim house, central to the village ring.

Signature wines: comtes-d-eguisheim-gewurztraminer, comtes-d-eguisheim-riesling, cuvee-particuliere

2 Rue de la Premiere Armee, 68420 Eguisheim, France →

Domaines Schlumberger ★ 4.8

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Kitterle, Kessler, Saering, Spiegel)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir130 haFounded 1810Schlumberger familyBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDGuebwiller

Guebwiller estate spanning 130 hectares including 70 hectares of Grand Cru terraces on Kitterle, Kessler, Saering and Spiegel, a substantial Grand Cru holding under single ownership.

Tasting: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Guebwiller estate; bookings via the website.

Signature wines: kitterle-riesling, kessler-gewurztraminer, saering-riesling

Tip: The Kitterle terraces above the cellar climb at angles up to 50 percent; the cellar visit walks part of the slope before tasting.

100 Rue Theodore Deck, 68500 Guebwiller, France → Book →

Domaine Weinbach ★ 4.9

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Schlossberg, Furstentum, Mambourg)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir31 haFounded 1898Faller family (Catherine, Eddy and Theo Faller)Demeter CertifiedORGANIC CERTIFIEDKaysersberg-Kientzheim-Sigolsheim

The Faller family's Clos des Capucins at the foot of Schlossberg in Kaysersberg, biodynamic since the 2000s and one of the touchstones for Alsace Riesling and Pinot Gris.

Tasting: Tastings by appointment at the Clos des Capucins; cellar visits run through the year.

Signature wines: schlossberg-riesling-cuvee-sainte-catherine, furstentum-gewurztraminer, altenbourg-pinot-gris

Tip: The walled Clos des Capucins surrounds the cellar; visits typically include a walk through the clos before tasting.

25 Route du Vin, 68240 Kaysersberg, France → Book →

Domaine Zind-Humbrecht ★ 5.0

Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Brand, Hengst, Goldert, Rangen) and Selection de Grains NoblesRiesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir40 haFounded 1959Humbrecht familyWinemaker Olivier Humbrecht MWDemeter CertifiedORGANIC CERTIFIEDTurckheim-Niedermorschwihr-Katzenthal

Turckheim estate built from the 1959 union of the Zind and Humbrecht families and run by Olivier Humbrecht MW, holding parcels in Brand, Hengst and the volcanic Clos Saint-Urbain on the Rangen.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Turckheim winery; reservations via the website.

Signature wines: clos-saint-urbain-rangen, brand-riesling, clos-windsbuhl-pinot-gris

Tip: Clos Saint-Urbain sits inside the Rangen amphitheatre of Thann; tastings at the Turckheim cellar walk through every Grand Cru in succession.

4 Route de Colmar, 68230 Turckheim, France → Book →

Domaine Marcel Deiss ★ 4.9

Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Altenberg de Bergheim, Schoenenbourg, Mambourg) and complantation field-blendsRiesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir, Sylvaner32 haFounded 1947Deiss family (Jean-Michel and Mathieu Deiss)Demeter CertifiedECOCERTRibeauville-Hunawihr-Bergheim

Bergheim estate that revived the Alsace climat tradition with co-planted Grand Cru field-blends, biodynamic since 1998 and Demeter certified, run by Mathieu Deiss with his father Jean-Michel.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings at the Bergheim domaine; bookings via the website.

Signature wines: altenberg-de-bergheim, schoenenbourg, burlenberg

Tip: Deiss bottles many of his Grands Crus as field-blends rather than single varietals; the Altenberg de Bergheim cuvee is a thirteen-variety complantation.

15 Route du Vin, 68750 Bergheim, France → Book →

Domaine Albert Boxler ★ 4.9

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Sommerberg, Brand)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir13 haFounded 1673Boxler family (Jean Boxler and son Jean-Marie)Turckheim-Niedermorschwihr-Katzenthal

Single-village Niedermorschwihr estate run by Jean Boxler and his son Jean-Marie, with thirteen hectares almost entirely on the granite Grands Crus Sommerberg and Brand. Allocation only.

Tasting: Cellar tastings strictly by appointment; the estate is small and allocation-driven.

Signature wines: sommerberg-riesling, brand-riesling, sommerberg-pinot-gris

Tip: The Sommerberg climbs straight above the village; book ahead and walk up before tasting.

78 Rue des Trois Epis, 68230 Niedermorschwihr, France →

Domaine Josmeyer ★ 4.7

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Hengst, Brand)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Muscat28 haFounded 1854Meyer family (Celine and Isabelle Meyer)Demeter CertifiedORGANIC CERTIFIEDColmar-Wintzenheim-Wettolsheim

Wintzenheim domaine farmed biodynamically across 28 hectares including Grand Cru Hengst and Brand, run by sisters Celine and Isabelle Meyer.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment; the modern domaine sits in central Wintzenheim.

Signature wines: hengst-riesling, brand-riesling, le-kottabe

76 Rue Clemenceau, 68920 Wintzenheim, France → Book →

Domaine Albert Mann ★ 4.8

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Hengst, Schlossberg, Furstentum, Pfersigberg, Steingrubler, Mambourg)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir24 haFounded 1984Barthelme family (Maurice and Jacky Barthelme)Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDColmar-Wintzenheim-Wettolsheim

Wettolsheim domaine run by brothers Maurice and Jacky Barthelme, biodynamic since 1997 with parcels in six Grands Crus across the Colmar belt and Kaysersberg amphitheatre.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Wettolsheim winery.

Signature wines: schlossberg-riesling, hengst-pinot-noir-grand-h, furstentum-gewurztraminer

13 Rue du Chateau, 68920 Wettolsheim, France → Book →

Domaine Bott-Geyl ★ 4.7

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Sonnenglanz, Schoenenbourg, Sporen, Mandelberg, Furstentum, Schlossberg)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir18 haFounded 1795Bott family (Jean-Christophe Bott)Demeter CertifiedECOCERTRiquewihr-Zellenberg

Beblenheim family domaine with parcels in six Grands Crus, converted to organic in 2000 and certified Demeter biodynamic, run by Jean-Christophe Bott.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings at the Beblenheim domaine; appointments preferred.

Signature wines: sonnenglanz-gewurztraminer, schoenenbourg-riesling, furstentum-pinot-gris

1 Rue du Petit Chateau, 68980 Beblenheim, France → Book →

Domaine Ostertag ★ 4.7

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Muenchberg) plus lieux-dits Fronholz, Zellberg, HeissenbergRiesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Sylvaner, Pinot Noir15 haFounded 1966Ostertag family (Andre and Arthur Ostertag)Demeter CertifiedORGANIC CERTIFIEDDambach-la-Ville

Epfig domaine biodynamic since 1996 and Demeter certified, founded by Andre Ostertag and now run with his son Arthur across 88 plots on schist, sandstone and granite.

Tasting: Cellar visits by appointment at the Epfig domaine.

Signature wines: muenchberg-riesling, fronholz-pinot-gris, heissenberg-riesling

87 Rue Finkwiller, 67680 Epfig, France →

Domaine Schoffit ★ 4.7

Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Rangen de Thann including monopole Clos Saint-Theobald, Sommerberg)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Sylvaner, Chasselas, Pinot Noir18 haFounded 1959Schoffit familyWinemaker Alexandre SchoffitColmar-Wintzenheim-Wettolsheim

Colmar family estate with the rare Clos Saint-Theobald monopole on the volcanic Rangen de Thann, plus a small Sommerberg holding and the broad Harth lieu-dit at Colmar.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Colmar winery.

Signature wines: clos-saint-theobald-rangen-riesling, clos-saint-theobald-rangen-pinot-gris, harth-vieilles-vignes

66-68 Nonnenholzweg, 68000 Colmar, France →

Domaine Dirler-Cade ★ 4.7

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Spiegel, Saering, Kessler, Kitterle)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Sylvaner, Muscat, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir18 haFounded 1871Dirler family (Jean and Ludivine Dirler)Demeter CertifiedORGANIC CERTIFIEDGuebwiller

Bergholtz family domaine biodynamic since 1998 with parcels in four Guebwiller Grands Crus and the only producer alongside Schlumberger holding all four cru in significant quantity.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Bergholtz winery.

Signature wines: kitterle-riesling, saering-riesling, spiegel-gewurztraminer

13 Rue d'Issenheim, 68500 Bergholtz, France →

Domaine Pierre Frick ★ 4.6

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Steinert, Vorbourg, Eichberg)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Sylvaner, Pinot Noir12 haFounded 1768Frick family (Jean-Pierre and Chantal Frick)Demeter CertifiedECOCERTRouffach-Pfaffenheim-Westhalten

Pfaffenheim natural-wine producer, Demeter certified since 1981 and an early Alsace biodynamic adopter. Many cuvees released without added sulphur.

Tasting: Cellar visits by appointment; the domaine focuses on natural-style bottlings.

Signature wines: steinert-pinot-gris, vorbourg-riesling, pinot-blanc-zero-sulfite

5 Rue Baer, 68250 Pfaffenheim, France →

Domaine Christian Binner ★ 4.6

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Schlossberg, Kaefferkopf)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Muscat, Pinot Noir11 haFounded 1770Binner family (Christian and Audrey Binner)Demeter CertifiedORGANIC CERTIFIEDKaysersberg-Kientzheim-Sigolsheim

Ammerschwihr family domaine since 1770, a recognised Alsace natural-wine address with low-to-zero sulphur cuvees and a strong skin-contact and orange-style line.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Ammerschwihr winery.

Signature wines: schlossberg-riesling, kaefferkopf-cuvee-beatrice, saveurs-printanieres

2 Rue des Romains, 68770 Ammerschwihr, France → Book →

Domaine Barmes-Buecher ★ 4.6

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Hengst, Steingrubler, Pfersigberg)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Muscat, Pinot Noir18 haFounded 1985Barmes-Buecher family (Sophie, Genevieve and Maxime Barmes)Demeter CertifiedORGANIC CERTIFIEDColmar-Wintzenheim-Wettolsheim

Wettolsheim biodynamic estate certified since 2001, with parcels on Hengst, Steingrubler and Pfersigberg, member of Biodyvin and Renaissance des Appellations.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Wettolsheim winery.

Signature wines: hengst-riesling, steingrubler-gewurztraminer, pfersigberg-pinot-gris

30 Rue Sainte-Gertrude, 68920 Wettolsheim, France → Book →

Domaine Mure - Clos Saint-Landelin ★ 4.7

Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Vorbourg, including monopole Clos Saint-Landelin)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot Noir, Sylvaner24 haFounded 1648Mure family (Veronique and Thomas Mure)Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDRouffach-Pfaffenheim-Westhalten

Rouffach domaine owning the monopole Clos Saint-Landelin on the Vorbourg Grand Cru, biodynamic since the 1990s and one of the strongest Alsace Pinot Noir addresses.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings at the Clos Saint-Landelin cellar on the Vorbourg.

Signature wines: clos-saint-landelin-riesling, clos-saint-landelin-pinot-noir, vorbourg-pinot-gris

Tip: Clos Saint-Landelin is a 15-hectare south-facing limestone slope at the southern end of Vorbourg; the tasting room sits at the foot of the clos.

Route du Vin, 68250 Rouffach, France → Book →

Kuentz-Bas ★ 4.5

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Eichberg, Pfersigberg)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir14 haFounded 1795Bas familyDemeter CertifiedORGANIC CERTIFIEDEguisheim-Husseren

Husseren-les-Chateaux house founded in 1795, certified organic since 2007 and biodynamic since 2008, sitting on the highest village of the Route des Vins.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings at the Husseren-les-Chateaux winery on the highest village of the Route des Vins.

Signature wines: eichberg-gewurztraminer, pfersigberg-riesling, tradition-riesling

14 Route des Vins, 68420 Husseren-les-Chateaux, France → Book →

Domaine Valentin Zusslin ★ 4.6

Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Pfingstberg) and Cremant d'AlsaceRiesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Muscat, Pinot Noir13 haFounded 1691Zusslin family (Marie and Jean-Paul Zusslin)Demeter CertifiedECOCERTGuebwiller

Orschwihr family domaine since 1691, biodynamic since 1997 and Demeter certified, run by 13th-generation siblings Marie and Jean-Paul Zusslin with Pfingstberg Grand Cru holdings.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Orschwihr winery.

Signature wines: pfingstberg-riesling, clos-liebenberg-riesling, cremant-pinot-noir

57 Grand Rue, 68500 Orschwihr, France → Book →

Domaine Leon Boesch ★ 4.5

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Zinnkoepfle)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Sylvaner, Pinot Noir15 haFounded 1640Boesch family (Gerard and Matthieu Boesch)Demeter CertifiedORGANIC CERTIFIEDRouffach-Pfaffenheim-Westhalten

Westhalten family domaine since 1640 in the dry Vallee Noble, Demeter certified biodynamic since 2000 with parcels on the steep Zinnkoepfle Grand Cru.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings at the bio-climatic stone-and-straw cellar in Westhalten.

Signature wines: zinnkoepfle-riesling, zinnkoepfle-gewurztraminer, luss-gewurztraminer

6 Rue Saint-Blaise, 68250 Westhalten, France → Book →

Domaine Rolly Gassmann ★ 4.6

Alsace AOC (Rorschwihr lieux-dits) and Selection de Grains NoblesRiesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot Blanc, Sylvaner, AuxerroisFounded 1611Rolly Gassmann family (Pierre and Marie-Therese Gassmann)Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDRibeauville-Hunawihr-Bergheim

Rorschwihr family domaine with library cellar stretching back four decades, specialising in long-aged Rorschwihr lieux-dits Riesling, Gewurztraminer and Pinot Gris.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Rorschwihr winery.

Signature wines: kappelweg-riesling, moenchreben-gewurztraminer, stegreben-de-rorschwihr

Tip: Rolly Gassmann releases its wines with extended bottle age; current vintages frequently sit five to ten years behind release.

1-2 Place de l'Eglise, 68590 Rorschwihr, France → Book →

Domaine Marc Kreydenweiss ★ 4.7

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Wiebelsberg, Moenchberg, Kastelberg)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir, Sylvaner13.5 haFounded 1971Kreydenweiss family (Antoine Kreydenweiss)Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDBarr-Mittelbergheim-Andlau

Andlau biodynamic pioneer farming since 1989, three Bas-Rhin Grands Crus on schist, sandstone and limestone, now run by Marc's son Antoine.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings at the Andlau winery; appointments preferred.

Signature wines: kastelberg-riesling, wiebelsberg-riesling, moenchberg-pinot-gris

12 Rue Deharbe, 67140 Andlau, France → Book →

Domaine Paul Blanck et Fils ★ 4.5

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Furstentum, Mambourg, Schlossberg, Wineck-Schlossberg)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir, Muscat, Sylvaner, Auxerrois36 haFounded 1923Blanck family (Philippe and Frederic Blanck)Kaysersberg-Kientzheim-Sigolsheim

Kientzheim domaine farmed by cousins Philippe and Frederic Blanck across 36 hectares with parcels in four neighbouring Grands Crus on granite and limestone.

Tasting: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Kientzheim winery.

Signature wines: schlossberg-riesling, furstentum-gewurztraminer, wineck-schlossberg-riesling

32 Grand Rue, 68240 Kientzheim, France → Book →

Domaine Marc Tempe ★ 4.5

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Mambourg, Schoenenbourg, Furstentum, Zinnkoepfle)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Muscat9 haFounded 1993Tempe family (Marc and Anne-Marie Tempe)Demeter CertifiedORGANIC CERTIFIEDRiquewihr-Zellenberg

Zellenberg micro-domaine biodynamic since 1996, run by Marc Tempe with extended elevage in foudre and old Burgundy barrels and a focus on Grand Cru terroir transparency.

Tasting: Cellar visits by appointment in Zellenberg.

Signature wines: mambourg-gewurztraminer, schoenenbourg-riesling, furstentum-pinot-gris

24 Rue du Schlossberg, 68340 Zellenberg, France →

Cave de Ribeauville ★ 4.3

Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Geisberg, Osterberg, Kirchberg de Ribeauville, Rosacker, Altenberg de Bergheim) and Cremant d'AlsaceRiesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Muscat, Sylvaner, Pinot NoirFounded 1895Cooperative (40 grower members)Ribeauville-Hunawihr-Bergheim

Founded in 1895, an early French wine cooperative pooling Riesling Grand Cru parcels from forty Ribeauville growers across Geisberg, Osterberg and Kirchberg.

Tasting: Daily cellar visits and free tastings at the boutique on Route de Colmar.

Signature wines: geisberg-riesling, osterberg-riesling, kirchberg-de-ribeauville

2 Route de Colmar, 68150 Ribeauville, France → Book →

Domaine Sipp Mack ★ 4.4

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Rosacker, Osterberg)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Noir22 haFounded 1959Sipp-Mack family (Laura and Jean-Jacques Sipp)ORGANIC CERTIFIEDRibeauville-Hunawihr-Bergheim

Hunawihr family domaine with parcels on the Rosacker and Osterberg Grands Crus, certified organic with a consistent mid-priced Riesling line.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings at the Hunawihr winery; appointments preferred.

Signature wines: rosacker-riesling, osterberg-riesling, vieilles-vignes-riesling

1 Rue des Vosges, 68150 Hunawihr, France → Book →

Domaine Meyer-Fonne ★ 4.6

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Wineck-Schlossberg, Sporen, Furstentum, Kaefferkopf, Kitterle)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Muscat, Pinot Noir15 haFounded 1732Meyer family (Felix Meyer)Turckheim-Niedermorschwihr-Katzenthal

Katzenthal family domaine since 1732, run by Felix Meyer with fifteen hectares of granite-and-limestone Grands Crus including the Wineck-Schlossberg behind the village.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings at the Katzenthal winery; appointments preferred.

Signature wines: wineck-schlossberg-riesling, kaefferkopf-gewurztraminer, furstentum-vieilles-vignes

24 Grand Rue, 68230 Katzenthal, France → Book →

Domaine Achillee ★ 4.4

Alsace AOC (granite lieux-dits of Scherwiller)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Sylvaner, Pinot Noir, MuscatFounded 2016Dietrich family (Jean and Pierre Dietrich)Demeter CertifiedORGANIC CERTIFIEDDambach-la-Ville

Scherwiller estate built around a distinctive straw-bale wine cellar, certified organic and Demeter biodynamic with a focus on indigenous yeast fermentations and minimal sulphur.

Tasting: Cellar visits at the straw-bale bioclimatic cellar in Scherwiller; tours by booking.

Signature wines: riesling-granite, pinot-noir-rouge, edelzwicker-achillee

50 Rue de Dambach, 67750 Scherwiller, France → Book →

Domaine Albert Seltz ★ 4.4

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Zotzenberg)Sylvaner, Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, MuscatFounded 1576Seltz family (Alexis Seltz)ORGANIC CERTIFIEDBarr-Mittelbergheim-Andlau

Fourteen-generation Mittelbergheim domaine, a long-standing advocate for Sylvaner's place at Grand Cru level and producer of well-regarded Zotzenberg cuvees.

Tasting: Tastings by appointment at the 16th-century Mittelbergheim cellar; guided tours at 10:45 and 16:30.

Signature wines: zotzenberg-sylvaner, zotzenberg-riesling, old-vines-sylvaner

21 Rue Principale, 67140 Mittelbergheim, France → Book →

Domaine Vincent Stoeffler ★ 4.3

Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Kirchberg de Barr, Muenchberg) and Klevener de HeiligensteinRiesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Sylvaner, Pinot Noir16 haFounded 1900Stoeffler family (Vincent and Adrien Stoeffler)Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDBarr-Mittelbergheim-Andlau

Barr family domaine with sixteen hectares between Barr, Mittelbergheim and Heiligenstein, organic for two decades and one of the few non-local Klevener de Heiligenstein bottlers.

Tasting: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Barr winery.

Signature wines: kirchberg-de-barr-riesling, muenchberg-pinot-gris, klevener-de-heiligenstein

1 Rue des Lievres, 67140 Barr, France → Book →

Domaine Melanie Pfister ★ 4.4

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Engelberg)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Sylvaner, Pinot Noir, MuscatFounded 1780Pfister family (Melanie Pfister)Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDMarlenheim-Westhoffen

Dahlenheim domaine run by eighth-generation Melanie Pfister, certified organic since 2021 with biodynamic preparations and access to the Engelberg Grand Cru.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings at the Dahlenheim winery; appointments preferred.

Signature wines: engelberg-riesling, pinot-gris-dahlenheim, gewurztraminer-silberberg

53 Rue Principale, 67310 Dahlenheim, France → Book →

Domaine Frederic Mochel ★ 4.4

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Altenberg de Bergbieten)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Muscat, Pinot Noir10 haFounded 1669Mochel family (Guillaume Mochel)ORGANIC CERTIFIEDMarlenheim-Westhoffen

Fourteen-generation Traenheim domaine working the Altenberg de Bergbieten Grand Cru and certified organic across its 10-hectare holding.

Tasting: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Traenheim winery; appointments preferred.

Signature wines: altenberg-de-bergbieten-riesling, altenberg-de-bergbieten-muscat, altenberg-de-bergbieten-gewurztraminer

56 Rue Principale, 67310 Traenheim, France → Book →

Domaine Beck-Hartweg ★ 4.5

Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Frankstein)Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Sylvaner, Pinot Noir8 haFounded 1510Beck-Hartweg family (Florian and Mathilde Beck-Hartweg)Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDDambach-la-Ville

Sixteen-generation Dambach-la-Ville domaine traceable to 1510, organic since 2011 and biodynamic, working the pink-granite Frankstein Grand Cru above the medieval village.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment in central Dambach-la-Ville.

Signature wines: frankstein-riesling, bungertal-riesling, dambach-rouge

5 Rue Clemenceau, 67650 Dambach-la-Ville, France → Book →

Zellenberg family domaine since 1610, organic and biodynamic, with parcels on the Froehn Grand Cru, a touchstone for dry Alsace Muscat from clay-marl soils.

Tasting: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Zellenberg winery.

Signature wines: froehn-muscat, schoenenbourg-riesling, pinot-noir-rimelsberg

Mittelbergheim family domaine, certified organic and a touchstone of the Alsace natural-wine current with skin-contact and zero-sulphur cuvees alongside Zotzenberg Sylvaner.

Westhoffen domaine in the far north of the Route des Vins, certified Demeter and Biodyvin, producing only around 3500 cases per year from limestone lieux-dits.

Eguisheim family domaine since 1580, fourteen generations in the village square at the foot of the Saint-Leon castle, now run biodynamically by Christian and Valerie Beyer.

Bergheim house since 1836, certified organic across its 33 estate hectares with Altenberg de Bergheim and Kanzlerberg Grand Cru bottlings at the top of the range.

Wolfberger ★ 4.0

Eguisheim cooperative founded in 1902, pooling 1200 hectares across 480 grower families. Major source of Cremant d'Alsace at volume and a substantial regional eaux-de-vie distiller.

Orschwihr Cremant specialist founded by Balthazar Albrecht in 1698, a historic house involved in the 1976 founding of the Cremant d'Alsace AOC.

Tasting: Cellar visits at the Orschwihr house; Cremant tastings are a particular focus.

Signature wines: cremant-brut-rose, pfingstberg-riesling, cremant-brut-blanc

28 Rue du Printemps, 68500 Orschwihr, France → Book →

Still wine in Beaujolais

Domaine Marcel Lapierre ★ 4.9

Morgon AOCGamayFounded 1970Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalMorgon AOC

Morgon estate and spiritual home of the natural Beaujolais movement, producing whole-cluster Gamay with minimal sulfur following Jules Chauvet's method. Marcel died in 2010; the domaine continues.

Tasting: Visits and tastings by appointment; the domaine is a pillar of the natural Beaujolais movement founded on Jules Chauvet's principles.

Signature wines: lapierre-morgon, lapierre-raisins-gaulois

Tip: Book tastings well ahead; allocation wines sell quickly through specialist importers.

Le Moulin, 69910 Villié-Morgon, France →

Domaine Jean Foillard ★ 4.9

Morgon AOCGamayFounded 1981Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalMorgon AOC

Morgon estate anchored on the Cote du Py lieu-dit, a Gang of Four natural pioneer. Jean Foillard studied under Jules Chauvet and has farmed organically for decades.

Tasting: Visits by appointment only. The estate is best known for its Cote du Py parcel.

Signature wines: foillard-morgon-cote-du-py, foillard-morgon-eponym

Tip: The Cote du Py cuvee rewards 3 to 5 years in bottle.

Le Clachet, 69910 Villié-Morgon, France →

Domaine Guy Breton ★ 4.8

Morgon AOCGamayBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalMorgon AOC

Gang of Four member producing Morgon Vieilles Vignes from old Gamay vines with zero-sulfur philosophy and whole-cluster fermentation. A key reference for natural Beaujolais.

Le Tracot, 69910 Villié-Morgon, France →

Domaine Jean-Paul Thevenet ★ 4.8

Morgon AOCGamayBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalMorgon AOC

Fourth member of the Gang of Four, farming Morgon with the same minimal-intervention approach shaped by Jules Chauvet. Produces deeply concentrated Gamay from old vines.

Le Bourg, 69910 Villié-Morgon, France →

Domaine Damien Coquelet ★ 4.7

Morgon AOCGamayBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalMorgon AOC

Morgon estate run by Damien Coquelet, stepson of Jean Foillard. Produces Gamay from the Cote du Py and surrounding lieux-dits with the same natural approach as his mentor.

69910 Villié-Morgon, France →

Domaine Jean-Marc Burgaud ★ 4.5

Morgon AOCGamayMorgon AOC

Morgon estate producing cuvees from the Cote du Py lieu-dit. Burgaud is known for well-structured Gamay with ageing potential drawn from the schist and iron-rich soils.

Morgon, 69910 Villié-Morgon, France →

Domaine Bouland ★ 4.2

Morgon AOCGamayMorgon AOC

Family estate in Morgon crafting structured Gamay from schist-rich soils. A reliable village producer delivering honest, food-friendly Morgon at accessible prices.

Domaine Mee Godard ★ 4.6

Morgon AOCGamayBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalMorgon AOC

Mee Godard produces natural Morgon from organically farmed old vines, with a focus on transparent, single-terroir expression of the schist and iron-rich Cote du Py soils.

Domaine Yvon Metras ★ 4.8

Fleurie AOCGamayBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalFleurie AOC

Yvon Metras produces Fleurie with uncompromising natural methods, old vines and zero sulfur additions. Tiny production, allocated through specialist importers.

Domaine Chignard ★ 4.5

Fleurie AOCGamayFleurie AOC

Family domaine in Fleurie producing elegant, perfumed Gamay from granite soils with a traditional winemaking approach balancing fruit and structure.

Fleurie AOCGamayFleurie AOC

Six-generation Despres family estate in Fleurie producing terroir-expressive Gamay from the hillside above the village. Historic estate name drawn from the local Madonna chapel.

Historic 17th-century chateau and one of the largest estates in Brouilly, acquired by Christophe Gruy in 2017. The chateau and its walled wine chai are protected monuments. The estate produces Brouilly from the largest of the crus.

69460 Odenas, France →

69115 Chiroubles, France →

Domaine du Cret des Garanches ★ 4.0

Brouilly AOCGamayBrouilly AOC

Brouilly estate on the gentle granite slopes around the village of Cercié, producing generous, round Gamay typical of the appellation's six-commune spread.

Cercié, 69220 Cercié, France →

Domaine de Bel Air ★ 4.3

Moulin-a-Vent AOCGamayMoulin-a-Vent AOC

Moulin-a-Vent estate producing age-worthy Gamay from granite and manganese-streaked soils around the iconic windmill lieu-dit.

Romanèche-Thorins, 71570 Romanèche-Thorins, France →

Domaine Jean-Marc Despres ★ 4.1

Régnié AOCGamayRegnié AOC

Family domaine in Régnié, the youngest of the ten crus granted AOC status in 1988. Produces fresh, fruit-forward Gamay from granitic-sandy soils on the west-facing slopes of the appellation.

Régnié-Durette, 69430 Régnié-Durette, France →

Domaine Breton Beaujolais ★ 4.0

Régnié AOCGamayRegnié AOC

Regnié estate producing straightforward, approachable Gamay from one of the most recently promoted crus. An honest, everyday wine producer anchored in the village tradition.

Régnié-Durette, 69430 Régnié-Durette, France →

Domaine Julien Sunier ★ 4.7

Regnié AOC and Fleurie AOCGamayBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalRégnié AOC

Natural-wine producer working across Régnié and Fleurie, known for transparent, mineral Gamay with whole-cluster character. Julien Sunier trained in Burgundy and brings terroir precision to Beaujolais.

Régnié-Durette, 69430 Régnié-Durette, France →

Domaine Christian Ducroux ★ 4.6

Régnié AOCGamayDemeter CertifiedECOCERTNaturalRégnié AOC

Regnié estate farmed under Demeter biodynamic certification, one of the few fully certified biodynamic producers in Beaujolais. Natural wine approach with wild-yeast fermentation.

Durette, 69430 Régnié-Durette, France →

Domaine Karim Vionnet ★ 4.5

Morgon AOCGamayBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalMorgon AOC

Natural-wine producer in Morgon working with old-vine Gamay on schist and granite. Karim Vionnet is part of the post-Gang-of-Four generation keeping Morgon natural viticulture vital.

Morgon, 69910 Villié-Morgon, France →

Domaine Romain Zordan ★ 4.2

Moulin-a-Vent AOCGamayMoulin-a-Vent AOC

Small domaine in the Moulin-a-Vent appellation producing mineral Gamay from granitic soils with careful low-intervention cellar work.

Moulin-a-Vent, 71570 Romanèche-Thorins, France →

Domaine Foret ★ 4.1

Morgon AOCGamayMorgon AOC

Morgon village estate producing traditional, schist-influenced Gamay with the earthy, iron-mineral character that defines the appellation's most structured wines.

Morgon, 69910 Villié-Morgon, France →

Domaine Christophe Pacalet ★ 4.6

Morgon AOC and Fleurie AOCGamayBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalMorgon AOC

Nephew of Marcel Lapierre producing natural Beaujolais across multiple crus. Christophe Pacalet is known for single-cru bottlings that articulate Gamay terroir with minimal intervention.

Villié-Morgon, 69910 Villié-Morgon, France →

Domaine des Terres Dorees ★ 4.5

Beaujolais AOC and L'Ancien Beaujolais Vieilles VignesGamay, Chardonnay, Pinot NoirBeaujolais AOC

Jean-Paul Brun's estate in the southern Beaujolais producing both classic Gamay cuvees and unusual old-vine Chardonnay and Pinot Noir blends. Known for terroir precision and low-sulfur winemaking.

Charnay-en-Beaujolais, 69380 Charnay-en-Beaujolais, France →

Domaine du Vissoux ★ 4.5

Beaujolais AOC and Fleurie AOC and Moulin-a-Vent AOCGamay, ChardonnayORGANIC CERTIFIEDBeaujolais AOC

Pierre-Marie Chermette's estate spanning Beaujolais, Fleurie and Moulin-a-Vent. Known for expressive, low-sulfur wines crafted with artisan precision and respect for terroir.

Le Vissoux, 69620 Saint-Vérand, France →

Domaine des Nugues ★ 4.1

Beaujolais-Villages AOCGamay, ChardonnayBeaujolais-Villages AOC

Beaujolais-Villages estate in Lancié producing reliable, well-made Gamay and Chardonnay for everyday drinking, with a small tasting room for direct sales.

Genas, 69330 Lancié, France →

Domaine des Crais ★ 4.1

Saint-Amour AOCGamaySaint-Amour AOC

Saint-Amour estate producing fragrant, cherry-forward Gamay from the northernmost cru, a favourite on Valentines Day menus for its evocative name and approachable style.

Saint-Amour-Bellevue, 71570 Saint-Amour-Bellevue, France →

Domaine des Sables d'Or ★ 4.0

Brouilly AOCGamayBrouilly AOC

Brouilly estate in the commune of Saint-Lager, one of the six communes making up the largest of the Beaujolais crus. Produces ripe, generous Gamay from sandy granite.

Saint-Lager, 69220 Saint-Lager, France →

Domaine du Fief des Sept Cles ★ 4.1

Juliénas AOCGamayJulienas AOC

Small Julienas estate producing spiced, full-flavoured Gamay on the granite and schist slopes of this Saone-et-Loire cru shared between the Rhone and Saone-et-Loire departments.

Juliénas, 69840 Juliénas, France →

Domaine du Prieure Saint-Romain ★ 4.2

Saint-Amour AOCGamaySaint-Amour AOC

Saint-Amour estate producing aromatic, fresh Gamay from the northernmost cru, historically connected to the Benedictine priory that gave the appellation its name.

Saint-Amour-Bellevue, 71570 Saint-Amour-Bellevue, France →

Domaine des Rabioux ★ 4.2

Chiroubles AOCGamayChiroubles AOC

Chiroubles estate at 400 metres altitude, producing the lightest and most aromatic of the Beaujolais crus. The high-altitude granite soils of Chiroubles give unusually floral, delicate Gamay.

Chiroubles, 69115 Chiroubles, France →

Domaine Chateauvieux ★ 4.0

Chiroubles AOCGamayChiroubles AOC

Chiroubles domaine at high altitude farming vines at over 400 metres on pure granitic soils. Produces the characteristic light-bodied, floral Chiroubles style with lifted violet and cherry notes.

Chiroubles, 69115 Chiroubles, France →

Domaine des Terres de la Cour ★ 4.0

Juliénas AOCGamayJulienas AOC

Julienas estate on mixed granite and schist soils, producing robust, well-structured Gamay with characteristic spice and full fruit of the appellation.

Juliénas, 69840 Juliénas, France →

Chateau de Pizay ★ 4.3

Beaujolais-Villages AOC and Morgon AOCGamay, ChardonnayFounded 1232Beaujolais-Villages AOC

Historic medieval chateau with 13th-century origins at Saint-Jean-d'Ardieres, operating as a hotel and wine estate. Produces Beaujolais-Villages and Morgon under the Chateau de Pizay label.

Tasting: The chateau and hotel-restaurant operates full cellar tours, tastings and vineyard walks; estate stay available.

Tip: One of the few wine-tourism estates in Beaujolais with a hotel and restaurant on site.

Pizay, 69220 Saint-Jean-d'Ardières, France →

Maison Georges Duboeuf ★ 4.3

Beaujolais AOC and multiple Beaujolais crusGamay, ChardonnayFounded 1964Moulin-a-Vent AOC

The largest Beaujolais negociant house, founded in 1964 and known for popularising Beaujolais Nouveau globally. The Hameau Duboeuf park at Romanèche-Thorins is a major wine-tourism draw.

Tasting: The Hameau Duboeuf wine theme park and museum at Romanèche-Thorins is open to visitors; includes cellar tour, flower exhibition and tasting.

La Gare, 71570 Romanèche-Thorins, France → Book →

Maison Louis Jadot (Beaujolais) ★ 4.5

Moulin-a-Vent AOC via Chateau des JacquesGamayMoulin-a-Vent AOC

Burgundy house Louis Jadot acquired Chateau des Jacques in Moulin-a-Vent, bringing Burgundy winemaking rigour to Beaujolais. Produces single-lieu-dit Gamay cuvees of notable depth.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings at Chateau des Jacques in Moulin-a-Vent. Book through Louis Jadot Beaune.

21 Rue Eugene Spuller, 21200 Beaune, France →

English-owned Entre-Deux-Mers estate known for crisp whites and reliable Bordeaux reds. 40 hectares; farmhouse accommodation; award-winning wines at accessible price points.

Tasting: Visits by appointment.

Signature wines: chateau-palmer, alter-ego-de-palmer

Scores: Wine Advocate 100 (2009), Wine Advocate 100 (1961)

Still wine in Burgundy

Domaine de la Romanee-Conti ★ 5.0

Grand Cru (Cote de Nuits, AOC)Pinot Noir, Chardonnay28 haFounded 1869de Villaine and Leroy familiesWinemaker Bertrand de Villaine and Perrine Fenal (co-direction)Biodynamic PracticingECOCERTVosne-Romanee

The most revered estate in Burgundy, monopole owner of Romanee-Conti and La Tache and farmer of a roughly 28-hectare grand-cru holding in Vosne-Romanee.

Tasting: No public tastings or visits. Wines are sold strictly on allocation through approved importers and en-bloc cases.

Signature wines: romanee-conti, la-tache

Tip: Allocation only; there is no cellar door. Bottles surface at auction and via a handful of importers.

1 Rue Derriere le Four, 21700 Vosne-Romanee, France →

Domaine Leroy ★ 4.9

Grand Cru and Premier Cru (Cote de Nuits, AOC)Pinot Noir, Chardonnay22 haFounded 1988Lalou Bize-Leroy familyWinemaker Lalou Bize-LeroyBiodynamic PracticingECOCERTVosne-Romanee

Lalou Bize-Leroy's biodynamic Vosne-Romanee domaine, built from 1988 on the former Noellat and Remy vines, now among the most sought-after and expensive wines in Burgundy.

Tasting: No public tastings. The biodynamic domaine sells on tight allocation through importers.

Tip: Demeter-certified and allocation-only; no visits. Look for the rare Domaine Leroy versus the separate Maison Leroy negociant label.

15 Rue de la Fontaine, 21700 Vosne-Romanee, France →

Domaine Meo-Camuzet ★ 4.8

Vosne-Romanee domaine shaped by Henri Jayer's influence, holding Richebourg, Clos de Vougeot and Vosne premier crus under Jean-Nicolas Meo.

Tasting: Visits and tastings by appointment only; the domaine also runs a Meo-Camuzet Frere et Soeurs negociant range.

11 Rue des Grands Crus, 21700 Vosne-Romanee, France → Book →

A family domaine run by the Mugneret sisters in Vosne-Romanee, prized for Echezeaux, Ruchottes-Chambertin and Clos de Vougeot of great purity.

5 Rue des Communes, 21700 Vosne-Romanee, France →
