Steel-only Chablis domaine making precise, unoaked grand and premier crus including Les Clos, Vaudesir and Montee de Tonnerre.

Tasting: Tastings by appointment; the domaine ferments entirely in stainless steel to showcase Chablis minerality.

9 Boulevard de Ferrieres, 89800 Chablis, France → Book →

Domaine Jean-Marc Brocard ★ 4.4

Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Chablis and Saint-Bris, AOC)Chardonnay, Sauvignon Blanc, Pinot Noir, Cesar200 haFounded 1973Brocard familyWinemaker Julien BrocardBiodynamic PracticingECOCERTChablis

Major Chablis grower around Prehy, organic and partly biodynamic, also bottling Saint-Bris Sauvignon and Irancy reds from the Grand Auxerrois.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at the Sainte-Claire estate by Prehy church; large organic and biodynamic holdings.

3 Route de Chablis, 89800 Prehy, France → Book →

Maison J. Moreau et Fils ★ 4.1

Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Chablis, AOC)ChardonnayFounded 1814Boisset familyChablis

One of the oldest Chablis houses, founded in 1814, now part of the Boisset group, with a long association with the Les Clos grand cru.

Tasting: Tastings and shop in Chablis; a historic house with grand-cru parcels including Les Clos.

Route d'Auxerre, 89800 Chablis, France → Book →

Domaine Laroche ★ 4.3

Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Chablis, AOC)Chardonnay90 haFounded 1850Advini groupWinemaker Gregory ViennoisECOCERTChablis

Chablis house cellared in a ninth-century monastic building, with grand crus including Les Clos, Blanchot and the Reserve de l'Obedience bottling.

Tasting: Visits and tastings in the medieval Obediencerie cellars in central Chablis, open to bookings.

L'Obediencerie, 22 Rue Louis Bro, 89800 Chablis, France → Book →

Domaines Devillard ★ 4.3

Premier Cru and Village (Cote Chalonnaise and Cote de Nuits, AOC)Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, AligoteFounded 1934Devillard familyWinemaker Amaury DevillardCote Chalonnaise

Cote Chalonnaise family group centred on Chateau de Chamirey in Mercurey, with Mercurey and Rully premier crus and a Nuits-Saint-Georges estate.

Tasting: Tastings at Chateau de Chamirey in Mercurey by appointment; the group also owns Domaine des Perdrix in Nuits.

Chateau de Chamirey, 71640 Mercurey, France → Book →

Maison Verget ★ 4.4

Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Maconnais, Chablis and Cote de Beaune, AOC)ChardonnayFounded 1990Jean-Marie GuffensWinemaker Jean-Marie GuffensMaconnais

Maconnais negociant founded by Jean-Marie Guffens, sourcing precise, terroir-driven Chardonnay from Pouilly-Fuisse to the Cote de Beaune and Chablis.

Tasting: Tastings by appointment at Sologny; the negociant arm of Guffens-Heynen sourcing across the Maconnais and Chablis.

Le Bourg, 71960 Sologny, France → Book →

Still wine in Douro Valley

Ferreira ★ 4.6

DOC PortoTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Tinta BarrocaFounded 1751SograpeVila Nova de Gaia

Portugal's most historic native-owned Port house, founded in 1751 and now Sogrape-owned, Ferreira is bound up with the legendary Dona Antonia Adelaide Ferreira and the Douro's still-wine pioneer Barca Velha.

Tasting: Cellar tour and tasting in Gaia telling the story of Dona Antonia Ferreira.

Signature wines: ferreira-vintage-port, ferreira-dona-antonia-reserva

Avenida Ramos Pinto 70, 4400-082 Vila Nova de Gaia, Portugal → Book →

Niepoort ★ 4.7

DOC Porto and DOC DouroTouriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Tinta Amarela, Sousao, RabigatoFounded 1842Niepoort family (Dirk Niepoort)Vila Nova de Gaia

A family-owned house founded in 1842 and led by Dirk Niepoort, equally renowned for classic Vintage Port and a benchmark range of DOC Douro reds and whites such as Redoma and Charme.

Tasting: Family Port house; cellar visits by reservation, with Douro DOC and Port flights.

Signature wines: niepoort-vintage-port, niepoort-redoma-tinto

Rua Candido dos Reis 598, 4400-071 Vila Nova de Gaia, Portugal → Book →

Real Companhia Velha ★ 4.4

DOC Porto and DOC DouroTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, SousaoFounded 1756Silva Reis familyVila Nova de Gaia

Founded by royal charter in 1756, Real Companhia Velha (Royal Oporto) is Portugal's oldest wine company, with vast Gaia cellars and an expanding range of DOC Douro wines.

Tasting: Multilingual guided tour of the historic Gaia cellars ending in a Port tasting.

Rua Azevedo Magalhaes 314, 4430-952 Vila Nova de Gaia, Portugal → Book →

Quinta do Noval ★ 4.8

DOC Porto and DOC DouroTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Tinto Cao, Sousao145 haFounded 1715AXA MillesimesPinhao

A single-quinta house above Pinhao owned by AXA Millesimes, Quinta do Noval is famed for its tiny ungrafted Nacional vineyard, source of one of the world's rarest and most expensive Vintage Ports.

Tasting: Estate visits and tastings at the Pinhao quinta, including the celebrated Nacional vineyard.

Signature wines: quinta-do-noval-nacional, quinta-do-noval-vintage-port

Vale de Mendiz, 5085-110 Pinhao, Portugal → Book →

Quinta do Crasto ★ 4.8

DOC Douro and DOC PortoTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Tinto Cao, Viosinho74 haFounded 1615Roquette familySabrosa

A Roquette-family estate on the right bank between Regua and Pinhao, Quinta do Crasto is a leader of the Douro still-wine renaissance, famed for its old-vine field-blend reds.

Tasting: Tours, tastings, lunches and boat trips overlooking the river, by reservation.

Signature wines: quinta-do-crasto-vinha-maria-teresa, quinta-do-crasto-reserva-old-vines

Gouvinhas, 5060-063 Sabrosa, Portugal → Book →

Quinta do Vale Meao ★ 4.8

DOC Douro and DOC PortoTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Tinta Barroca, Tinto Cao62 haFounded 1877Olazabal family (Francisco Olazabal)Douro Superior

Founded in 1877 by Dona Antonia Ferreira and run by her descendant Francisco Olazabal, this Douro Superior estate makes one of Portugal's most sought-after reds plus the Meandro second wine.

Tasting: Estate visits by appointment at the Douro Superior quinta.

Signature wines: quinta-do-vale-meao-tinto, meandro-do-vale-meao

Quinta do Vale Meao, 5150-501 Vila Nova de Foz Coa, Portugal → Book →

Prats & Symington (Chryseia) ★ 4.7

DOC DouroTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Tinta BarrocaFounded 1998Prats and Symington familiesCima Corgo

A 1998 partnership of the Symingtons of the Douro and Bruno Prats of Bordeaux, Prats & Symington makes the elegant Chryseia and its Post Scriptum second wine from Quinta de Roriz and Quinta da Perdiz.

Tasting: Douro DOC project of the Symington and Prats families; wines shown via Symington visits.

Signature wines: chryseia, post-scriptum-de-chryseia

Quinta de Roriz, Ervedosa do Douro, Sao Joao da Pesqueira, Cima Corgo, Portugal → Book →

Quinta do Vallado ★ 4.6

DOC Douro and DOC PortoTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Sousao, RabigatoFounded 1716Ferreira and Cabral familiesPeso da Regua

One of the oldest quintas in the Douro, dating to 1716 and owned by descendants of Dona Antonia Ferreira, Vallado pairs a design wine hotel at Regua with old-vine reds and the Adelaide top cuvee.

Tasting: Wine hotel with tours, tastings and a winery visit beside the Corgo river at Regua.

Signature wines: quinta-do-vallado-adelaide, quinta-do-vallado-reserva

Vilarinho dos Freires, 5050-364 Peso da Regua, Portugal → Book →

Wine & Soul ★ 4.6

DOC Douro and DOC PortoTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Sousao, Tinta AmarelaFounded 2001Sandra Tavares da Silva and Jorge Serodio BorgesPinhao

A small, acclaimed Vale de Mendiz project from winemaking couple Sandra Tavares da Silva and Jorge Serodio Borges, Wine & Soul makes the old-vine Pintas red, Guru white and a 5G tawny Port.

Tasting: Boutique producer at Vale de Mendiz; tastings by appointment.

Signature wines: wine-and-soul-pintas, wine-and-soul-guru

Avenida Julio de Freitas, Vale de Mendiz 186, 5085-101 Pinhao, Portugal → Book →

Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo ★ 4.6

DOC Douro and DOC PortoTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Tinto CaoFounded 1764Amorim familyCima Corgo

An Amorim-family estate in the Cima Corgo at Covas do Douro, Quinta Nova is a pioneer of Douro wine tourism, combining a Relais & Chateaux winery hotel with a serious DOC Douro range.

Tasting: Relais & Chateaux winery hotel with tastings, vineyard walks and the Terracu's restaurant.

Signature wines: quinta-nova-grande-reserva, quinta-nova-referencia

Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo, 5085-222 Covas do Douro, Portugal → Book →

Quinta do Portal ★ 4.4

DOC Douro and DOC PortoTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Moscatel Galego BrancoFounded 1994Mansilha familySabrosa

A Mansilha-family estate in the Pinhao river valley near Sabrosa, Quinta do Portal makes DOC Douro reds, Port and a rare Moscatel do Douro, with a striking architect-designed barrel cellar.

Tasting: Visits, tastings and stays at the estate, with a Siza Vieira-designed cellar.

Signature wines: quinta-do-portal-grande-reserva, portal-moscatel-do-douro

EN 323, 5060-020 Celeiros do Douro, Sabrosa, Portugal → Book →

Quinta de la Rosa ★ 4.6

DOC Douro and DOC PortoTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Sousao, ViosinhoFounded 1815Bergqvist familyPinhao

A Bergqvist-family estate on the river just below Pinhao, Quinta de la Rosa is a single-quinta producer of both Port and DOC Douro wines, with a wine hotel and restaurant on site.

Tasting: Riverside wine hotel near Pinhao with tours, tastings and the Cozinha da Clara restaurant.

Signature wines: quinta-de-la-rosa-tinto, quinta-de-la-rosa-vintage-port

Quinta de la Rosa, 5085-241 Pinhao, Portugal → Book →

Quinta da Pacheca ★ 4.5

DOC Douro and DOC PortoTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Viosinho, ArintoFounded 1738Maria Pacheco Serpa group (Pacheca)Lamego

An 18th-century estate at Cambres near Lamego on the south bank, Quinta da Pacheca was one of the first Douro quintas to bottle under its own name and now runs the Wine House Hotel with its landmark barrel rooms.

Tasting: Wine House Hotel with tours, tastings, harvest treading and wine-barrel rooms.

Signature wines: quinta-da-pacheca-reserva, pacheca-vintage-port

Rua Relogio do Sol 261, 5100-424 Cambres, Lamego, Portugal → Book →

Quinta do Tedo ★ 4.4

DOC Porto and DOC DouroTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Tinta BarrocaFounded 1992Bouchard familyECOCERTBaixo Corgo

An 18th-century estate at the confluence of the Tedo and Douro run by the Bouchard family, Quinta do Tedo farms organically and makes Port, DOC Douro reds and olive oil with river-view tastings.

Tasting: Organic estate at Folgosa with cellar tours, tastings, picnics and rooms.

Signature wines: quinta-do-tedo-vintage-port

Folgosa 5110-207, Douro Valley, Armamar, Portugal → Book →

Quinta do Pego ★ 4.3

DOC Douro and DOC PortoTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Tinta BarrocaFounded 1991Quinta do PegoTabuaco

A boutique wine hotel and estate perched above the Douro at Valenca do Douro, Quinta do Pego makes award-winning DOC Douro Grande Reserva reds and Vintage Port with daily tastings.

Tasting: Country wine hotel above the river with daily tastings of estate Douro and Port wines.

Signature wines: quinta-do-pego-grande-reserva

EN 222, Valenca do Douro, 5120-493 Tabuaco, Portugal → Book →

Domingos Alves de Sousa ★ 4.5

DOC Douro and DOC PortoTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Tinto Cao, SousaoFounded 1987Alves de Sousa familyBaixo Corgo

A five-generation Baixo Corgo family producer led from Quinta da Gaivosa at Santa Marta de Penaguiao, Alves de Sousa is a DOC Douro specialist known for the Gaivosa and old-vine Abandonado reds.

Tasting: Family estate at Santa Marta de Penaguiao with tours and tastings, booking advised.

Signature wines: quinta-da-gaivosa-tinto, alves-de-sousa-abandonado

Quinta da Gaivosa, 5030-055 Santa Marta de Penaguiao, Portugal → Book →

Quinta da Romaneira ★ 4.5

DOC Douro and DOC PortoTouriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz, Tinta Barroca, Sousao85 haFounded 1757Quinta da RomaneiraAlijo

A large historic estate between Pinhao and Alijo at Cotas, Quinta da Romaneira pairs a five-star hotel with DOC Douro reds and Port across one of the region's most scenic stretches of river.

Tasting: 400-hectare estate with a luxury hotel, restaurant, tastings and river activities at Cotas.

Signature wines: quinta-da-romaneira-tinto

Cotas, 5070-252 Alijo, Portugal → Book →

Still wine in Mosel

Weingut Dr. Loosen ★ 4.8

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingFounded 1988Ernst LoosenWinemaker Ernst LoosenBernkastel-Kues

Ernst Loosen's estate holds parcels of ungrafted old vines in many of the Mittelmosel's grand crus, from Wehlener Sonnenuhr to Erdener Pralat and the Bernkasteler Lay. A leading global ambassador for Mosel Riesling.

Tasting: Wine tastings and cellar-door sales Monday to Saturday 10:00 to 17:00 by appointment at St. Johannishof.

St. Johannishof, 54470 Bernkastel-Kues, Germany → Book →

Weingut Egon Muller - Scharzhof ★ 5.0

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingEgon MullerWinemaker Egon MullerSaar

The most prestigious estate of the Saar, owner of the heart of the Scharzhofberg. Its Trockenbeerenauslese and Auslese rank among the most expensive and ageworthy white wines in the world.

Tasting: Visits by appointment only; the estate sells primarily through auction and allocation.

Scharzhof, 54459 Wiltingen, Germany → Book →

Weingut Joh. Jos. Prum ★ 4.9

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingFounded 1911Prum family (Katharina Prum)Wehlen

The most famous Riesling estate in Wehlen, known worldwide for long-lived, reductive, off-dry wines from the Wehlener Sonnenuhr. Family-run and dedicated entirely to Riesling on blue Devonian slate.

Tasting: Tastings by prior appointment only; contact the estate office by email to arrange a visit.

Uferallee 19, 54470 Bernkastel-Wehlen, Germany → Book →

Weingut Fritz Haag ★ 4.8

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingFounded 1605Haag family (Oliver Haag)Winemaker Oliver HaagBrauneberg

A benchmark estate on the south-facing Brauneberger Juffer and Juffer-Sonnenuhr, run by Oliver Haag. Crystalline, racy Rieslings with documented roots back to 1605.

Tasting: Tastings and sales by appointment at the Dusemonder Hof estate in Brauneberg.

Dusemonder Strasse 44, 54472 Brauneberg, Germany → Book →

Weingut Selbach-Oster ★ 4.6

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingFounded 1661Selbach family (Johannes Selbach)Winemaker Johannes SelbachZeltingen-Rachtig

A long-established family estate working steep parcels in Zeltingen, Wehlen, Graach and Bernkastel. Known for old-vine, hand-harvested Rieslings and characterful dry and off-dry bottlings.

Tasting: Tastings at the Selbach Vinothek, Gansfelder Strasse 20; Saturday 11:00 to 17:00 and Sundays and holidays by appointment.

Gansfelder Strasse 20, 54492 Zeltingen-Rachtig, Germany → Book →

Weingut Maximin Grunhaus ★ 4.8

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRiesling, Weissburgunder (Pinot Blanc), Spatburgunder (Pinot Noir)von Schubert family (Maximin von Schubert)Winemaker Maximin von SchubertRuwer

A monastic-rooted Ruwer estate whose three monopole vineyards, Abtsberg, Herrenberg and Brudersberg, surround the manor. Filigree, long-lived Rieslings with documented winegrowing back to the 10th century.

Tasting: Open Monday to Friday 08:00 to 17:00; Saturdays 10:00 to 16:00 from May to October, and by appointment.

Maximin Grunhaus 1, 54318 Mertesdorf, Germany → Book →

Weingut Van Volxem ★ 4.7

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingFounded 2000Roman NiewodniczanskiSaar

A modern Saar powerhouse revived by Roman Niewodniczanski, reclaiming historic grand-cru sites and championing dry, full-bodied, old-vine Riesling from a striking new winery above Wiltingen.

Tasting: Vinothek and wine sales open Tuesday to Sunday 11:00 to 18:00.

Zum Schlossberg 347, 54459 Wiltingen, Germany → Book →

Weingut Forstmeister Geltz-Zilliken ★ 4.7

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingFounded 1742Zilliken family (Dorothee Zilliken)Winemaker Dorothee ZillikenSaar

A historic Saar estate centred on the Saarburger Rausch and Ockfener Bockstein, producing piercingly mineral, age-worthy Rieslings matured in a famously deep slate cellar.

Tasting: Tastings and sales by appointment; the estate is known for its deep, cold Saarburg cellar.

Heckingstrasse 20, 54439 Saarburg, Germany → Book →

Weingut Schloss Lieser ★ 4.7

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingHaag family (Thomas Haag)Winemaker Thomas HaagMittelmosel

Thomas Haag's estate at the castle village of Lieser, with parcels in the Brauneberger Juffer, Wehlener Sonnenuhr and Niederberg Helden. Multiple-time German winery of the year for its precise Rieslings.

Tasting: Vinothek tastings and sales at the estate in Lieser; visits by arrangement.

Am Markt 1-5, 54470 Lieser, Germany → Book →

Weingut Clemens Busch ★ 4.7

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingBusch family (Clemens and Florian Busch)Winemaker Clemens BuschBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDMittelmosel

A biodynamic pioneer in Punderich working the single Marienburg slope as separate blue, red and grey-slate parcels. Organic since 1984 and certified biodynamic since 2015; characterful, often skin-influenced dry Riesling.

Tasting: Tastings and sales by appointment at the Punderich estate.

Kirchstrasse 37, 56862 Punderich, Germany → Book →

Weingut Heymann-Lowenstein ★ 4.7

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingLowenstein family (Reinhard Lowenstein, Sarah Lowenstein)Winemaker Reinhard LowensteinTerrassenmosel

A pioneer of dry, terroir-driven Lower Mosel Riesling from the terraced Winninger Uhlen and Rottgen sites. Powerful, parcel-specific, spontaneously fermented wines on the Terrassenmosel.

Tasting: Tastings and sales at the Winningen estate by appointment.

Bahnhofstrasse 10, 56333 Winningen, Germany → Book →

Weingut Markus Molitor ★ 4.7

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRiesling, Spatburgunder (Pinot Noir), Weissburgunder (Pinot Blanc)Founded 1984Markus MolitorWinemaker Markus MolitorWehlen

An ambitious estate spanning the Mosel and Saar with one of the largest collections of top sites in the region, plus serious Mosel Pinot Noir. Famous for its star-coded labelling of dry, off-dry and sweet styles.

Tasting: Vinothek open Monday to Friday 10:00 to 17:00, weekends and holidays by appointment.

Haus Klosterberg, 54470 Bernkastel-Wehlen, Germany → Book →

Weingut Peter Lauer ★ 4.7

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingLauer family (Florian Lauer)Winemaker Florian LauerSaar

A fifth-generation Ayl estate famous for its barrel-fermented, dry-tasting Fass-numbered Rieslings from the Ayler Kupp. Vegan production and a cult following for its precise, gastronomic Saar style.

Tasting: Tastings at the estate and at the attached Weinhotel Ayler Kupp restaurant; by arrangement.

Trierer Strasse 49, 54441 Ayl, Germany → Book →

Weingut Willi Schaefer ★ 4.8

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingSchaefer family (Christoph Schaefer)Winemaker Christoph SchaeferGraach

A tiny, revered grower in Graach producing precise, mineral Rieslings from the Domprobst and Himmelreich sites. Hand-worked steep slate, classic fruity Pradikat styles.

Tasting: Small family estate with no fixed opening hours; visits and tastings by appointment, typically April to July.

Hauptstrasse 130, 54470 Graach an der Mosel, Germany → Book →

Weingut von Hovel ★ 4.5

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingvon Kunow family (Maximilian von Kunow)Winemaker Maximilian von KunowSaar

A Saar estate at Oberemmel with sole ownership of the Oberemmeler Hutte monopole and parcels in the Scharzhofberg. Maximilian von Kunow makes classically styled, transparent Saar Riesling.

Tasting: Office open Monday to Friday mornings; tastings by arrangement at the Oberemmel estate.

Agritiusstrasse 5-6, 54329 Konz-Oberemmel, Germany → Book →

Hofgut Falkenstein ★ 4.6

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingFounded 1981Weber family (Erich and Johannes Weber)Winemaker Johannes WeberSaar

A cult Saar grower making spontaneously fermented, barrel-aged Rieslings labelled in an old-fashioned cask-numbered style. Dry-leaning, transparent wines from sites around Niedermennig and Krettnach.

Tasting: Small estate; tastings and sales by appointment at the Falkensteinerhof.

Falkensteinerhof 1, 54329 Konz-Niedermennig, Germany → Book →

Weingut Karthauserhof ★ 4.7

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingFounded 1335Christoph Tyrell and Albert BehlerRuwer

A historic Ruwer estate founded by Carthusian monks in 1335, owner of the Eitelsbacher Karthauserhofberg monopole. Distinctive neck-only labels and racy, mineral Riesling.

Tasting: Open Monday to Friday 08:30 to 17:00; Saturdays April to October 10:00 to 15:00, and by appointment.

Karthauserhof 1, 54292 Trier-Eitelsbach, Germany → Book →

Weingut Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt ★ 4.5

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRiesling, Weissburgunder (Pinot Blanc)Reh family (managing director Dr. Karsten Weyand)Ruwer

A large historic estate with holdings across the Mosel, Saar and Ruwer, based at the listed Schloss Marienlay above the Ruwer. Riesling from grand crus including Scharzhofberg, Josephshofer and Kaseler Nies'chen.

Tasting: Tastings at Schloss Marienlay; office hours Monday to Thursday to 16:45, Friday to 13:00.

Schlossgut Marienlay, 54317 Morscheid, Germany → Book →

Weingut Knebel ★ 4.6

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingKnebel family (Matthias Knebel)Winemaker Matthias KnebelTerrassenmosel

A leading Terrassenmosel grower with prime parcels in the Winninger Rottgen, Uhlen and Hamm. Matthias Knebel makes precise, mineral, mostly dry Riesling from the terraced Lower Mosel.

Tasting: Open Monday to Saturday by prior telephone appointment at the Winningen estate.

August-Horch-Strasse 24, 56333 Winningen, Germany → Book →

Weingut Reinhold Haart ★ 4.7

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingHaart family (Theo and Johannes Haart)Winemaker Johannes HaartPiesport

The leading estate of Piesport, with the lion's share of the perfumed Goldtropfchen. Family vineyard ownership documented since 1337; aromatic, peach-and-citrus Riesling of great finesse.

Tasting: Tastings by appointment at the riverside estate in Piesport.

Ausoniusufer 18, 54498 Piesport, Germany → Book →

Weingut Immich-Batterieberg ★ 4.6

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingGernot Kollmann and partnersWinemaker Gernot KollmannMittelmosel

One of the oldest estates on the Mosel, revived by Gernot Kollmann around the old-vine Enkircher sites of Batterieberg, Steffensberg and Ellergrub. Dry, spontaneously fermented, low-intervention Riesling.

Tasting: Tastings and sales by appointment at the estate in Enkirch.

Im Alten Tal 2, 56850 Enkirch, Germany → Book →

Weingut Vollenweider ★ 4.5

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingFounded 2000Moritz Hoffmann (estate founded by Daniel Vollenweider)Winemaker Moritz HoffmannMittelmosel

A boutique estate founded by Swiss-born Daniel Vollenweider and centred on the steep old-vine Wolfer Goldgrube, now led by Moritz Hoffmann. Concentrated, classically fruity Mosel Riesling from the Traben-Trarbach area.

Tasting: Small estate; tastings and sales by appointment in Traben-Trarbach.

Wolfer Weg 53, 56841 Traben-Trarbach, Germany → Book →

Weingut Weiser-Kunstler ★ 4.6

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingFounded 2005Konstantin Weiser and Alexandra KunstlerWinemaker Konstantin WeiserMittelmosel

A sought-after small grower working steep, organically farmed parcels in the Enkircher Ellergrub and Trabener Gaispfad. Detailed, low-intervention Riesling in dry and fruity styles.

Tasting: Small estate; tastings and sales by appointment in Traben-Trarbach.

Wilhelmstrasse 11, 56841 Traben-Trarbach, Germany → Book →

Weingut Joh. Jos. Christoffel Erben ★ 4.5

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingRobert EymaelUrzig and Erden

A storied Urzig label held by the Christoffel family for four centuries and now made and distributed by Robert Eymael of Monchhof. Classic, delicately sweet Riesling from the Urziger Wurzgarten and Erdener Treppchen.

Tasting: Tastings at the Monchhof Vinothek, where both estates' wines can be tasted and bought.

Monchhof, 54539 Urzig, Germany → Book →

Weingut Monchhof - Robert Eymael ★ 4.4

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingEymael family (Robert Eymael)Urzig and Erden

One of the oldest estates in the Mosel, with records back to the Cistercian abbey of Himmerod in 1177. Robert Eymael makes traditional fruity Riesling from the Urziger Wurzgarten and Erdener sites.

Tasting: Vinothek tastings of both Monchhof and Joh. Jos. Christoffel Erben wines; visits by arrangement.

Monchhof, 54539 Urzig, Germany → Book →

Weingut Dr. Hermann ★ 4.4

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingHermann family (Christian Hermann)Winemaker Christian HermannUrzig and Erden

An Erden family estate with prime holdings in the steep Erdener Pralat and Treppchen and the Urziger Wurzgarten. Classic fruity-style Mosel Riesling across the Pradikat ladder.

Tasting: Tastings and sales by appointment at the riverside estate in Erden.

Uferallee 25, 54492 Erden, Germany → Book →

Weingut S.A. Prum ★ 4.4

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingPrum family (Saskia Prum)Winemaker Saskia PrumWehlen

A Wehlen estate housed in a 1920s reform-style villa, with parcels in the Wehlener Sonnenuhr and Graacher Himmelreich. Now led by Saskia Prum, producing classic and dry Mosel Riesling.

Tasting: Tastings and sales at the listed villa estate in Wehlen; visits by arrangement.

Uferallee 25, 54470 Bernkastel-Wehlen, Germany → Book →

Weingut Studert-Prum ★ 4.3

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingFounded 1581Studert familyWehlen

A long-established Wehlen family estate, the Maximinhof, with parcels in the Wehlener Sonnenuhr and Graacher sites. Traditional Mosel Riesling and a riverside cellar door.

Tasting: Tastings and sales by arrangement at the Maximinhof estate in Wehlen.

Uferallee 22, 54470 Bernkastel-Wehlen, Germany →

Weingut Kerpen ★ 4.4

Mosel PradikatsweinRiesling, Spatburgunder (Pinot Noir)Founded 1803Kerpen family (Matthias Kerpen)Winemaker Matthias KerpenWehlen

A nine-generation family estate in Wehlen with parcels in the Wehlener Sonnenuhr, Graacher Domprobst and Bernkasteler sites. Riesling-focused, with a small amount of Spatburgunder.

Tasting: Vinothek and Riesling cafe in the garden; tastings and sales at the Wehlen estate.

Uferallee 6, 54470 Bernkastel-Wehlen, Germany → Book →

Weingut Dr. Pauly-Bergweiler ★ 4.3

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingPauly family (Helmut and Stefan Pauly)Bernkastel-Kues

A Bernkastel estate with holdings in the Bernkasteler alte Badstube am Doctorberg, Wehlener Sonnenuhr and Urziger Wurzgarten, plus the linked Peter Nicolay label. Classic fruity-style Mosel Riesling.

Tasting: Tastings and sales at the Bernkastel-Kues estate; visits by arrangement.

Gestade 15, 54470 Bernkastel-Kues, Germany → Book →

Weingut Wwe. Dr. H. Thanisch - Erben Muller-Burggraef ★ 4.3

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingMuller-Burggraef heirsBernkastel-Kues

One of two Thanisch estates with historic parcels in the Bernkasteler Doctor, based in the Andel district of Bernkastel-Kues. Over 375 years of family winegrowing and classic Mosel Riesling.

Tasting: Open Monday to Friday 10:00 to 17:00 and Saturday 11:00 to 16:00, or by appointment, in Andel.

Junkerland 14, 54470 Bernkastel-Kues, Germany → Book →

Weingut Max Ferd. Richter ★ 4.4

Mosel PradikatsweinRiesling, Muller-ThurgauFounded 1680Richter family (Constantin Richter)Winemaker Constantin RichterMittelmosel

A ninth-generation estate at Mulheim with parcels across the Mittelmosel including the Brauneberger Juffer and a Mulheimer Helenenkloster site noted for Eiswein. Wide range of fruity and dry Riesling.

Tasting: Tastings and sales at the Mulheim estate; visits by arrangement.

Hauptstrasse 85, 54486 Mulheim, Germany → Book →

Weingut Heinrichshof ★ 4.1

Mosel PradikatsweinRiesling, Muller-ThurgauSchmitges-Loosen familyZeltingen-Rachtig

A family estate in Zeltingen-Rachtig with parcels in the Zeltinger Sonnenuhr and Himmelreich. Approachable, fruit-driven Mosel Riesling and everyday whites.

Tasting: Tastings and sales at the Zeltingen-Rachtig estate; visits by arrangement.

Chur-Kolner-Strasse 23, 54492 Zeltingen-Rachtig, Germany → Book →

Weingut Nik Weis - St. Urbans-Hof ★ 4.6

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingFounded 1947Weis family (Nik Weis)Winemaker Nik WeisMittelmosel

A leading Leiwen estate with sites on both the Mosel and Saar, including the Leiwener Laurentiuslay, Ockfener Bockstein and Wiltinger Schlangengraben. Pure, classically styled Riesling for a global market.

Tasting: Tastings and sales at the Leiwen estate; visits by appointment.

Urbanusstrasse 16, 54340 Leiwen, Germany → Book →

Weingut Grans-Fassian ★ 4.5

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRiesling, Weissburgunder (Pinot Blanc)Grans family (Gerhard Grans)Winemaker Gerhard GransMittelmosel

A Leiwen estate with parcels in the Leiwener Laurentiuslay, Trittenheimer Apotheke and Piesporter Goldtropfchen. Precise dry and fruity Riesling plus a little Weissburgunder.

Tasting: Tastings and sales at the Leiwen estate; visits by arrangement.

Romerstrasse 28, 54340 Leiwen, Germany → Book →

Weingut Carl Loewen ★ 4.6

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingLoewen family (Karl Josef and Christopher Loewen)Winemaker Christopher LoewenMittelmosel

A Leiwen estate celebrated for the ungrafted old-vine Maximin Herrenberg and the Laurentiuslay. Christopher Loewen makes detailed dry and fruity Rieslings, including the 1896 Riesling from pre-phylloxera vines.

Tasting: Tastings and sales at the Leiwen estate; visits by arrangement.

Matthiasstrasse 30, 54340 Leiwen, Germany → Book →

Weingut A.J. Adam ★ 4.6

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingAdam family (Andreas Adam)Winemaker Andreas AdamMittelmosel

A small, sought-after grower who revived the steep Dhroner Hofberg. Andreas Adam makes concentrated dry and fruity Rieslings that have become collector favourites.

Tasting: Small estate; tastings and sales by appointment in Neumagen-Dhron.

Metschert 14, 54347 Neumagen-Dhron, Germany → Book →

Weingut Loersch ★ 4.4

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingLoersch family (Alexander Loersch)Winemaker Alexander LoerschMittelmosel

A young, ambitious Leiwen grower working old-vine parcels in the Trittenheimer Apotheke and Leiwener Laurentiuslay. Precise, mostly dry Rieslings from steep slate.

Tasting: Small estate; tastings and sales by appointment in Leiwen.

Tannenweg 11, 54340 Leiwen, Germany → Book →

Weingut Stein ★ 4.4

Mosel PradikatsweinRieslingStein family (Ulrich and Peter Stein)Winemaker Ulrich SteinNaturalMittelmosel

Ulli Stein's individualist estate around St. Aldegund and Alf, known for low-intervention, sometimes provocative Rieslings from steep, near-abandoned slate sites and a hilltop tasting house.

Tasting: Tastings at Haus Waldfrieden above Alf and at the Bullay estate; by arrangement.

Brautrockstrasse 40, 56859 Bullay, Germany → Book →

Weingut Daniel Twardowski ★ 4.5

Mosel PradikatsweinSpatburgunder (Pinot Noir)Daniel TwardowskiWinemaker Daniel TwardowskiMittelmosel

A cult micro-estate proving the slate Mosel can make serious Pinot Noir. Daniel Twardowski's Pinot Noix is grown on Devonian slate and made in a Burgundian, low-yield style.

Tasting: Very small estate; visits and tastings strictly by appointment.

Folzerweg 20, 54347 Neumagen-Dhron, Germany → Book →

Weingut Vereinigte Hospitien ★ 4.3

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRiesling, Weissburgunder (Pinot Blanc)Vereinigte Hospitien charitable foundationTrier

A charitable-foundation estate in Trier sitting atop Roman-era cellars, with parcels in the Scharzhofberg, Serriger and Trier sites. Classic Mosel and Saar Riesling supporting the hospital foundation.

Tasting: Tastings and sales in central Trier; visits to the historic Roman cellar by arrangement.

Krahnenufer 19, 54290 Trier, Germany → Book →

Bischofliche Weinguter Trier ★ 4.2

VDP.Grosse Lage MoselRieslingFounded 1966Bischofliche Weinguter Trier (church foundation)Trier

A large church-owned estate formed in 1966 from historic Trier ecclesiastical wineries, with holdings across the Mosel, Saar and Ruwer and a monumental cellar dating to late antiquity. Broad range of Riesling.

Tasting: Wine shop open Monday to Saturday; guided tastings and tours of the vast historic cellar by booking.

Gervasiusstrasse 1, 54290 Trier, Germany → Book →

Still wine in Piedmont

Gaja ★ 4.9

Barbaresco DOCG, Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Cabernet Sauvignon, Chardonnay, Sauvignon BlancFounded 1859Gaja family (Gaia, Rossana and Giovanni Gaja)Barbaresco DOCG

The Gaja family has made wine in Barbaresco since 1859. Angelo Gaja modernised the estate from the 1960s and pushed single-vineyard Nebbiolo onto the world stage; the house now also farms in Barolo and beyond.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings strictly by appointment in Barbaresco; book months ahead.

Signature wines: sori-san-lorenzo, sori-tildin, barbaresco-gaja

Via Torino 18, 12050 Barbaresco CN, Italy → Book →

Vietti ★ 4.8

Barolo DOCG, Barbaresco DOCG and Barbera d'Asti DOCGNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, Moscato Bianco, ArneisFounded 1919Krause Holdings (Kyle Krause); historically the Vietti-Currado familyBarolo DOCG

A historic Castiglione Falletto house founded in 1919, known for single-cru Barolo (Rocche di Castiglione, Brunate, Ravera) and artist-label bottlings. Acquired by the Krause family in 2016.

Tasting: Guided cellar visits, classic and experience tastings, and a vineyard trekking experience by online booking.

Signature wines: barolo-rocche-di-castiglione, barolo-ravera, barbera-dasti-la-crena

Piazza Vittorio Veneto 5, 12060 Castiglione Falletto CN, Italy → Book →

Bruno Rocca ★ 4.6

Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, ChardonnayFounded 1978Rocca familyBarbaresco DOCG

Bruno Rocca began bottling in 1978 and built a leading Barbaresco estate on the Rabaja cru, making refined, structured Nebbiolo now with the next generation in the cellar.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by reservation on the Rabaja hill in Barbaresco.

Signature wines: barbaresco-rabaja, barbaresco-currà, barbaresco-bruno-rocca

Strada Rabaja 60, 12050 Barbaresco CN, Italy → Book →

G.D. Vajra ★ 4.8

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, Freisa, Moscato Bianco, RieslingFounded 1972Vaira family (Aldo and Milena Vaira)Barolo DOCG

Established in 1972 in Vergne, the highest village in Barolo, the Vaira family farms organically and makes a fragrant, high-toned style led by Barolo Bricco delle Viole alongside a rare Langhe Riesling.

Tasting: Winery visits and tastings on reservation, Monday to Sunday, in the high Vergne hamlet of Barolo.

Signature wines: barolo-bricco-delle-viole, barolo-albe, langhe-riesling-petracine

Via delle Viole 25, Frazione Vergne, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy → Book →

Giacomo Conterno ★ 4.9

Barolo DOCG and Barbera d'Alba DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, FreisaFounded 1908Conterno family (Roberto Conterno)Barolo DOCG

Founded in 1908, Giacomo Conterno is the standard-bearer of traditional Barolo, famous for the Monfortino Riserva from the Cascina Francia and Arione vineyards in Serralunga. Now led by Roberto Conterno.

Tasting: Traditionalist estate behind Barolo Monfortino; visits are limited and by appointment only.

Signature wines: monfortino-barolo-riserva, barolo-cascina-francia

Localita Ornati 2, 12065 Monforte d'Alba CN, Italy → Book →

Bartolo Mascarello ★ 4.8

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, FreisaFounded 1920Mascarello family (Maria Teresa Mascarello)Barolo DOCG

A small, fiercely traditional Barolo house in the village itself, now led by Maria Teresa Mascarello, producing a single blended Barolo from four crus and the famous hand-drawn labels.

Tasting: Tiny traditionalist cellar in the village of Barolo; visits by appointment only.

Signature wines: barolo-bartolo-mascarello, barbera-dalba-bartolo-mascarello, dolcetto-dalba

Via Roma 15, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy → Book →

Giuseppe Mascarello e Figlio ★ 4.7

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, FreisaFounded 1881Mascarello family (Mauro Mascarello)Barolo DOCG

Founded in 1881, Giuseppe Mascarello e Figlio is renowned for the Monprivato cru in Castiglione Falletto, a benchmark of traditional, long-lived Barolo, cellared today at Monchiero.

Tasting: Historic cellar in Monchiero; visits and tastings by appointment.

Signature wines: barolo-monprivato, barolo-villero, barolo-santo-stefano-di-perno

Via Borgonuovo 108, 12060 Monchiero CN, Italy → Book →

Poderi Aldo Conterno ★ 4.8

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Chardonnay, FreisaFounded 1969Aldo Conterno family (Franco, Stefano and Giacomo Conterno)Barolo DOCG

Aldo Conterno split from the family firm in 1969 to found this Bussia estate, now a Monforte benchmark for the Granbussia Riserva and the Cicala, Colonnello and Romirasco single-vineyard Barolos.

Tasting: Visits and tastings by appointment in the Bussia hamlet at the heart of Monforte.

Signature wines: barolo-granbussia-riserva, barolo-cicala, barolo-romirasco

Localita Bussia 48, 12065 Monforte d'Alba CN, Italy → Book →

Marchesi di Gresy ★ 4.6

Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, Chardonnay, Sauvignon BlancFounded 1973Cisa Asinari dei Marchesi di Gresy family (Alberto di Gresy)Barbaresco DOCG

The Gresy family has owned the Martinenga monopole in Barbaresco since 1797 and has bottled its own wine since 1973, making elegant, perfumed Nebbiolo from this prized amphitheatre.

Tasting: Tastings and direct sales by appointment at the Martinenga estate in Barbaresco.

Signature wines: barbaresco-martinenga, barbaresco-camp-gros-riserva, barbaresco-gaiun

Strada della Stazione 21, 12050 Barbaresco CN, Italy → Book →

Elvio Cogno ★ 4.7

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, NascettaFounded 1990Cogno-Fissore family (Valter Fissore and Nadia Cogno)Barolo DOCG

Elvio Cogno left Marcarini in 1990 to found this Novello estate on the Ravera cru. Now run by son-in-law Valter Fissore, it is also a champion of the revived local white grape Nascetta.

Tasting: Visits and tastings by appointment at the 18th-century farmhouse atop Bricco Ravera in Novello.

Signature wines: barolo-ravera, barolo-bricco-pernice, anas-cetta-nascetta

Localita Ravera 2, 12060 Novello CN, Italy → Book →

Sottimano ★ 4.6

Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, DolcettoFounded 1975Sottimano family (Andrea Sottimano)Barbaresco DOCG

Based in the Cotta district of Neive since the mid-1970s, Sottimano makes detailed single-cru Barbaresco from Cotta, Pajore, Curra and Fausoni in a fresh, transparent style.

Tasting: Family Barbaresco estate in the Cotta district of Neive; visits and tastings by appointment.

Signature wines: barbaresco-cotta, barbaresco-pajore, barbaresco-curra

Loc. Cotta, 12052 Neive CN, Italy → Book →

Cordero di Montezemolo ★ 4.5

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Chardonnay, Pinot NeroFounded 1340Cordero di Montezemolo familyBarolo DOCG

One of the oldest landowning families in the Langhe, Cordero di Montezemolo farms the Monfalletto estate in La Morra and the Villero cru, making refined, classically styled Barolo.

Tasting: Tastings and direct sales at the Monfalletto estate in La Morra, beneath its landmark cedar of Lebanon.

Signature wines: barolo-monfalletto, barolo-enrico-vi, barolo-gorette

Frazione Annunziata 67, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy → Book →

Produttori del Barbaresco ★ 4.7

Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe Nebbiolo DOCNebbioloFounded 1958Cooperative of Barbaresco grower-membersBarbaresco DOCG

Founded in 1958, this grower cooperative bottles classic Barbaresco from member vineyards, releasing nine single-cru Riserva bottlings in strong vintages alongside its village Barbaresco.

Tasting: Wine shop and tastings in the main square of Barbaresco, open to the public.

Signature wines: barbaresco-asili-riserva, barbaresco-rabaja-riserva, barbaresco-produttori

Via Torino 54, 12050 Barbaresco CN, Italy → Book →

Michele Chiarlo ★ 4.5

Barbera d'Asti DOCG, Nizza DOCG, Barolo DOCG and Gavi DOCGBarbera, Nebbiolo, Moscato Bianco, CorteseFounded 1956Chiarlo family (Stefano and Alberto Chiarlo)Barbera d'Asti DOCG

Founded in 1956, Michele Chiarlo is one of Monferrato's leading houses, with holdings across Barbera d'Asti, Nizza, Gavi and Barolo and the La Court vineyard art park near Asti.

Tasting: Tastings at the Calamandrana cellar and the open-air Art Park La Court; visits by reservation.

Signature wines: barbera-dasti-la-court, nizza-cipressi, barolo-cerequio

Strada Nizza-Canelli 14, 14042 Calamandrana AT, Italy → Book →

Marchesi di Barolo ★ 4.5

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, Moscato Bianco, CorteseFounded 1807Abbona familyBarolo DOCG

Rooted in the cellars where the modern Barolo style was shaped under the Falletti marquises, Marchesi di Barolo is the village's most historic house, now run by the Abbona family.

Tasting: Guided tours of the historic cellars and tastings daily, by reservation, opposite the castle of Barolo.

Signature wines: barolo-cannubi-marchesi, barolo-sarmassa, barolo-del-comune-di-barolo

Via Roma 1, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy → Book →

La Scolca ★ 4.5

Gavi DOCG and Alta Langa DOCGCorteseFounded 1919Soldati familyGavi DOCG

The Soldati family pioneered premium Gavi at Rovereto, trademarking Gavi dei Gavi in 1969. La Scolca makes age-worthy 100% Cortese whites and traditional-method sparkling wines.

Tasting: Tastings and visits by appointment at the Rovereto di Gavi estate, with Villa Scolca for events.

Signature wines: gavi-dei-gavi-etichetta-nera, gavi-valentino, soldati-la-scolca-brut-millesimato

Strada per Rovereto 170/r, 15066 Gavi AL, Italy → Book →

Pecchenino ★ 4.4

Dogliani DOCG, Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCDolcetto, Nebbiolo, BarberaFounded 1900Pecchenino family (Orazio and Attilio Pecchenino)Dogliani DOCG

A Dogliani family estate that helped raise Dolcetto's profile with its San Luigi and Siri d'Jermu bottlings, and that also makes Barolo from a Monforte parcel.

Tasting: Family estate at Dogliani; cellar visits and tastings by appointment.

Signature wines: dogliani-sirì-d-jermu, dogliani-san-luigi, barolo-le-coste-di-monforte

Borgata Valdiberti 59, 12063 Dogliani CN, Italy → Book →

Matteo Correggia ★ 4.5

Roero DOCG, Roero Arneis DOCG and Barbera d'Alba DOCArneis, Nebbiolo, BarberaFounded 1985Correggia family (Ornella Correggia)ORGANIC CERTIFIEDRoero DOCG

A reference estate of the Roero, founded by Matteo Correggia in 1985 and continued by his family, making organically farmed Arneis whites and structured Nebbiolo-based Roero reds.

Tasting: Family Roero estate at Canale; tastings and visits by appointment.

Signature wines: roero-arneis-val-dei-preti, roero-ròche-d-ampsej, barbera-dalba-marun

Via Santo Stefano Roero 124, 12043 Canale CN, Italy → Book →

Ca' del Baio ★ 4.5

Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, Moscato Bianco, RieslingFounded 1870Grasso familyBarbaresco DOCG

A long-established Treiso family farm near Barbaresco, Ca' del Baio is run by the Grasso sisters and makes classic, well-priced single-cru Barbaresco from Asili, Pora and Vallegrande.

Tasting: Family estate in Treiso; cellar visits and tastings by appointment.

Signature wines: barbaresco-asili, barbaresco-vallegrande, barbaresco-pora

Via Ferrere 33, 12050 Treiso CN, Italy → Book →

Poderi e Cantine Oddero ★ 4.6

Barolo DOCG, Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, Moscato BiancoFounded 1878Oddero family (Isabella Oddero)ORGANIC CERTIFIEDBarolo DOCG

A historic La Morra estate bottling since the 19th century, Oddero owns parcels in many of the greatest Barolo and Barbaresco crus and works to organic certification.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment in the Santa Maria hamlet of La Morra.

Signature wines: barolo-brunate, barolo-bussia-vigna-mondoca-riserva, barolo-rocche-di-castiglione

Borgata Tetti 28, Frazione Santa Maria, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy → Book →

Trediberri ★ 4.5

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, DolcettoFounded 2007Vola family (Nicola Vola)Barolo DOCG

Founded in 2007 by the Vola family, Trediberri is a young La Morra estate that has quickly won acclaim for value-driven, fragrant Barolo from Berri and Rocche dell'Annunziata.

Tasting: Small La Morra estate; visits on Mondays by appointment only.

Signature wines: barolo-del-comune-di-la-morra, barolo-rocche-dell-annunziata, barolo-berri

Borgata Torriglione 4, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy → Book →

Cantine Damilano ★ 4.4

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, ArneisFounded 1890Damilano familyBarolo DOCG

A Barolo family house since 1890, Damilano is closely associated with the Cannubi cru and runs a busy tasting shop in the centre of Barolo village.

Tasting: Wine shop and tastings daily on Via Roma in Barolo; cru-focused experiences by booking.

Signature wines: barolo-cannubi, barolo-lecinquevigne, barolo-liste

Via Roma 31, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy → Book →

Pio Cesare ★ 4.7

Barolo DOCG, Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, Cortese, Arneis, ChardonnayFounded 1881Pio Boffa family (Federica Rosy Boffa)Langhe DOC

Founded in 1881 and still cellaring within Alba's ancient Roman walls, Pio Cesare is one of Piedmont's historic houses, making both classic blended Barolo and the single-vineyard Ornato.

Tasting: Tours and tastings by appointment, Monday to Saturday morning, in the historic Alba cellars inside the Roman walls.

Signature wines: barolo-ornato, barolo-pio-cesare, barbaresco-il-bricco

Via Cesare Balbo 4, 12051 Alba CN, Italy → Book →

Paolo Scavino ★ 4.7

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, DolcettoFounded 1921Scavino family (Enrico Scavino with daughters Enrica and Elisa)Barolo DOCG

Founded in 1921, Paolo Scavino is a modern-classic Castiglione Falletto estate famed for the single-vineyard Bric del Fiasc and a portfolio of Barolo crus across the zone.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at Castiglione Falletto.

Signature wines: barolo-bric-del-fiasc, barolo-cannubi, barolo-rocche-dell-annunziata-riserva

Via Alba Barolo 157, 12060 Castiglione Falletto CN, Italy →

Massolino ★ 4.7

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, Chardonnay, Moscato BiancoFounded 1896Massolino familyBarolo DOCG

Founded in 1896, Massolino is a Serralunga d'Alba mainstay producing structured, classic Barolo led by the Vigna Rionda Riserva from one of the zone's greatest crus.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment in the village of Serralunga d'Alba.

Signature wines: barolo-vigna-rionda-riserva, barolo-parafada, barolo-margheria

Piazza Cappellano 8, 12050 Serralunga d'Alba CN, Italy → Book →

Ceretto ★ 4.7

Barolo DOCG, Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, Arneis, Moscato BiancoFounded 1937Ceretto familyBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDLanghe DOC

Founded in 1937 and expanded by brothers Bruno and Marcello Ceretto, the house farms organically across Barolo and Barbaresco crus and is known for the Bricco Rocche glass cube and Blange Arneis.

Tasting: Tastings by appointment at the estate near Alba; the family also runs the Michelin-starred Piazza Duomo in Alba and the Acino glass tasting pod at Bricco Rocche.

Signature wines: barolo-bricco-rocche, barbaresco-asili, blange-arneis

Localita San Cassiano 34, 12051 Alba CN, Italy → Book →

Luciano Sandrone ★ 4.8

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, DolcettoFounded 1978Sandrone family (Barbara Sandrone and Luca Sandrone)Barolo DOCG

Luciano Sandrone started bottling his own Barolo in 1978 and built one of the Langhe's most admired modern estates, led by the Cannubi Boschis cru, renamed Aleste from the 2013 vintage.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment between Barolo and Castiglione Falletto.

Signature wines: barolo-cannubi-boschis-aleste, barolo-le-vigne, dolcetto-dalba

Via Pugnane 4, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy → Book →

Renato Ratti ★ 4.6

Barolo DOCG, Barbera d'Alba DOC and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, DolcettoFounded 1965Ratti family (Pietro Ratti)Barolo DOCG

Renato Ratti pioneered single-vineyard Barolo at Marcenasco in 1965 and mapped the zone's crus. The La Morra estate, now led by son Pietro, sits by the 15th-century Annunziata abbey.

Tasting: Tastings and visits by appointment beside the historic Abbey of the Annunziata in La Morra.

Signature wines: barolo-rocche-dell-annunziata, barolo-marcenasco, barolo-conca

Frazione Annunziata 7, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy → Book →

Roberto Voerzio ★ 4.7

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, MerlotFounded 1986Voerzio familyBarolo DOCG

Roberto Voerzio set up on his own in 1986 and became famous for radically low yields and meticulous viticulture, producing concentrated, sought-after single-cru Barolo from La Morra.

Tasting: Small low-yield estate; visits strictly by appointment in La Morra.

Signature wines: barolo-la-serra, barolo-brunate, barolo-cerequio

Localita Cerreto 7, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy → Book →

Elio Altare ★ 4.7

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, DolcettoFounded 1976Altare family (Silvia Altare)Barolo DOCG

Elio Altare led the Barolo Boys modernist movement from the 1970s, introducing rotary fermenters and barrique ageing at Cascina Nuova. The La Morra estate is now run by daughter Silvia.

Tasting: Visits and tastings by appointment at Cascina Nuova in the Annunziata hamlet of La Morra.

Signature wines: barolo-arborina, barolo-cerretta, langhe-larigi

Frazione Annunziata 51, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy → Book →

Domenico Clerico ★ 4.6

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, DolcettoFounded 1976Domenico Clerico estate (Giuliana Clerico)Barolo DOCG

Founded in 1976, Domenico Clerico was a leading Barolo modernist, building a sleek Monforte cellar and crafting concentrated single-vineyard Barolo such as Ciabot Mentin and Pajana.

Tasting: Modern winery with tastings by appointment in the Manzoni hamlet of Monforte d'Alba.

Signature wines: barolo-ciabot-mentin, barolo-pajana, barolo-percristina

Localita Manzoni 67, 12065 Monforte d'Alba CN, Italy → Book →

Conterno Fantino ★ 4.5

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, ChardonnayFounded 1982Conterno and Fantino familiesORGANIC CERTIFIEDBarolo DOCG

Founded in 1982 by the Conterno and Fantino families, this Monforte estate farms organically on the Ginestra and Mosconi hills and makes modern, age-worthy single-cru Barolo.

Tasting: Hilltop Monforte winery with a tasting room overlooking the vineyards; visits by reservation.

Signature wines: barolo-sori-ginestra, barolo-vigna-del-gris, barolo-mosconi

Via Ginestra 1, 12065 Monforte d'Alba CN, Italy → Book →

Mauro Veglio ★ 4.5

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, DolcettoFounded 1992Veglio family (Mauro and Daniela Veglio)Barolo DOCG

Mauro Veglio began estate-bottling in 1992 at Cascina Nuova in La Morra, making modern, polished single-vineyard Barolo from Arborina, Gattera and Rocche dell'Annunziata.

Tasting: La Morra estate at Cascina Nuova in Annunziata; visits and tastings by appointment.

Signature wines: barolo-arborina, barolo-rocche-dell-annunziata, barolo-gattera

Cascina Nuova 50, Frazione Annunziata, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy → Book →

Albino Rocca ★ 4.5

Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, ChardonnayFounded 1958Rocca family (Daniela, Monica and Paola Rocca)Barbaresco DOCG

A family Barbaresco house developed under Angelo Rocca and now run by his daughters, making polished single-cru Nebbiolo from Ovello, Ronchi and other Barbaresco sites.

Tasting: Family winery offering guided visits and tastings in Barbaresco.

Signature wines: barbaresco-ovello-vigna-loreto, barbaresco-ronchi, barbaresco-angelo

Strada Ronchi 18, 12050 Barbaresco CN, Italy → Book →

Ettore Germano ★ 4.5

Barolo DOCG, Alta Langa DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, Riesling, Chardonnay, Pinot NeroFounded 1856Germano family (Sergio Germano)Barolo DOCG

Five generations on the Cerretta cru in Serralunga, the Germano family makes structured Barolo plus a notable Langhe Riesling and Alta Langa sparkling from higher-elevation plots.

Tasting: Visits and tastings by appointment on the Cerretta cru in Serralunga d'Alba.

Signature wines: barolo-cerretta, barolo-prapo, langhe-riesling-herzu

Localita Cerretta 1, 12050 Serralunga d'Alba CN, Italy → Book →

Braida ★ 4.6

Barbera d'Asti DOCG, Nizza DOCG and Monferrato DOCBarbera, Moscato Bianco, Grignolino, NebbioloFounded 1961Bologna family (Raffaella and Giuseppe Bologna)Barbera d'Asti DOCG

The Bologna family has made Barbera at Rocchetta Tanaro since 1961. Giacomo Bologna's barrique-aged Bricco dell'Uccellone (first 1982) redefined Barbera as a serious, age-worthy red.

Tasting: Guided tastings and cellar visits at Rocchetta Tanaro, with a wine resort on the estate.

Signature wines: bricco-dell-uccellone, ai-suma, bricco-della-bigotta

Via Roma 94, 14030 Rocchetta Tanaro AT, Italy → Book →

Coppo ★ 4.5

Barbera d'Asti DOCG, Nizza DOCG, Asti DOCG and Alta Langa DOCGBarbera, Moscato Bianco, Chardonnay, Pinot Nero, CorteseFounded 1892Coppo familyCanelli DOCG

Founded in Canelli in 1892, Coppo is a Monferrato house known for the barrique-aged Pomorosso Barbera and traditional-method sparkling wines, cellaring in UNESCO-listed tufa caves.

Tasting: Guided visits and tastings in the UNESCO-listed underground cathedral cellars beneath Canelli.

Signature wines: pomorosso-barbera-dasti, coppo-riserva-spumante, moncalvina-moscato-dasti

Via Alba 68, 14053 Canelli AT, Italy → Book →

La Spinetta ★ 4.6

Barbaresco DOCG, Barolo DOCG and Barbera d'Asti DOCGNebbiolo, Barbera, Moscato Bianco, Sauvignon BlancFounded 1977Rivetti familyBarbaresco DOCG

The Rivetti family began with Moscato in 1977 and moved into single-vineyard Barbaresco and Barolo, marking its top crus with the rhinoceros label. Known for a polished, modern style.

Tasting: Visits and tastings by reservation at Castagnole delle Lanze, usually mid-morning or afternoon.

Signature wines: barbaresco-vigneto-gallina, barbaresco-vigneto-starderi, barolo-campe

Via Annunziata 17, 14054 Castagnole delle Lanze AT, Italy → Book →

Giacomo Borgogno e Figli ★ 4.5

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, FreisaFounded 1761Farinetti family (Oscar Farinetti)Barolo DOCG

Founded in 1761, Borgogno is one of the oldest Barolo houses, known for traditional long ageing and a deep library of Riserva bottlings, on the main square of Barolo village.

Tasting: Tours and tastings in the historic Barolo-village cellars, with a panoramic terrace; visits via the booking platform.

Signature wines: barolo-liste, barolo-cannubi, barolo-riserva

Via Gioberti 1, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy → Book →

E. Pira e Figli - Chiara Boschis ★ 4.6

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, DolcettoFounded 1980Boschis family (Chiara Boschis)ORGANIC CERTIFIEDBarolo DOCG

Chiara Boschis took over the historic E. Pira cellar in 1990, becoming one of the Langhe's leading women winemakers and farming organically across Cannubi, Mosconi and other crus.

Tasting: Small organic estate in Barolo village; visits and tastings by appointment.

Signature wines: barolo-cannubi, barolo-via-nuova, barolo-mosconi

Via Vittorio Veneto 1, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy → Book →

Prunotto ★ 4.5

Barolo DOCG, Barbaresco DOCG and Barbera d'Asti DOCGNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, ArneisFounded 1904Marchesi AntinoriLanghe DOC

A historic Alba house dating to 1904 and owned by Marchesi Antinori since the 1990s, Prunotto makes single-vineyard Barolo and Barbaresco plus benchmark Barbera d'Asti.

Tasting: Visits to the Cantina di Bussia in Monforte d'Alba by reservation, Friday to Monday.

Signature wines: barolo-bussia, barbaresco-bric-turot, barbera-dasti-costamiole

Corso Barolo 14, 12051 Alba CN, Italy → Book →

Cavallotto Tenuta Bricco Boschis ★ 4.6

Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, Freisa, GrignolinoFounded 1948Cavallotto familyORGANIC CERTIFIEDBarolo DOCG

A family estate farming the Bricco Boschis monopole since the 1940s, Cavallotto makes traditional, long-aged Barolo with organic certification and is a benchmark for Castiglione Falletto.

Tasting: Estate tastings by appointment on the Bricco Boschis hill above Castiglione Falletto.

Signature wines: barolo-bricco-boschis, barolo-vignolo-riserva, barolo-bricco-boschis-vigna-san-giuseppe-riserva

Via Alba-Monforte 104, Bricco Boschis, 12060 Castiglione Falletto CN, Italy → Book →

Fontanafredda ★ 4.5

Barolo DOCG, Alta Langa DOCG and Langhe DOCNebbiolo, Barbera, Dolcetto, Moscato Bianco, Chardonnay, Pinot NeroFounded 1858Eataly group (Oscar Farinetti family)ORGANIC CERTIFIEDBarolo DOCG

Born in 1858 from the estate of King Vittorio Emanuele II, Fontanafredda is the largest contiguous wine estate in the Langhe, farming certified-organic vineyards in Serralunga d'Alba.

Tasting: Tours, tastings and dining at the Villaggio Narrante estate village, including the Michelin-starred Guido restaurant.

Signature wines: barolo-serralunga-dalba, barolo-vigna-la-rosa, alta-langa-contessa-rosa

Via Alba 15, 12050 Serralunga d'Alba CN, Italy → Book →

Still wine in Priorat

Clos Mogador ★ 4.9

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, Garnacha Blanca, MacabeoFounded 1979Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDGratallops

Founded in 1979 by Rene Barbier, Clos Mogador is one of the five estates that revived DOQ Priorat with the celebrated 1989 harvest. The estate's Manyetes plot holds Vinya Classificada status in the DOQ Priorat classification.

Tasting: Visits by appointment; contact the estate directly.

Signature wines: clos-mogador, manyetes, clos-nelin

Cami Manyetes, s/n, 43737 Gratallops, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Alvaro Palacios ★ 5.0

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, Garnacha BlancaFounded 1989Gratallops

Alvaro Palacios established his Priorat estate in 1989 as one of the five founding producers of modern Priorat. His L'Ermita vineyard in Bellmunt del Priorat holds Gran Vinya Classificada status, the highest tier of the DOQ Priorat classification.

Tasting: Visits strictly by appointment.

Signature wines: lermita, finca-dofi, gratallops-vi-de-vila, camins-del-priorat, manyetes

Afores s/n, 43738 Gratallops, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Clos i Terrasses (Clos Erasmus) ★ 4.9

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Syrah, Cabernet SauvignonFounded 1989Daphne GlorianWinemaker Daphne GlorianBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDGratallops

Founded in 1989 by Daphne Glorian as one of the five founding estates of modern Priorat. Now operating as Clos i Terrasses, the estate produces the Erasmus and Laurel cuvees from terraced Garnacha plots in Gratallops.

Tasting: Visits by appointment only; very limited production boutique estate.

Signature wines: clos-erasmus, laurel

Afores, s/n, 43737 Gratallops, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Mas Martinet Viticultors ★ 4.8

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Syrah, Merlot, Cabernet SauvignonFounded 1981Perez Ovejero familyWinemaker Sara PerezBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDGratallops

Founded by Josep Lluis Perez in 1981 and now led by his daughter Sara Perez, Mas Martinet is one of the five founding estates of modern Priorat. The estate produces ecologically farmed wines from steep llicorella slate plots.

Tasting: Unique tasting experiences in the vineyard by appointment.

Signature wines: clos-martinet, martinet-bru, cami-pesseroles

Carretera Falset-Gratallops km 6, 43730 Falset, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Clos de l'Obac / Costers del Siurana ★ 4.8

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, Merlot, Tempranillo, Cariñena, Garnacha Blanca, Macabeo, Xarel-lo, MuscatFounded 1989Winemaker Mariona JarqueGratallops

Founded in 1989 by Carles Pastrana as one of the five original Priorat pioneers. Winemaker Mariona Jarque leads production. Their fixed-coupage approach uses consistent vineyard parcels and blend percentages each vintage.

Tasting: Guided visits by arrangement; contact visits@obac.es.

Signature wines: clos-de-lobac, miserere, kyrie

Cami Manyetes, 43737 Gratallops, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Cellers de Scala Dei ★ 4.7

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Garnacha BlancaFounded 1878Escaladei

Established in 1878 at the site of the 12th-century Carthusian monastery of Escaladei, the origin of organised wine cultivation in Priorat. Specialises in Garnacha from high-altitude mountain vineyards near Montsant.

Tasting: Guided visits of winery and heritage site; book through the estate website.

Signature wines: scala-dei-cartoixa, scala-dei-prior, scala-dei-masdeu

Rambla Cartoixa, S/N, 43379 Escaladei, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Terroir al Limit ★ 4.9

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Garnacha Blanca, Pedro XimenezFounded 2002Winemaker Dominik HuberBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalTorroja del Priorat

Founded in 2002 by Dominik Huber, Terroir al Limit champions minimal-intervention winemaking and biodynamic viticulture across steep single-vineyard plots in Torroja del Priorat and surrounding villages.

Tasting: Visits by appointment at their cellar in Torroja del Priorat.

Signature wines: les-manyes, les-tosses, pedra-de-guix, arbossar, dits-del-terra

Carrer Major, 43737 Torroja del Priorat, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Celler Vall Llach ★ 4.8

DOQ PrioratCariñena, Garnacha, Merlot, SyrahFounded 1997Enric Costa and Lluis LlachPorrera

Founded in the early 1990s by singer Lluis Llach and his partner Enric Costa in Porrera. Mas de la Rosa holds Gran Vinya Classificada status, the highest tier in DOQ Priorat. The estate is known for Cariñena-dominant blends.

Tasting: Guided visits by appointment at the Porrera cellar.

Signature wines: mas-de-la-rosa, idus-de-vall-llach, embruix-de-vall-llach

Carrer Pont, 9, 43739 Porrera, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Celler Mas Doix ★ 4.8

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, CariñenaFounded 1998Llagostera familyWinemaker Sergi BatetBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDPoboleda

Established in 1998 by the Llagostera family, continuing a tradition from 1850. Winemaker Sergi Batet applies organic and biodynamic practices. The 1902 Tossal d'en Bou cuvee holds Gran Vinya Classificada status.

Tasting: Wine tourism experiences available; contact info@masdoix.com.

Signature wines: mas-doix-carinena-centenaria, salanques-mas-doix, doix-costers-vinyes-velles

Carretera T-702, Km. 4, 43376 Poboleda, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Ferrer Bobet ★ 4.7

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Cabernet Sauvignon, SyrahFounded 2005Sergi Ferrer-Salat and Raul BobetWinemaker Raul BobetPorrera

Founded in 2005 by pharmaceutical entrepreneur Sergi Ferrer-Salat and former Torres technical director Raul Bobet, Ferrer Bobet focuses on old-vine Garnacha and Cariñena from the mountain terrain between Falset and Porrera.

Tasting: Barrels and Aromas (1.5 hrs, 35 euros) or From Vine to Glass (2.5 hrs, 50 euros); book via website.

Signature wines: ferrer-bobet-vinyes-velles, ferrer-bobet-selecci-especial

Carretera de Falset a Porrera (T-740) Km 6.5, 43730 Falset, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Finca Mas d'en Gil ★ 4.7

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Garnacha Blanca, MacabeoFounded 1998Rovira Carbonell familyWinemaker Marta Rovira CarbonellCAAEBellmunt del Priorat

The Rovira Carbonell family acquired this 125-hectare estate in 1998 and introduced CAAE-certified organic farming under Marta Rovira Carbonell. Based in Bellmunt del Priorat within the Bellmunt Vi de Vila appellation.

Tasting: Mon-Fri 10:30, 12:00, 15:30; Sat 10:30, 12:00; advance booking required. Email visita@masdengil.com.

Signature wines: coma-vella, clos-fonta, bellmunt-mas-den-gil

Ctra. de Falset a Bellmunt, km 4, 43738 Bellmunt del Priorat, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Vinicola del Priorat ★ 4.3

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Garnacha Blanca, MacabeoFounded 1917Gratallops

Established in 1917, Vinicola del Priorat is the only cooperative managing the entire process from vine to commercialisation across Gratallops, El Lloar, La Vilella Alta and La Vilella Baixa. Produces the Onix range from over 200 hectares of old-vine parcels.

Tasting: Wine and olive oil shop on site; cooperative welcomes visitors.

Signature wines: onix-classic, onix-evolucio, onix-fusio

C/ Piro, s/n, 43737 Gratallops, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Celler Pasanau ★ 4.4

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Cabernet Sauvignon, MerlotFounded 1995Pasanau familyORGANIC CERTIFIEDLa Morera de Montsant

A five-generation family winery in the northern DOQ Priorat, where the Pasanau brothers established the modern estate in 1995. High-altitude plots from 540 to 740 metres elevation with organic farming.

Tasting: Enoturisme experiences available; book through the estate.

Signature wines: finca-la-planeta, la-morera-de-montsant, ceps-nous

C/ de la Bassa, s/n, 43361 La Morera de Montsant, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Marco Abella ★ 4.5

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, Garnacha BlancaFounded 2005David Marco and Olivia BayesPorrera

The Marco family has cultivated vineyards in Porrera since the 15th century; David Marco and Olivia Bayes built the modern winery in 2005. Produces seven red and two white cuvees in DOQ Priorat.

Tasting: Enoturisme experiences available; contact enoturisme@marcoabella.com.

Signature wines: mas-mallola, loidana, olbia

Carretera Porrera a Cornudella del Montsant, Km. 0.7, 43739 Porrera, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Buil and Gine ★ 4.4

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, Merlot, Garnacha BlancaFounded 1996Buil and Gine familiesGratallops

Founded in 1996 by the Buil and Gine families, with roots six generations deep in Priorat. The first bodega in the region purposely built for wine tourism, featuring a hotel and restaurant Amics on the estate.

Tasting: First winery in Priorat built for tourism; wine experiences, 4-star hotel and restaurant Amics on site.

Signature wines: gine-gine, joan-gine, pleret

Carretera Gratallops-Vilella Baixa Km 11.5, 43737 Gratallops, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Cims de Porrera ★ 4.5

DOQ PrioratCariñena, GarnachaFounded 1996Porrera

Established in 1996 to recover old-vine Cariñena and Garnacha from Porrera's steep llicorella slopes. Led since 2004 by Adria and Marc Perez together with around 20 local growers from the Porrera cooperative.

Tasting: Tastings and visits by arrangement with the cooperative.

Signature wines: cims-de-porrera-classic, cims-de-porrera-vi-de-vila

Ctra. de Torroja s/n, 43739 Porrera, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Mas Igneus ★ 4.4

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Garnacha BlancaFounded 1997ORGANIC CERTIFIEDGratallops

A pioneer of organic viticulture in Priorat since 1997, Mas Igneus operates a modern winery in Gratallops and produces a range from the entry-level FA 112 to the premium Costers de l'Ermita Carignan.

Tasting: Visits open 11:00 to 15:00; contact info@masigneus.com.

Signature wines: mas-igneus-fa-112, mas-igneus-ig

Ctra T-710 km 11.1, 43737 Gratallops, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Sao del Coster ★ 4.6

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, CariñenaFounded 2004Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalGratallops

Established in 2004 in Gratallops, Sao del Coster is a biodynamic-practising estate working with old Garnacha and Cariñena vines, some over 100 years old, on steep llicorella terraces.

Tasting: Visits by arrangement; contact through website.

Signature wines: sao-roig, sao-blanco

Cami de la Font, s/n, 43737 Gratallops, Tarragona, Spain →

Bodegas Mas Alta ★ 4.5

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Cabernet Sauvignon, SyrahFounded 1999La Vilella Alta

Founded in 1999 on the historic Mas Romani estate in La Vilella Alta. First wines were released in 2004, focusing on old-vine Garnacha and Cariñena from steep llicorella terraces in the DOQ Priorat.

Tasting: Visits available; contact info@bodegasmasalta.com.

Signature wines: la-creu-alta, artigot, bodegas-mas-alta-garnacha

Carretera T-702, km 16.8, 43375 La Vilella Alta, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Costers del Priorat ★ 4.4

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, Garnacha BlancaBellmunt del Priorat

Produces eight wines from vineyards across the DOQ Priorat, including parcels planted in 1934 in Bellmunt, Torroja and other villages. The Memories ranci draws on a solera from 1927.

Tasting: Visits by arrangement; contact via website.

Signature wines: costers-del-priorat-pissarres, costers-del-priorat-carinyena

Afores, s/n, 43738 Bellmunt del Priorat, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Sangenis i Vaque ★ 4.4

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Cabernet Sauvignon, SyrahFounded 1978Sangenis and Vaque familyPorrera

A family winery founded in 1978 by Pere Sangenis and Coxita Vaque, based on the village square of Porrera. Produces 25,000 to 30,000 bottles annually from 15 hectares of DOQ Priorat vineyards.

Tasting: Visits by arrangement at the village cellar in Porrera.

Signature wines: sangenis-i-vaque-coster-de-les-vinyes, sangenis-i-vaque-el-serral, sangenis-i-vaque-porrera-vi-de-vila

Placa Catalunya, 3, 43739 Porrera, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Celler Rotllan Torra ★ 4.3

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Syrah, Cabernet SauvignonFounded 1984Rotllan Torra familyTorroja del Priorat

A family winery housed since 1984 in a 17th-century building in Torroja del Priorat originally constructed by the Carthusian monks of Scala Dei. Produces small-batch wines from DOQ Priorat.

Tasting: Artisanal winery visits by arrangement; contact comercial@rotllantorra.com.

Signature wines: rotllan-torra-crianza, rotllan-torra-tirant

Calle Balandra, 6, 43737 Torroja del Priorat, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Clos Berenguer ★ 4.2

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Syrah, Cabernet SauvignonEl Molar

A family winery in the DOQ Priorat combining traditional viticulture on llicorella slate soils with modern cellar techniques, producing the Clos de Tafall and MIN cuvees from Garnacha and Cariñena.

Tasting: Wine tourism experiences; contact carolclosberenguer@gmail.com.

Signature wines: clos-berenguer-negre, clos-berenguer-blanc

Celler Cal Pla ★ 4.5

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Syrah, Garnacha BlancaFounded 1814Joan SangenisWinemaker Joan SangenisPorrera

The seventh generation of the Sangenis family at Porrera, with estate history dating to 1814. The modern Celler Cal Pla facility, built in 2017, vinifies Garnacha and Cariñena from the family's Mas d'en Compte vineyard.

Tasting: Visits by appointment at the Porrera cellar.

Signature wines: celler-cal-pla-planots, celler-cal-pla-nou-naix

Established in 2002 by Rhone winemakers on 18 hectares in Torroja del Priorat, producing Garnacha and Cariñena blends from llicorella terraces.

Tasting: Visits by appointment in Falset.

Ester Nin and Carles Ortiz produce low-intervention wines from organically farmed old Cariñena and Garnacha vines in Porrera, using indigenous yeasts and minimal additions.

Tasting: Visits by prior arrangement only; limited allocation.

Signature wines: nit-de-les-animes

Torres Salmos ★ 4.3

Venus la Universal ★ 4.5

Cooperativa Falset Marca (Etim) ★ 4.2

DO Montsant / DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Garnacha Blanca, MacabeoFounded 1920Cooperative membersFalset

Founded in 1920, this Falset cooperative is the region's largest producer under the Etim brand, covering DO Montsant and DOQ Priorat with a wide range from everyday to premium.

Tasting: Open cellar-door shop and tasting room; tasting packs available on site.

Clos Galena ★ 4.2

Cami de l'Horta, s/n, 43736 El Lloar, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Cellers Cecilio ★ 4.1

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, Cariñena, Garnacha BlancaGratallops

A Gratallops-based producer working old Garnacha and Cariñena vines on llicorella, listed in the DOQ Priorat winery directory with village tasting access.

Tasting: Village cellar visits and late-evening tastings by appointment.

Signature wines: cecilio-vi-de-vila-gratallops

Carrer de Dalt, 4, 43736 Gratallops, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Mas d'en Compte ★ 4.1

DOQ PrioratCariñena, Garnacha, Garnacha BlancaPorrera

A Porrera-based estate producing Cariñena-led blends from old terraced vineyards in the Porrera Vi de Vila zone, available at local retailers and through direct sales.

Tasting: Visits by prior appointment.

Signature wines: mas-den-compte-porrera, mas-den-compte-vi-de-vila

Carretera T-702, s/n, 43739 Porrera, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Celler Joan Simo ★ 4.3

DOQ PrioratCariñena, GarnachaJoan Simo familyPorrera

A Porrera producer working old Cariñena and Garnacha vines in the Cariñena heartland of the DOQ, known for the Les Eres old-vine range.

Tasting: Visits by arrangement; contact via DOQ winery directory.

Signature wines: les-eres-vinyes-velles, les-eres-anfora

Carrer Horta, 3, 43739 Porrera, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Celler el Masroig ★ 4.1

DO MontsantGarnacha, Cariñena, Garnacha BlancaFounded 1917Cooperative membersEl Masroig

Founded in 1917, this El Masroig cooperative is one of the Montsant appellation's leading producers, with the Les Sorts range from old Garnacha and Cariñena on Montsant slate.

Tasting: Cellar-door visits and shop in El Masroig village; advance booking recommended.

Signature wines: les-sorts-sycar, les-sorts-blanc, solpost

A small DOQ Priorat estate in El Lloar working old Garnacha and Cariñena vines on the llicorella terraces above the Siurana valley.

Tasting: Visits by prior arrangement.

Signature wines: mas-den-brunell-vi-de-vila

Carrer Sant Roc, 2, 43376 Poboleda, Tarragona, Spain → Book →

Celler Arrels del Priorat ★ 4.1

DOQ PrioratGarnacha, CariñenaGratallops

A small Gratallops producer working old Garnacha and Cariñena vines, known locally for a Vi Ranci oxidative style and white Garnacha from llicorella soils.

Tasting: Village-based visits by arrangement; specialises in Vi Ranci and old-vine styles.

Signature wines: arrels-vi-ranci, arrels-garnatxa-blanca

Still wine in Rhone Valley

E. Guigal ★ 5.0

Cote-Rotie AOC, Condrieu AOC, Hermitage AOC, Cotes du Rhone AOCSyrah, Viognier, Marsanne, Roussanne, GrenacheFounded 1946Cote-Rotie

Ampuis-based negociant and domaine celebrated for the single-vineyard Cote-Rotie La La La trilogy from old Syrah vines.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at Chateau d'Ampuis by appointment

Signature wines: guigal-cote-rotie-la-mouline, guigal-cote-rotie-la-landonne, guigal-cote-rotie-la-turque

Tip: The La La La trilogy (La Mouline, La Landonne, La Turque) are allocated; the Chateau d'Ampuis Cote-Rotie is the most accessible flagship.

Chateau d'Ampuis, 69420 Ampuis, France → Book →

M. Chapoutier ★ 4.9

Hermitage AOC, Crozes-Hermitage AOC, Cotes du Rhone AOCSyrah, Marsanne, Roussanne, Grenache, ViognierFounded 1808Demeter CertifiedECOCERTHermitage

Tain-l'Hermitage negociant under biodynamic conversion since the 1990s, producing benchmark Hermitage Syrah and rare Marsanne whites.

Tasting: Open tasting room in Tain-l'Hermitage; cellar tours by appointment

Signature wines: chapoutier-ermitage-le-pavillon, chapoutier-ermitage-de-lore

Tip: The Braille labels on all bottles reflect the family's commitment to accessibility. The tasting room in town is walk-in friendly.

18 Avenue du Docteur Paul Durand, 26600 Tain-l'Hermitage, France → Book →

Paul Jaboulet Aine ★ 4.8

Hermitage AOC, Crozes-Hermitage AOC, Cotes du Rhone AOCSyrah, Marsanne, Roussanne, GrenacheFounded 1834ECOCERTCrozes-Hermitage

One of the Rhone's oldest negociants, producers of the iconic Hermitage La Chapelle Syrah from the hill's granite flanks in Tain.

Tasting: La Cave by appointment; wine shop open in Tain-l'Hermitage

Signature wines: jaboulet-hermitage-la-chapelle

Tip: La Chapelle from top vintages (1961, 1990, 2010) is among the most age-worthy wines in France.

25 Place du Taurobole, 26600 Tain-l'Hermitage, France → Book →

Domaine Jean-Louis Chave ★ 5.0

Hermitage AOC, Saint-Joseph AOCSyrah, MarsanneFounded 1481Biodynamic PracticingECOCERTSaint-Joseph

Mauves family domaine with Hermitage holdings tracing to the 15th century, producing assemblage Hermitage Syrah and Marsanne from multiple lieux-dits.

Tasting: No public visits. Wines sold on allocation via importers.

Signature wines: chave-hermitage-rouge, chave-hermitage-blanc

Tip: The Cuvee Cathelin is a single-vineyard rarity made only in exceptional vintages.

37 Avenue de Saint-Joseph, 07300 Mauves, France →

Auguste Clape ★ 5.0

Cornas AOCSyrahFounded 1955Cornas

The reference producer of Cornas AOC, farming old Syrah vines on granite terraces. Pierre-Marie Clape continues Auguste's work on the village's most celebrated terroir.

Tasting: By appointment only at the family cellar in Cornas

Signature wines: clape-cornas

Tip: Wines are frequently released later than other Cornas producers; patience rewards with extraordinary mineral concentration.

146 Route Nationale, 07130 Cornas, France →

Thierry Allemand ★ 5.0

Cornas AOCSyrahFounded 1982Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDCornas

Cornas artisan with cult status, producing old-vine Syrah from Chaillot (younger vines) and Reynard (centenarian-vine parcel) with zero intervention.

Tasting: No regular public visits. Allocation-only through importers.

Signature wines: allemand-cornas-reynard, allemand-cornas-chaillot

Tip: Among the most sought-after and hardest-to-find bottles in the entire Rhone Valley.

Quartier de l'Equerre, 07130 Cornas, France →

Vincent Paris ★ 4.6

Cornas AOC, Saint-Joseph AOCSyrahFounded 1997ORGANIC CERTIFIEDCornas

Rising star of Cornas farming granite terraces, producing age-worthy Syrah cuvees named after vine age: Granit 30 and Granit 60.

Tasting: By appointment at the domaine

Signature wines: paris-cornas-granit-30, paris-cornas-granit-60

Tip: The Granit 60 from old vines is a benchmark for granite-terroir Cornas at a fraction of the price of Clape or Allemand.

3 Impasse des Charmes, 07130 Cornas, France →

Domaine Jamet ★ 4.9

Cote-Rotie AOCSyrah, ViognierFounded 1976Cote-Rotie

Benchmark Cote-Rotie domaine producing a single assemblage from multiple Cote Brune and Cote Blonde terraces, known for depth and finesse.

Tasting: By appointment only at the domaine in Ampuis

Signature wines: jamet-cote-rotie

Tip: Jean-Paul and Jean-Luc Jamet blend all their lieux-dits into one estate Cote-Rotie that consistently ranks among the appellation's finest.

Le Vallin, 69420 Ampuis, France →

Rene Rostaing ★ 4.7

Cote-Rotie AOC, Condrieu AOCSyrah, ViognierFounded 1971Cote-Rotie

Ampuis domaine with parcels on La Landonne lieu-dit of the Cote Brune and the Cote Blonde, producing structured, terroir-expressive Cote-Rotie.

Tasting: By appointment at the domaine

Signature wines: rostaing-cote-rotie-la-landonne, rostaing-cote-rotie-cote-blonde

Tip: La Landonne is the most mineral and iron-tinged cuvee; La Vialliere is a more accessible early-drinker.

35 Route de la Cote, 69420 Ampuis, France →

Domaine Stephane Ogier ★ 4.8

Cote-Rotie AOC, Condrieu AOCSyrah, ViognierFounded 1997ECOCERTCote-Rotie

Modern Cote-Rotie producer crafting single-vineyard cuvees from the Cote Brune and Blonde with precision viticulture.

Tasting: Tastings by appointment at the domaine in Ampuis

Signature wines: ogier-cote-rotie-belle-helene, ogier-cote-rotie-lancement

Tip: The Belle Helene single-vine cuvee is among the most expressive Cote-Rotie bottlings of its generation.

3 Chemin du Bac, 69420 Ampuis, France →

Domaine Bonnefond ★ 4.5

Cote-Rotie AOC, Condrieu AOCSyrah, ViognierFounded 1980Cote-Rotie

Brothers Patrick and Christophe Bonnefond farm Cote-Rotie parcels on the Coteaux de Tupin, producing elegant, floral Syrah and classic Viognier.

Tasting: By appointment at the domaine

Signature wines: bonnefond-cote-rotie-coteaux-de-tupin

Le Moidon, 69420 Ampuis, France →

Georges Vernay ★ 4.8

Condrieu AOC, Cote-Rotie AOCViognier, SyrahFounded 1953ECOCERTCondrieu

The domaine credited with saving Condrieu from near-extinction in the 1970s, now run by Christine Vernay with benchmark single-vineyard Viognier.

Tasting: Tasting room in Condrieu; open Monday to Saturday, appointments preferred

Signature wines: vernay-condrieu-coteau-de-vernon, vernay-condrieu-terrasses-de-lempire
