Tip: Coteau de Vernon is the top cuvee from the oldest vines and is the most age-worthy Viognier in the appellation.

1 Route Nationale, 69420 Condrieu, France →

Yves Cuilleron ★ 4.7

Condrieu AOC, Saint-Joseph AOC, Cote-Rotie AOCViognier, Syrah, Marsanne, RoussanneFounded 1987ECOCERTCondrieu

Leading Condrieu domaine producing multiple single-vineyard Viognier bottlings alongside Cote-Rotie and Saint-Joseph.

Tasting: Open tasting room in Chavanay; appointment recommended

Signature wines: cuilleron-condrieu-les-chaillets, cuilleron-condrieu-la-petite-cote

Tip: Les Chaillets is the classic cuvee; Vertige is from the highest-altitude vineyards.

58 Route de Montchardon, 42410 Chavanay, France → Book →

Andre Perret ★ 4.5

Condrieu AOC, Saint-Joseph AOCViognier, Syrah, MarsanneFounded 1984Condrieu

Chavanay domaine producing expressive Condrieu from the Coteau Chery lieu-dit, one of the appellation's most concentrated aromatic terroirs.

Tasting: By appointment at the domaine in Chavanay

Signature wines: perret-condrieu-coteau-chery, perret-condrieu-clos-chanson

17 Route de la Taquete, 42410 Chavanay, France →

Domaine Francois Villard ★ 4.5

Condrieu AOC, Saint-Joseph AOC, Cote-Rotie AOCViognier, Syrah, Marsanne, RoussanneFounded 1991ECOCERTCondrieu

Saint-Michel-sur-Rhone domaine producing ambitious Northern Rhone whites and reds with low-intervention and site-specific focus.

Tasting: Tastings by appointment at the domaine

Signature wines: villard-condrieu-le-grand-vallon, villard-saint-joseph-le-grand-pompee

18 Petit Route de Romans, 42410 Saint-Michel-sur-Rhone, France →

Pierre Gaillard ★ 4.5

Cote-Rotie AOC, Saint-Joseph AOC, Condrieu AOCSyrah, Marsanne, Roussanne, ViognierFounded 1987ECOCERTSaint-Joseph

Malleval domaine with vines across Cote-Rotie, Saint-Joseph and Condrieu, known for rich, concentrated Syrah with reliable terroir expression.

Tasting: By appointment at the domaine

Signature wines: gaillard-cote-rotie-esprit-de-blonde, gaillard-saint-joseph-clos-de-cuminal

42520 Malleval, Loire, France →

Domaine Gonon ★ 4.8

Saint-Joseph AOCSyrah, Marsanne, RoussanneFounded 1963Biodynamic PracticingECOCERTSaint-Joseph

Mauves reference estate for Saint-Joseph, run by brothers Pierre and Jean Gonon on old-vine granite parcels; one of the most elegant Saint-Josephs made.

Tasting: By appointment only at the estate in Mauves

Signature wines: gonon-saint-joseph-rouge, gonon-saint-joseph-blanc

Tip: Wines sell out quickly. The Saint-Joseph blanc from Marsanne and Roussanne is a rare and beautiful white.

10 Rue des Launays, 07300 Mauves, France →

Domaine Alain Graillot ★ 4.6

Crozes-Hermitage AOCSyrah, Marsanne, RoussanneFounded 1985Crozes-Hermitage

Alain Graillot elevated the Crozes-Hermitage appellation's reputation for serious Syrah beginning in 1985; La Guiraude is the old-vine prestige cuvee.

Tasting: Visits by appointment at the domaine

Signature wines: graillot-crozes-hermitage-rouge, graillot-crozes-hermitage-la-guiraude

Tip: The straightforward Crozes rouge is one of the best value reds in the Northern Rhone.

Les Chenes Verts, 26600 Pont-de-l'Isere, France →

Domaine Combier ★ 4.4

Crozes-Hermitage AOCSyrah, Marsanne, RoussanneFounded 1961Biodynamic PracticingECOCERTCrozes-Hermitage

Organic and biodynamic-practicing Crozes-Hermitage estate with the Clos des Grives single-vineyard Syrah as its standout red.

Tasting: Tasting room open weekdays; Saturday by appointment

Signature wines: combier-crozes-clos-des-grives

Tip: Among the most reliable value-for-quality bottlings in the Northern Rhone; biodynamic farming at accessible prices.

Route de Larnage, 26600 Pont-de-l'Isere, France → Book →

Domaine du Tunnel ★ 4.4

Saint-Peray AOC, Cornas AOCMarsanne, Roussanne, SyrahFounded 1994ORGANIC CERTIFIEDSaint-Peray

Saint-Peray specialist producing still and sparkling Marsanne alongside compelling Cornas from adjacent granite terraces.

Tasting: By appointment at the cellar in Saint-Peray

Signature wines: tunnel-saint-peray-marsanne, tunnel-cornas-pur-noir

20 Rue de la Republique, 07130 Saint-Peray, France →

Domaine Belle ★ 4.4

Crozes-Hermitage AOC, Hermitage AOCSyrah, Marsanne, RoussanneFounded 1990ECOCERTCrozes-Hermitage

Larnage Crozes-Hermitage estate with parcels on the Hermitage hill, known for purity of fruit and mineral expression.

Tasting: By appointment at the domaine in Larnage

Signature wines: belle-crozes-hermitage-les-pierrelles, belle-crozes-hermitage-cuvee-louis-belle

Le Mas, 26600 Larnage, France →

Domaine des Lises (Maxime Graillot) ★ 4.5

Crozes-Hermitage AOCSyrah, Marsanne, RoussanneFounded 2012ECOCERTCrozes-Hermitage

Maxime Graillot's own domaine in Mercurol. Old-vine Crozes-Hermitage parcels farmed organically with a site-specific focus.

Tasting: By appointment at the domaine

Signature wines: maxime-graillot-crozes-les-lises

Tip: A producer to follow; each vintage shows increasing terroir precision and elegance.

Route de Romans, 26600 Mercurol, France →

Domaine Faury ★ 4.3

Condrieu AOC, Saint-Joseph AOCSyrah, Viognier, MarsanneFounded 1979ORGANIC CERTIFIEDSaint-Joseph

Chavanay family domaine across Condrieu and Saint-Joseph, producing clean, precise Viognier and Syrah with organic farming.

Tasting: By appointment at the domaine

Signature wines: faury-saint-joseph-rouge, faury-condrieu

La Ribaudy, 42410 Chavanay, France →

Delas Freres ★ 4.5

AOCSyrah, Marsanne, RoussanneFounded 1835Hermitage

Delas Freres, founded 1835 in Tain-l'Hermitage, is a leading Northern Rhone negociant producing benchmark Hermitage rouge, Les Bessards cuvee, and Saint-Joseph.

Tasting: By appointment at the Delas Freres cellars in Tain-l'Hermitage

Signature wines: delas-hermitage-les-bessards, delas-hermitage-domaine-des-tourettes

Tip: The Les Bessards single-parcel Hermitage is the flagship; Saint-Joseph Sainte-Epine is excellent value.

40 Rue Jules Nadi, 26600 Tain-l'Hermitage, France →

Chateau de Beaucastel ★ 5.0

Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOCGrenache, Mourvedre, Syrah, Counoise, Cinsault, Roussanne, Grenache Blanc, ClairetteFounded 1549Demeter CertifiedECOCERTChateauneuf-du-Pape

The Perrin family estate at Courthezon, one of the oldest and most biologically farmed CdP properties, using all 13 permitted varieties and producing the benchmark Roussanne Vieilles Vignes blanc.

Tasting: Visits and tastings by appointment at the estate; wine shop on site

Signature wines: beaucastel-chateauneuf-rouge, beaucastel-roussanne-vieilles-vignes, beaucastel-hommage-a-jacques-perrin

Tip: The Hommage a Jacques Perrin is the prestige red; the Roussanne Vieilles Vignes white is the estate's flagship white wine.

Chemin Gironde, 84350 Courthezon, France → Book →

Chateau Rayas ★ 5.0

Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOCGrenacheFounded 1880ORGANIC CERTIFIEDChateauneuf-du-Pape

The most celebrated estate in Chateauneuf-du-Pape, producing pure old-vine Grenache from shaded, sandy soils with extraordinary longevity.

Tasting: No public visits. Wines available by allocation through importers.

Signature wines: rayas-chateauneuf-rouge, rayas-chateauneuf-blanc

Tip: Emmanuel Reynaud also produces Chateau des Tours Vacqueyras and the Pignan second label; Rayas is extremely rare.

Chemin de Maucoil, 84230 Chateauneuf-du-Pape, France →

Domaine du Vieux Telegraphe ★ 4.9

Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, Cinsault, Clairette, BourboulencFounded 1898ECOCERTChateauneuf-du-Pape

The Brunier family estate on the La Crau plateau, one of Chateauneuf-du-Pape's most consistent properties, farming the stony galets roulees terrain.

Tasting: Tasting room open Monday to Friday; appointment for groups

Signature wines: vieux-telegraphe-la-crau, vieux-telegraphe-la-crau-blanc

Tip: The La Crau plateau stones absorb heat and radiate it at night, defining the opulent fruit of Vieux Telegraphe.

3 Route de Chateauneuf-du-Pape, 84370 Bedarrides, France → Book →

Clos des Papes ★ 4.9

Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOCGrenache, Mourvedre, Syrah, Vaccarese, Counoise, Roussanne, ClairetteFounded 1896ORGANIC CERTIFIEDChateauneuf-du-Pape

Paul-Vincent Avril's family estate, one of Chateauneuf-du-Pape's most consistent producers, making densely structured reds from a large parcel mosaic.

Tasting: By appointment at the estate in Chateauneuf-du-Pape

Signature wines: clos-des-papes-rouge, clos-des-papes-blanc

Tip: The red uses Mourvedre more generously than most CdP estates, lending dark-earth, game-meat character.

13 Avenue Saint-Pierre de Luxembourg, 84230 Chateauneuf-du-Pape, France →

Domaine du Pegau ★ 4.8

Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, Counoise, Cinsault, VaccareseFounded 1987Chateauneuf-du-Pape

The Feraud family domaine producing Chateauneuf-du-Pape of extraordinary concentration and longevity, particularly the special-edition Da Capo cuvee.

Tasting: Tastings by appointment at the estate

Signature wines: pegau-cuvee-da-capo, pegau-cuvee-reservee

15 Avenue Imperiale, 84230 Chateauneuf-du-Pape, France →

Domaine de la Janasse ★ 4.7

Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOC, Cotes du Rhone AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, Counoise, Clairette, Roussanne, Grenache BlancFounded 1973Chateauneuf-du-Pape

Courthezon family estate producing approachable and reliably excellent Chateauneuf-du-Pape including the old-vine Chaupin Grenache cuvee.

Tasting: Tasting room open Monday to Saturday at the domaine

Signature wines: janasse-chateauneuf-chaupin, janasse-chateauneuf-vieilles-vignes

Tip: Cuvee Chaupin from a single old-vine Grenache parcel is a must-try.

29 Chemin du Moulin, 84350 Courthezon, France → Book →

Domaine Charvin ★ 4.7

Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOC, Cotes du Rhone AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, VaccareseFounded 1924Chateauneuf-du-Pape

Laurent Charvin's estate on the northern edge of Chateauneuf-du-Pape produces a single estate red of unusual elegance and purity, prized for finesse over power.

Tasting: By appointment at the domaine near Orange

Signature wines: charvin-chateauneuf

Route de Chateauneuf-du-Pape, 84100 Orange, France →

Clos Saint-Jean ★ 4.6

Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, Counoise, Clairette, RoussanneFounded 1955Chateauneuf-du-Pape

Chateauneuf-du-Pape estate run by brothers Pascal and Vincent Maurel, producing concentrated cuvees including the rare Deus ex Machina prestige bottling.

Tasting: Tastings by appointment at the estate

Signature wines: clos-saint-jean-deus-ex-machina, clos-saint-jean-la-combe-des-fous

Grande Rue, 84230 Chateauneuf-du-Pape, France →

Domaine de Marcoux ★ 4.8

Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOCGrenache, Mourvedre, Syrah, Counoise, Vaccarese, Grenache Blanc, ClairetteFounded 1936Demeter CertifiedECOCERTChateauneuf-du-Pape

Demeter-certified biodynamic Chateauneuf-du-Pape estate producing profound old-vine Grenache of great depth and terroir expression.

Tasting: By appointment at the domaine

Signature wines: marcoux-chateauneuf-vieilles-vignes, marcoux-chateauneuf-classique

Tip: The Vieilles Vignes cuvee from centenarian Grenache is among the most complex and age-worthy in CdP.

Route de Sorgues, 84230 Chateauneuf-du-Pape, France →

Le Vieux Donjon ★ 4.6

Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, Cinsault, VaccareseFounded 1882Chateauneuf-du-Pape

One of CdP's most traditional domaines, the Michel family producing classically-structured Grenache-dominant reds of great consistency.

Tasting: By appointment at the estate in Chateauneuf-du-Pape

Signature wines: vieux-donjon-chateauneuf

1 Rue de l'Eglise, 84230 Chateauneuf-du-Pape, France →

Domaine de la Mordoree ★ 4.7

Tavel AOC, Lirac AOC, Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOCGrenache, Clairette, Mourvedre, Cinsault, Picpoul, Grenache Blanc, BourboulencFounded 1986ECOCERTTavel

Tavel domaine producing the reference rose of the appellation, the Dame Rousse, alongside Lirac red and white and a Chateauneuf-du-Pape.

Tasting: Tasting room open at the domaine in Tavel; appointment preferred

Signature wines: mordoree-dame-rousse-tavel, mordoree-reine-des-bois-lirac-rouge

Tip: La Dame Rousse is the reference Tavel rose; structured enough to age 3-5 years.

250 Chemin des Oliviers, 30126 Tavel, France → Book →

Chateau La Nerthe ★ 4.7

Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, Counoise, Roussanne, Clairette, Grenache BlancFounded 1560ECOCERTChateauneuf-du-Pape

One of the oldest estates in CdP with winemaking cited to the 16th century, producing refined GSM-led reds and whites from organic farming.

Tasting: Open tasting room and shop at the chateau; cellar tours by appointment

Signature wines: nerthe-chateauneuf-rouge, nerthe-cuvee-des-cadettes

Tip: The Cuvee des Cadettes white from very old Roussanne and Clairette vines is a rare and age-worthy white CdP.

4213 Route de Sorgues, 84230 Chateauneuf-du-Pape, France → Book →

Tardieu-Laurent ★ 4.5

Chateauneuf-du-Pape AOC, Gigondas AOC, Hermitage AOC, Cote-Rotie AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, Marsanne, Roussanne, ViognierFounded 1994Chateauneuf-du-Pape

Negociant house sourcing old-vine Rhone fruit across both north and south for barrel-aged cuvees of concentration and depth.

Tasting: By appointment; offices in Lourmarin

Signature wines: tardieu-laurent-chateauneuf-vv, tardieu-laurent-gigondas

Les Grandes Bastides, Route de Cucuron, 84160 Lourmarin, France →

Famille Perrin ★ 4.3

Vinsobres AOC, Cotes du Rhone Villages AOC, Cotes du Rhone AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, Grenache Blanc, ViognierFounded 1992Cotes du Rhone

The Perrin family secondary label (Beaucastel owners) producing Cotes du Rhone, Cotes du Rhone Villages and Vinsobres.

Tasting: Tasting room in Orange; open Monday to Saturday

Signature wines: famille-perrin-reserve-rouge, famille-perrin-les-sinards

Tip: La Vieille Ferme is the entry-level range; the Vinsobres Les Cornuds is a genuinely serious wine at a moderate price.

Route de Jonquieres, 84100 Orange, France → Book →

Domaine Santa Duc ★ 4.7

Gigondas AOCGrenache, Syrah, MourvedreFounded 1874Biodynamic PracticingECOCERTGigondas

Historic Gigondas estate with old-vine parcels across the Dentelles de Montmirail producing structured, spice-laden reds.

Tasting: Tasting room open at the domaine in Gigondas

Signature wines: santa-duc-gigondas-classique, santa-duc-gigondas-prestige

Tip: Aux Relagnes is the classic Gigondas cuvee; Hautes Garrigues is the prestige single-vineyard bottling.

Route de Gigondas, 84190 Gigondas, France → Book →

Domaine Raspail-Ay ★ 4.6

Gigondas AOCGrenache, Syrah, MourvedreFounded 1875Gigondas

One of Gigondas's most respected traditional estates, producing Grenache-dominant reds from old vines on clay-limestone slopes.

Tasting: Tasting room at the domaine; open weekdays

Signature wines: raspail-ay-gigondas

Route de Sablet, 84190 Gigondas, France →

Chateau de Saint Cosme ★ 4.7

Gigondas AOCGrenache, Syrah, MourvedreFounded 1416ECOCERTGigondas

Gigondas estate with recorded winemaking history to the 15th century, run by Louis Barruol as both domaine and Southern Rhone negociant.

Tasting: Tasting room open at the chateau; appointment recommended

Signature wines: saint-cosme-gigondas, saint-cosme-le-claux

Tip: The estate occupies the hillside above the village with Roman-era cellars carved from the rock.

Route du Chateau de Saint-Cosme, 84190 Gigondas, France → Book →

Domaine du Cayron ★ 4.5

Gigondas AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, CinsaultFounded 1960Gigondas

Small traditional Gigondas domaine producing a single estate wine from old vines on the clay-limestone slopes at the foot of the Dentelles de Montmirail.

Tasting: Tasting at the domaine by appointment

Signature wines: cayron-gigondas

Route de Sablet, 84190 Gigondas, France →

Domaine Le Sang des Cailloux ★ 4.8

Vacqueyras AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, CinsaultFounded 1993Biodynamic PracticingECOCERTVacqueyras

The most celebrated Vacqueyras estate, producing intense, mineral-driven Grenache reds that rank among the best value serious wines in the Southern Rhone.

Tasting: By appointment at the domaine

Signature wines: sang-des-cailloux-vacqueyras-rouge

Tip: The Vacqueyras frequently outperforms wines twice its price; production is small and it sells quickly.

Route de Gigondas, 84190 Sarrians, France →

Domaine Brusset ★ 4.4

Cairanne AOC, Gigondas AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, Counoise, CinsaultFounded 1947Cairanne

Cairanne and Gigondas producer with a large portfolio, a reference estate for the newly elevated Cairanne AOC.

Tasting: Open tasting room at the domaine in Cairanne

Signature wines: brusset-cairanne

Route de Sablet, 84290 Cairanne, France → Book →

Domaine La Soumade ★ 4.5

Rasteau AOC, Rasteau VDN AOCGrenache, Syrah, MourvedreFounded 1979Rasteau

Rasteau family domaine producing both the dry appellation red and the traditional Rasteau vin doux naturel (VDN), one of the few maintaining this fortified wine tradition.

Tasting: Tasting room open at the domaine in Rasteau

Signature wines: soumade-rasteau-confiance, soumade-rasteau-vdn

Tip: The Rasteau VDN is a fortified Grenache in Rancio style; a disappearing tradition well worth seeking out.

Route de Rasteau, 84110 Rasteau, France →

Domaine Gourt de Mautens ★ 4.9

IGP VaucluseGrenache, Mourvedre, Syrah, Counoise, CamareseFounded 1990Biodynamic PracticingECOCERTRasteau

The cult producer of Rasteau, deliberately declassifying wines to IGP Vaucluse, farming rare old vine field blends including obscure varieties like Camarese for extraordinary complexity.

Tasting: By appointment at the domaine

Signature wines: gourt-de-mautens-igp

Tip: Production is tiny; wines are sought by collectors globally. One of the great value-to-quality stories in the Southern Rhone.

Route de Cairanne, 84110 Rasteau, France →

Domaine de la Presidente ★ 4.3

Cairanne AOC, Cotes du Rhone Villages AOCGrenache, Syrah, Mourvedre, Grenache Blanc, MarsanneFounded 1963Cairanne

Long-established Cairanne estate well-known for the Grande Garde cuvee from old vines across multiple appellations.

Tasting: Open tasting room; Monday to Saturday

Signature wines: presidente-cairanne

Route de Nyons, 84290 Cairanne, France →

Chateau d'Aqueria ★ 4.6

Tavel AOC, Lirac AOCGrenache, Clairette, Cinsault, Picpoul, Mourvedre, BourboulencFounded 1920Tavel

Tavel reference estate producing one of the most consistent and elegant Tavel roses, farming the galets plateau.

Tasting: Tasting room open at the chateau in Tavel; Monday to Saturday

Signature wines: aqueria-tavel, aqueria-lirac-rouge

Route de Roquemaure, 30126 Tavel, France → Book →

Still wine in Ribera del Duero

Bodegas Vega Sicilia ★ 5.0

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Malbec, AlbilloFounded 1864Quintanilla de Onésimo

The Ribera del Duero estate founded in 1864, producing Unico after a minimum of ten years' ageing and the Valbuena 5 degree as an earlier-release sibling.

Tasting: Visits strictly by appointment; contact the estate directly.

Carretera N-122, km 323, 47359 Valbuena de Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Alión ★ 4.8

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1991Quintanilla de Onésimo

The second estate of the Vega Sicilia group, founded in 1991, producing a 100% Tinto Fino wine aged in French oak with a modern, approachable style relative to its famous sibling Unico.

Tasting: Visits by appointment through the Vega Sicilia group.

Carretera Peñafiel-Encinas, km 2, 47316 Padilla de Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

Dominio de Pingus ★ 5.0

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1995Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDLa Horra

Peter Sisseck's cult estate in La Horra, producing Pingus from a single old-vine parcel and the more accessible Flor de Pingus and PSI from older purchased fruit across the appellation.

La Horra, 09311 La Horra, Burgos, Spain →

Tinto Pesquera ★ 4.8

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1972Pesquera de Duero

Alejandro Fernandez's estate that brought global attention to Ribera del Duero in the 1980s, producing Tinto Pesquera Crianza, Reserva, Gran Reserva and the prestige Janus label from old Tinto Fino vines.

Tasting: Visits and tastings by prior arrangement at the estate.

Calle Real 2, 47315 Pesquera de Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Aalto ★ 4.8

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1999Winemaker Mariano GarcíaQuintanilla de Onésimo

Founded in 1999 at Quintanilla de Arriba by former Vega Sicilia winemaker Mariano García and Javier Zaccagnini, producing single-vineyard PS and the Aalto flagship from old Tinto Fino vines.

Tasting: Visits by appointment.

Paraje Vallejo de Carril s/n, 47360 Quintanilla de Arriba, Valladolid, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Mauro ★ 4.7

VT Castilla y LeónTinto Fino, Garnacha Tinta, SyrahFounded 1980Sardón de Duero

Founded in 1980 by Mariano García at Tudela del Duero, producing the Mauro, Mauro VS and Terreus cuvées as VT Castilla y Leon because some vineyards fall outside the DO boundary, with 90 hectares under vine averaging 35 years.

Carretera de Villabañez km 1, 47320 Tudela del Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

Dominio del Águila ★ 4.8

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Albillo Mayor, Garnacha TintaFounded 2010ORGANIC CERTIFIEDLa Horra

Jorge Monzón's certified-organic estate at La Aguilera producing Pícaro del Águila rosé, Dominio del Águila and single-parcel Peñas Aladas from pre-phylloxera old vines.

La Aguilera, 09317 La Aguilera, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Emilio Moro ★ 4.7

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1987Pesquera de Duero

A four-generation family estate at Pesquera de Duero founded commercially in 1987, producing the Emilio Moro Crianza, the Malleolus range and single-vineyard Malleolus de Valderramiro and Malleolus de Sanchomartín.

Tasting: Wine tourism with visits, tastings, restaurant and wine bar on site.

Tip: Book the on-site restaurant El Rincón de Emilio for local roast lamb after the cellar tour.

Carretera Peñafiel-Valoria s/n, 47315 Pesquera de Duero, Valladolid, Spain → Book →

Pago de Carraovejas ★ 4.8

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Cabernet Sauvignon, MerlotFounded 1987Peñafiel

A landmark estate near Peñafiel famous for its Pago de Carraovejas Crianza and Reserva and the single-vineyard El Anejon, with the Michelin-starred Ambivium restaurant drawing visitors from across Castile.

Tasting: Wine tourism and cellar visits by appointment; on-site Ambivium restaurant holds a Michelin star.

Camino Carraovejas s/n, 47316 Peñafiel, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Arzuaga Navarro ★ 4.6

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Cabernet Sauvignon, MerlotFounded 1993Quintanilla de Onésimo

A family estate and hospitality complex at Quintanilla de Onesimo, producing the Arzuaga Crianza, Reserva, Gran Arzuaga and single-vineyard Fan d'Or, with a hotel and spa on the vineyard.

Tasting: Full wine tourism complex with tastings, hotel, spa and on-site restaurant.

Carretera N-122, km 325, 47350 Quintanilla de Onésimo, Valladolid, Spain →

Hacienda Monasterio ★ 4.7

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc, MalbecFounded 1991Pesquera de Duero

A classic estate near Pesquera de Duero founded in 1991, with veteran consultant winemaker Peter Sisseck shaping the Hacienda Monasterio and Reserva Especial from a diverse five-variety blend.

Carretera Peñafiel-Pesquera km 4, 47315 Pesquera de Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Hermanos Sastre ★ 4.6

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1992La Horra

A La Horra estate founded by the Sastre brothers in 1992, producing the Roble, Crianza, Viña Sastre Reserva and single-parcel Pago de Santa Cruz from old Tinto Fino vines in the Burgos sector.

Camino de la Rábida s/n, 09311 La Horra, Burgos, Spain →

Goyo García Viadero ★ 4.7

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Albillo MayorFounded 2001Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalLa Horra

A natural-wine pioneer in the Burgos sector working with old pre-phylloxera Tinto Fino and Albillo Mayor vines, producing low-intervention wines with minimal sulfur under the Villalbilla and other labels.

Villalbilla de Gumiel, 09314 Villalbilla de Gumiel, Burgos, Spain →

Dominio de Atauta ★ 4.8

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Albillo MayorFounded 2000ORGANIC CERTIFIEDSoria Sub-zone

Specialists in extreme-altitude old-vine Tinto Fino from the Soria sub-zone at Atauta, where pre-phylloxera ungrafted vines at over 1000 metres produce the Llanos del Almendro and single-parcel La Mala cuvées.

Calle Real 21, 42312 Atauta, Soria, Spain →

Pago de los Capellanes ★ 4.6

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Cabernet SauvignonFounded 1996Pedrosa de Duero

A Pedrosa de Duero estate (note: a producer name within DO Ribera del Duero, not a Pago appellation) producing Roble, Crianza, Reserva and the single-vineyard El Nogal and El Picón from estate Tinto Fino vines.

Camino de los Fornos s/n, 09314 Pedrosa de Duero, Burgos, Spain →

Matallana (Telmo Rodríguez) ★ 4.6

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoQuintanilla de Onésimo

Telmo Rodríguez's Ribera del Duero project producing Matallana from old-vine Tinto Fino parcels in the Valladolid sector, applying his low-intervention philosophy shared across his Spanish estate portfolio.

Quintanilla de Onésimo, 47350 Quintanilla de Onésimo, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Protos ★ 4.6

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Albillo MayorFounded 1927Peñafiel

Founded in 1927 as a cooperative under Peñafiel Castle, Protos is the historic anchor of the appellation and the region's most-visited winery, housed in a spectacular Richard Rogers-designed cellar opened in 2008.

Tasting: The most-visited winery in Ribera del Duero, with guided tours open to the public since 2009; book via enoturismo@bodegasprotos.com or +34 659 843 463.

Tip: Combine the cellar tour with a visit to the castle-top Museo Provincial del Vino above.

C/ Bodegas Protos 24-28, 47300 Peñafiel, Valladolid, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Félix Callejo ★ 4.6

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoORGANIC CERTIFIEDPedrosa de Duero

A certified organic estate at Sotillo de la Ribera in Burgos province, where José Félix and daughter Noelia craft village-and single-vineyard Tinto Fino wines from high-altitude limestone soils at 830 to 930 metres.

Avenida del Cid km 16, 09441 Sotillo de la Ribera, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Carmelo Rodero ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Cabernet SauvignonFounded 1990Pedrosa de Duero

A Pedrosa de Duero family estate producing the Carmelo Rodero Crianza, Reserva and 9 Meses, as well as TSM from old vines, with the founding family still at the helm after more than three decades.

Calle Las Escuelas 12, 09314 Pedrosa de Duero, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Pérez Pascuas ★ 4.6

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Cabernet SauvignonFounded 1980Pedrosa de Duero

Three-brother estate at Pedrosa de Duero producing the Viña Pedrosa Crianza, Reserva and Gran Reserva, Finca La Navilla and the Pérez Pascuas Gran Reserva Gran Selección from estate Tinto Fino.

Tasting: Estate visits and wine tourism in Pedrosa de Duero.

Pedrosa de Duero, 09314 Pedrosa de Duero, Burgos, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Valdubón ★ 4.4

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1999Aranda de Duero

A modern Burgos-sector estate at Milagros producing the Valdubón Crianza, Reserva and Élite from high-altitude Tinto Fino vineyards on limestone soils, with a distinctive cave-cellar under the winery.

Carretera N-122 km 297, 09318 Milagros, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Cepa 21 ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 2001Pesquera de Duero

The Moro family's second estate, founded in 2001 at Pesquera de Duero, focused on younger-vine Tinto Fino with the Cepa 21 and Hito entry labels and the premium Malabrigo from selected old parcels.

Carretera Renedo-Pesquera km 35, 47315 Pesquera de Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Tamaral ★ 4.3

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoPeñafiel

A modern estate near Peñafiel crafting value-driven Tinto Fino wines in Crianza, Reserva and Finca la Meda styles, widely available at Spanish restaurants and a good introduction to the appellation.

Carretera N-122, km 310.5, 47315 Peñafiel, Valladolid, Spain →

Comenge ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Cabernet Sauvignon, Albillo MayorORGANIC CERTIFIEDCuriel de Duero

An organic estate at Curiel de Duero producing Don Miguel Comenge, Familia Comenge and the Biberius entry range, plus an Albillo Mayor white and rosé from their castle-view vineyard overlooking the Duero.

Camino del Castillo s/n, 47316 Curiel de Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Montebaco ★ 4.4

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1994Valbuena de Duero

An estate between Pesquera de Duero and Valbuena de Duero converting a former dairy farm, with the Montebaco Crianza first released in 1994 from old Tempranillo clones and the premium Semele range.

Finca Monte Alto, 47359 Valbuena de Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodega Matarromera ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Albillo MayorValbuena de Duero

Carlos Moro's estate at Valbuena de Duero producing Matarromera Crianza, Reserva and Gran Reserva alongside the Melior range, with vineyards between Olivares and Valbuena and a focus on balanced, elegant Tinto Fino.

Carretera Renedo-Pesquera km 30, 47359 Valbuena de Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Cillar de Silos ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1994La Horra

A Burgos-sector estate at Quintanamanvirgo producing Cillar de Silos Roble, Crianza, Reserva and the prestige Torresilo single-vineyard from old-vine Tinto Fino, well regarded for consistency and quality-to-price ratio.

Carretera de La Horra, 09370 Quintanamanvirgo, Burgos, Spain →

Viñas del Jaro ★ 4.3

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoPeñafiel

A Peñafiel area estate producing the Jaro and Pago de los Capellanes sibling ranges, known for well-structured Crianza and Reserva at accessible price points from the Valladolid sector.

Ctra. Peñafiel-Valoria km 1, 47316 Peñafiel, Valladolid, Spain →

Resalte de Peñafiel ★ 4.4

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1997Peñafiel

A Peñafiel estate producing the Resalte Gran Resalte, Crianza and Viña Resalte lines from estate Tinto Fino vineyards at the foot of Peñafiel castle, with a tasting room open to visitors.

Avenida de la Constitución 10, 47300 Peñafiel, Valladolid, Spain →

Vega Real ★ 4.3

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoValbuena de Duero

A well-regarded family estate at Valbuena de Duero producing the Vega Real Roble, Crianza, Reserva and prestige Pagos de Vega Real from limestone-rich vineyards beside the river.

Carretera N-122 km 321.5, 47350 Valbuena de Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

Convento San Francisco ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoLa Horra

A La Horra estate occupying a former Franciscan convent, crafting single-vineyard Tinto Fino wines of depth from the high-altitude Burgos sector, with the historic stone cellar providing ideal natural temperature for ageing.

Calle Bodegas s/n, 09311 La Horra, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Briego ★ 4.4

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoRoa de Duero

A family estate near Roa de Duero on the Burgos plateau producing the Briego Crianza, Reserva, Fiel and single-parcel Infidel from Tinto Fino vines on chalky limestone clay soils.

Ctra. Roa-La Horra km 2, 09312 Roa de Duero, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas y Viñedos Neo ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoAranda de Duero

A Burgos-area estate at Castrillo de la Vega producing the Neo Punta Esencia, Compromiso and Prieto from old-vine Tinto Fino, with a reputation for concentrated, structured reds from the eastern Burgos sub-zone.

Camino Canalejas s/n, 09318 Castrillo de la Vega, Burgos, Spain →

Legaris ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Cabernet SauvignonFounded 2000Curiel de Duero

A Torres family estate at Curiel de Duero founded in 2000 producing the Legaris Crianza, Reserva and Premium from estate Tinto Fino, housed in a modern gravity-fed winery designed to minimise intervention.

Carretera Curiel-Peñafiel s/n, 47316 Curiel de Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Trus ★ 4.3

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoBocos de Duero

An estate near Valladolid producing the Trus Roble, Crianza, Reserva and the premium Trus 12 Meses from carefully selected Tinto Fino parcels on the western edge of the appellation.

Carretera de Traspinedo s/n, 47012 Valladolid, Spain →

Hacienda Solano ★ 4.3

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoAranda de Duero

A Burgos-province estate near Hontangas producing the Hacienda Solano Crianza, Reserva and the Señorío de Solano range from parcels on the chalk-clay high plateau east of Aranda de Duero.

Carretera de Aranda km 3.5, 09318 Hontangas, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Vizcarra ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 2000Pedrosa de Duero

Juan Carlos Vizcarra's family estate at Mambrilla de Castrejón producing Vizcarra Roble, Crianza, Senda del Oro and the prestige Vizcarra 15 Meses from high-altitude Tinto Fino, respected for freshness and balance.

Carretera de Aranda km 1, 09314 Mambrilla de Castrejón, Burgos, Spain →

Astrales ★ 4.7

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1998La Horra

A small La Aguilera estate focused on single-vineyard old-vine Tinto Fino, producing just two labels, Astrales and Christina (a second wine), with the winery respected by critics for its consistently elegant interpretation.

Calle Las Eras s/n, 09317 La Aguilera, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Reyes ★ 4.4

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoPedrosa de Duero

A Pedrosa de Duero estate producing the Teófilo Reyes Crianza, Reserva and Gran Reserva from old-vine Tinto Fino, a historic bodega that influenced a generation of Ribera winemakers in the 1980s.

Carretera de Aranda s/n, 09314 Pedrosa de Duero, Burgos, Spain →

Pradorey ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Albillo MayorLa Horra

A La Horra estate producing the Pradorey Élite, Reserva and the Albillo Mayor white, with modern winemaking in a 21st-century gravity-flow cellar and a hotel for wine-tourism stays.

Carretera de Gumiel km 1, 09311 La Horra, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Aster ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 2000Aranda de Duero

The La Rioja Alta group's Ribera del Duero estate at Anguix, applying the same philosophy of long oak ageing from the Rioja parent to produce Aster Crianza, Reserva and the prestige Finca el Otero from estate Tinto Fino.

Ctra. Nacional 122 km 297, 09318 Anguix, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas O Fournier ★ 4.4

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Garnacha TintaFounded 2000Roa de Duero

The Spanish arm of the Fournier family group at Berlangas de Roa, producing Urban Ribera, Alfa Spiga and the O Fournier prestige label from Tinto Fino and Garnacha in the Burgos sector.

Ctra. Olmedillo de Roa s/n, 09316 Berlangas de Roa, Burgos, Spain →

Convento de Oreja ★ 4.3

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoPeñafiel

An estate near Peñafiel producing Convento de Oreja Crianza, Reserva and Selección from Tinto Fino, named for the historic convent that once occupied the site and known for their accessible, well-balanced style.

Calle Convento s/n, 47315 Peñafiel, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Tarsus ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1994La Horra

A La Horra estate producing the Tarsus Crianza, Reserva and the Quintus prestige label from high-altitude Tinto Fino vineyards in the Burgos sector, with consistent critical recognition for their structured, age-worthy style.

Carretera de Roa km 2, 09311 La Horra, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Vatan ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoLa Horra

A small La Horra producer crafting the Vatan Ribera del Duero from old-vine Tinto Fino parcels on calcareous clay soils in the Burgos province, with a reputation for extractive, age-worthy style at the premium tier.

Carretera de Aranda s/n, 09311 La Horra, Burgos, Spain →

Hacienda Zorita ★ 4.3

VT Castilla y LeónTinto Fino, Cabernet Sauvignon, SyrahORGANIC CERTIFIEDSardón de Duero

An organic estate in Salamanca province outside the DO boundary classified as VT Castilla y León, producing the Hacienda Zorita Natural Reserve and Reserva from Tinto Fino and international varieties with a hotel and ecotourism complex.

Carretera Salamanca-Valladolid km 310.5, 37200 Ledesma, Salamanca, Spain →

Bodegas Vizcarra Ramos ★ 4.3

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoAranda de Duero

A Fuentelcesped estate in the Burgos sector producing concentrated Tinto Fino wines in Crianza and Reserva styles from clay-limestone soils on the eastern plateau near Aranda de Duero.

Carretera Aranda-Roa km 6, 09316 Fuentelcésped, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Leda ★ 4.5

VT Castilla y LeónTinto FinoQuintanilla de Onésimo

A Quintanilla de Onésimo project vinifying old-vine Tinto Fino sourced from across the plateau, classified as VT Castilla y León due to multi-province sourcing, producing the Leda and Mas de Leda from ungrafted centenarian vines.

Camino de la Granja s/n, 47350 Quintanilla de Onésimo, Valladolid, Spain →

Rolland and Galarreta ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 2005La Horra

A La Horra project between Michel Rolland and Spanish partner Iñigo Galarreta producing a single Ribera del Duero from old-vine Tinto Fino parcels in the Burgos sector, advised by the celebrated Bordeaux consultant.

Camino de la Vid s/n, 09311 La Horra, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Mas Que Vinos ★ 4.4

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Albillo MayorBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalValbuena de Duero

A biodynamic-practicing estate at Valbuena de Duero producing the Ercavio and Alma del Duero ranges from Tinto Fino and Albillo Mayor with a natural wine philosophy, minimal intervention and low added sulphur.

Calle Cabeza Gorda s/n, 47359 Valbuena de Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Roa ★ 4.3

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoRoa de Duero

An estate anchored to the historic town of Roa de Duero, producing the Costera, Crianza and Sylis range from Tinto Fino on the limestone-chalk plateau above the Duero in Burgos province.

Camino Real s/n, 09311 Roa de Duero, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Condado de Haza ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1989La Horra

Alejandro Fernandez's second Ribera estate at La Horra, producing Condado de Haza Roble and Crianza alongside the premium Alenza Gran Reserva, sharing the same Tinto Fino philosophy as Tinto Pesquera.

Carretera N-122 km 325, 09318 La Horra, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Díez Llorente ★ 4.3

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoPesquera de Duero

A small family estate at Pesquera de Duero producing the Díez Llorente Crianza and Reserva from estate Tinto Fino, with a focus on terroir expression from the clay-limestone soils of the Valladolid valley floor.

Camino de los Praos s/n, 47315 Pesquera de Duero, Valladolid, Spain →

García Figuero ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 1998La Horra

A La Horra family estate producing the García Figuero Roble, Crianza, Reserva, Tinto 15 Meses and the premium Único label from estate old-vine Tinto Fino, widely distributed and a reliable benchmark for the La Horra sub-zone.

Camino de las Viñas s/n, 09311 La Horra, Burgos, Spain →

Alonso del Yerro ★ 4.6

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoFounded 2003Quintanilla de Onésimo

A small estate at Quintanilla de Onésimo producing the Alonso del Yerro and María single-vineyard labels from old-vine Tinto Fino, with consulting input from Stéphane Derenoncourt, focused on freshness and precision.

Carretera N-122 km 325.5, 47359 Quintanilla de Onésimo, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Portia ★ 4.5

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Garnacha TintaFounded 2010Aranda de Duero

A Gumiel de Izán estate notable for its Foster + Partners-designed star-shaped winery building, producing the Portia Prima, Ebeia and Triennia from Tinto Fino with a strong wine-tourism programme.

Tip: The Norman Foster-designed star winery is one of the most photographed wine buildings in Spain.

Carretera de Gumiel km 3, 09300 Gumiel de Izán, Burgos, Spain →

Bodegas Emina ★ 4.4

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Cabernet Sauvignon, Albillo MayorPeñafiel

A large Peñafiel estate within the Matarromera group, producing the Emina Crianza, Reserva and single-vineyard Emina Atio, with a Centro de Interpretación Vitivinícola museum and hospitality complex on site.

Carretera de Valbuena km 5, 47315 Peñafiel, Valladolid, Spain →

Finca Museum ★ 4.4

DO Ribera del DueroTinto Fino, Cabernet SauvignonPeñafiel

An estate near Peñafiel producing the Museum Real Crianza, Reserva and Gran Reserva alongside the Museum Collection range, with an underground cellar museum that gives the winery its name.

Carretera de Valbuena km 4, 47316 Peñafiel, Valladolid, Spain →

Bodegas Ribera del Pelayo ★ 4.3

DO Ribera del DueroTinto FinoRoa de Duero

A small Burgos-province estate in Hoyales de Roa producing single-vineyard Tinto Fino wines from old vines on the Roa de Duero plateau, known to local specialists for its terroir-driven, restrained style.

Calle Mayor s/n, 09312 Hoyales de Roa, Burgos, Spain →

Still wine in Rioja

R. Lopez de Heredia Vina Tondonia ★ 4.9

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Graciano, Mazuelo, Viura, Malvasia RiojanaFounded 1877Lopez de Heredia familyBarrio de la Estacion

The most traditional house in Rioja, founded in 1877 in Haro and still run by the founding family, famous for decade-long barrel ageing of Vina Tondonia reds, whites and rosados.

Tasting: Tours of the historic cellars and the Zaha Hadid-designed pavilion shop with tastings of Vina Tondonia and Vina Bosconia; by reservation.

Avenida de Vizcaya 3, Barrio de la Estacion, 26200 Haro, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Marques de Murrieta ★ 4.8

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Mazuelo, Graciano, Garnacha, ViuraFounded 1852Vicente Cebrian-Sagarriga familyRioja Alta

A founding house of modern Rioja established in 1852, based at the Ygay estate near Logrono and renowned for its long-aged Castillo Ygay Gran Reserva and the Capellania white.

Tasting: Tours and tastings of the Ygay estate and castle by reservation, including the flagship Castillo Ygay Gran Reserva.

Finca Ygay, Carretera Logrono-Zaragoza km 5, 26006 Logrono, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

La Rioja Alta S.A. ★ 4.8

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, Mazuelo, ViuraFounded 1890La Rioja Alta S.A. (founding families)Barrio de la Estacion

A classic Haro house founded in 1890 in the Barrio de la Estacion, celebrated for traditional long oak ageing in its Vina Ardanza Reserva and Gran Reserva 904 and 890.

Tasting: Guided tours of the historic Barrio de la Estacion cellars and tastings of the Vina Ardanza and Gran Reserva 904 and 890; by reservation.

Avenida de Vizcaya 8, Barrio de la Estacion, 26200 Haro, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

CVNE ★ 4.8

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Graciano, Mazuelo, ViuraFounded 1879Real de Asua familyBarrio de la Estacion

Compania Vinicola del Norte de Espana, founded in 1879 in Haro and still family-owned, makes the Cune, Imperial Gran Reserva and Monopole white ranges from the Barrio de la Estacion.

Tasting: Tours of the historic Haro cellars, including a building attributed to the Eiffel workshop, with tastings of Cune, Imperial and Monopole; by reservation.

Barrio de la Estacion, 26200 Haro, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Muga ★ 4.7

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Graciano, Mazuelo, Viura, Malvasia RiojanaFounded 1932Muga familyBarrio de la Estacion

A family house founded in 1932 in the Barrio de la Estacion at Haro, one of the last bodegas with its own cooperage, making Prado Enea Gran Reserva and the modern Torre Muga and Aro.

Tasting: Guided cellar tours showing the in-house cooperage, with tastings of Prado Enea, Torre Muga and Aro; also a wine bar and balloon rides.

Barrio de la Estacion, 26200 Haro, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Roda ★ 4.7

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, GracianoFounded 1987Mariano and Carmen Daniela Bujanda Rotaeche familyBarrio de la Estacion

A modern Haro estate founded in 1987 in the Barrio de la Estacion, focused on old-vine Tempranillo selections in its Roda, Roda I and the prestige Cirsion.

Tasting: Tours of the gravity-flow cellar and barrel halls with tastings of Roda, Roda I and Cirsion, plus a wine bar; by reservation.

Avenida de Vizcaya 5, Barrio de la Estacion, 26200 Haro, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Artadi ★ 4.7

Vino de Espana (formerly DOCa Rioja, left 2015)Tempranillo, ViuraFounded 1985Juan Carlos Lopez de Lacalle familyECOCERTRioja Alavesa

A pioneering single-vineyard producer at Laguardia founded in 1985, famous for Vina El Pison. Artadi withdrew from the Rioja DOCa in 2015 to label by site rather than ageing tier.

Tasting: Tours and tastings of single-vineyard wines such as Vina El Pison; by reservation. The estate left the Rioja DOCa in 2015 over terroir labelling.

Carretera de Logrono, 01300 Laguardia, Alava, Spain → Book →

Bodega Contador ★ 4.7

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, GarnachaFounded 1996Benjamin RomeoSan Vicente de la Sonsierra

The cult San Vicente de la Sonsierra project of winemaker Benjamin Romeo, founded in 1996, whose garage-born Contador earned 100-point recognition and reset Rioja's modern ceiling.

Tasting: Tours of the cave cellar beneath San Vicente with tastings of Contador, La Cueva del Contador and La Vina de Andres Romeo; by reservation.

Carretera Logrono, 26338 San Vicente de la Sonsierra, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Remirez de Ganuza ★ 4.6

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, Viura, Malvasia RiojanaFounded 1989Fernando Remirez de Ganuza familyRioja Alavesa

A meticulous Samaniego estate founded in 1989, known for selecting only the shoulders of each bunch, producing structured Reserva, Gran Reserva and the cult Trasnocho.

Tasting: Tours showing the estate's grape-selection and shoulder-bunch method, with tastings of the Reserva and Gran Reserva; by reservation.

Constitucion 1, 01307 Samaniego, Alava, Spain → Book →

Marques de Riscal ★ 4.8

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, Mazuelo, ViuraFounded 1858Herederos del Marques de Riscal (Hurtado de Amezaga family)Rioja Alavesa

One of the oldest bodegas in Rioja, founded in 1858 at Elciego, famous for its titanium City of Wine hotel designed by Frank Gehry and its long-aged Reserva and Gran Reserva reds.

Tasting: Guided winery tours and tastings at the City of Wine complex, plus the Frank Gehry-designed hotel, restaurants and spa; book through the estate.

Calle Torrea 1, 01340 Elciego, Alava, Spain → Book →

Gomez Cruzado ★ 4.5

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Viura, Tempranillo BlancoFounded 1886Gonzalez Pacheco and Garcia familiesBarrio de la Estacion

A small Barrio de la Estacion house at Haro dating to 1886, making classic Crianza, Reserva and Gran Reserva alongside a terroir range of single-village bottlings.

Tasting: Boutique-winery tours and tastings in the Barrio de la Estacion, including the terroir-selection wines Pancrudo and Cerro las Cuevas; by reservation.

Avenida de Vizcaya 6, Barrio de la Estacion, 26200 Haro, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Bilbainas ★ 4.5

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Graciano, Mazuelo, ViuraFounded 1901Codorniu Raventos groupBarrio de la Estacion

A historic Haro bodega founded in 1901, now part of the Codorniu Raventos group, known for its Vina Pomal label and the La Vicalanda Reserva and Gran Reserva.

Tasting: Tours of the historic cellars with tastings of the Vina Pomal range and the La Vicalanda Gran Reserva; by reservation.

Calle La Estacion 3, 26200 Haro, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Ysios ★ 4.6

DOCa RiojaTempranilloFounded 2001Vinarchy (Australian Wine Holdco / Accolade Wines, since 2025; formerly Pernod Ricard)Rioja Alavesa

A Rioja Alavesa landmark near Laguardia housed in a wave-roofed cellar designed by Santiago Calatrava in 2001, focused on Tempranillo Reserva from estate vineyards.

Tasting: Tours of the Santiago Calatrava-designed cellar with its undulating roof, with tastings of Tempranillo Reserva; by reservation.

Camino de la Hoya, 01300 Laguardia, Alava, Spain → Book →

Vinedos del Contino ★ 4.6

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, Garnacha, MazueloFounded 1973CVNE (Real de Asua family)Rioja Alavesa

Rioja's pioneering chateau-concept estate, founded in 1973 at Laserna on a bend of the Ebro and part of CVNE, making single-estate reds including a varietal Graciano.

Tasting: Tours of the single-estate property at Laserna with its 16th-century cellars, with tastings of the estate and single-vineyard reds; by reservation.

Finca San Gregorio, Laserna, 01321 Laguardia, Alava, Spain → Book →

Finca Allende ★ 4.6

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, Viura, Malvasia RiojanaFounded 1995Miguel Angel de Gregorio familyBriones

A quality-driven estate at Briones founded in 1995 by Miguel Angel de Gregorio, making the Allende red and white plus the prestige Aurus and the old-vine Calvario.

Tasting: Tours of the cellar in the historic Palacio de Ibarra at Briones, with tastings of Allende, Aurus and Calvario; by reservation.

Plaza Ibarra 1, 26330 Briones, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Sierra Cantabria ★ 4.6

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Graciano, ViuraFounded 1957Eguren familySan Vicente de la Sonsierra

The flagship of the Eguren family at San Vicente de la Sonsierra, founded in 1957, making the Sierra Cantabria range plus the prestige Amancio and El Bosque.

Tasting: Tours and tastings at San Vicente de la Sonsierra of the Sierra Cantabria, Amancio and Coleccion Privada reds; by reservation.

Amorebieta 3, 26338 San Vicente de la Sonsierra, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Luis Canas ★ 4.5

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, Garnacha, ViuraFounded 1970Luis Canas family (Juan Luis Canas)Rioja Alavesa

A family bodega at Villabuena de Alava in Rioja Alavesa, founded in 1970, drawing on old-vine parcels for its Reserva de Familia and top cuvee Hiru Racimos.

Tasting: Guided tours and tastings at Villabuena de Alava, including the Hiru Racimos and the old-vine Reserva de Familia; by reservation.

Carretera Samaniego 10, 01307 Villabuena de Alava, Alava, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Amaren ★ 4.5

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, ViuraFounded 1996Luis Canas familyRioja Alavesa

The premium project of the Luis Canas family in Villabuena de Alava, created in 1996, specialising in old-vine Tempranillo and Graciano and a barrel-fermented Viura white.

Tasting: Tours of the gravity-fed cellar at Villabuena with tastings of the old-vine Amaren reds and the barrel-fermented white; by reservation.

Carretera Banos de Ebro, 01307 Villabuena de Alava, Alava, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Ostatu ★ 4.4

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, Viura, Malvasia RiojanaFounded 1970Saenz de Samaniego familyRioja Alavesa

A grower-family bodega at Samaniego in Rioja Alavesa, producing fresh estate reds and whites and the old-vine Gloria de Ostatu from a single plot.

Tasting: Family-run tours and tastings at Samaniego, including the Gloria de Ostatu old-vine selection; by reservation.

Carretera Vitoria 1, 01307 Samaniego, Alava, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Izadi ★ 4.4

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, ViuraFounded 1987Artevino group (Lalanne family)Rioja Alavesa

A Villabuena de Alava bodega founded in 1987 and flagship of the Artevino group, making modern oak-aged reds and a barrel-fermented white with an on-site restaurant.

Tasting: Tours, tastings and a restaurant at Villabuena de Alava, including the El Regalo and single-vineyard reds; by reservation.

Herreria Travesia 2, 01307 Villabuena de Alava, Alava, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Vivanco ★ 4.5

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, Garnacha, Mazuelo, Maturana TintaFounded 1915Vivanco familyBriones

A family winery and foundation at Briones tracing to 1915, pairing its Rioja range with one of the world's largest wine museums, the Vivanco Museum of Wine Culture.

Tasting: Winery tours, tastings and the large Vivanco Museum of Wine Culture and gardens of Bacchus at Briones; book through the estate.

Carretera Nacional 232, 26330 Briones, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Dinastia Vivanco Vineyards ★ 4.3

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, Garnacha, Maturana Tinta, MazueloFounded 1915Vivanco familyBriones

The Vivanco family's vineyard and brand arm at Briones, planting a living collection of Rioja and international varieties alongside its core Tempranillo and Graciano reds.

Tasting: Vineyard walks and varietal-focused tastings at the Vivanco estate in Briones, showcasing the family's collection of recovered Rioja grape varieties; by reservation.

Carretera Nacional 232, 26330 Briones, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Faustino ★ 4.3

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, Mazuelo, ViuraFounded 1861Faustino family (Grupo Faustino)Rioja Alavesa

A large historic house at Oyon tracing to 1861, best known internationally for the etched-bottle Faustino I Gran Reserva and a broad range of Reserva reds.

Tasting: Tours and tastings at Oyon, including the frosted-bottle Faustino I Gran Reserva; by reservation.

Calle Faustino 1, 01320 Oyon, Alava, Spain → Book →

Campo Viejo ★ 4.2

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Graciano, MazueloFounded 1959Vinarchy (Australian Wine Holdco / Accolade Wines, since 2025; formerly Pernod Ricard)Rioja Alta

One of the best-selling Rioja brands, founded in 1959 and owned since 2025 by Vinarchy (the Australian Wine Holdco / Accolade Wines group, having previously belonged to Pernod Ricard), with a large visitor-friendly winery near Logrono producing Crianza, Reserva and Gran Reserva.

Tasting: Tours of the large sustainable winery near Logrono with tastings across the Campo Viejo range; book through the estate.

Camino de Lapuebla 50, 26006 Logrono, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas LAN ★ 4.3

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Mazuelo, GracianoFounded 1972Sogrape VinhosRioja Alta

A Fuenmayor bodega founded in 1972, now owned by Sogrape, sourcing its top wines from the river-bounded Vina Lanciano estate, including the modern Culmen Reserva.

Tasting: Tours of the Fuenmayor winery and the Vina Lanciano estate on a meander of the Ebro, with tastings of the Culmen and Vina Lanciano; by reservation.

Paraje del Buicio, Carretera Fuenmayor, 26360 Fuenmayor, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Baron de Ley ★ 4.3

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, GracianoFounded 1985Baron de Ley S.A.Rioja Oriental

An estate built around a 16th-century former Benedictine monastery on the eastern edge of Rioja, founded in 1985 and known for its Finca Monasterio and Reserva reds.

Tasting: Tours of the 16th-century former monastery estate with tastings of the Finca Monasterio and Gran Reserva; by reservation.

Carretera Mendavia-Lodosa km 5.5, 31587 Mendavia, Navarra, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Ramon Bilbao ★ 4.4

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, Garnacha, ViuraFounded 1924Zamora CompanyHaro

A Haro house founded in 1924, now part of the Zamora Company, producing a wide Rioja range led by the Mirto and Lalomba single-vineyard wines.

Tasting: Guided tours of the Haro winery with barrel and bottle halls, tastings and a wine bar; by reservation.

Avenida Santo Domingo de la Calzada 34, 26200 Haro, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Valenciso ★ 4.5

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, ViuraFounded 1998Luis Valentin and Carmen EncisoRioja Alta

A boutique Rioja Alta producer at Ollauri founded in 1998 by Luis Valentin and Carmen Enciso, making essentially one classically styled Reserva plus a barrel-fermented Viura.

Tasting: Tours and tastings at Ollauri of the single-label Valenciso Reserva and the barrel-fermented white; by reservation.

Carretera Ollauri-Najera km 0.4, 26220 Ollauri, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Baigorri ★ 4.5

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Graciano, MazueloFounded 2002Jesus Baigorri familyRioja Alavesa

An architectural Rioja Alavesa winery at Samaniego opened in 2002, built as a glass cube over seven underground levels using gravity-flow winemaking, with a panoramic restaurant.

Tasting: Tours of the seven-level gravity-flow glass cube cellar at Samaniego with tastings and a restaurant; by reservation.

Carretera Vitoria-Logrono km 53, 01307 Samaniego, Alava, Spain → Book →

Vina Real ★ 4.5

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Graciano, MazueloFounded 2004CVNE (Real de Asua family)Rioja Alavesa

CVNE's Rioja Alavesa cellar near Laguardia, a striking circular building opened in 2004, home to the Vina Real range and the single-estate Pagos de Vina Real.

Tasting: Tours of the circular barrel-shaped Philippe Mazieres cellar near Laguardia with tastings of Vina Real and Pagos de Vina Real; by reservation.

Carretera Logrono-Laguardia km 4.8, 01300 Laguardia, Alava, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Franco-Espanolas ★ 4.3

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Viura, MazueloFounded 1890Eguizabal familyLogrono

The only historic bodega in the urban centre of Logrono, founded in 1890 as a French-Spanish venture after phylloxera, known for the Bordon reds and the off-dry Diamante white.

Tasting: Tours of the historic city-centre winery in Logrono with tastings of the Bordon and Diamante ranges; book through the wine-tourism site.

Calle Cabo Noval 2, 26009 Logrono, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Montecillo ★ 4.2

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, GarnachaFounded 1874Osborne groupRioja Alta

One of the oldest Rioja houses, founded in 1874 and now owned by the Osborne group, based at Fuenmayor and known for traditional Reserva and Gran Reserva reds.

Tasting: Tours of the Fuenmayor winery with tastings of the Montecillo Reserva and Gran Reserva; by reservation.

Carretera Navarrete-Fuenmayor km 2, 26360 Fuenmayor, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Riojanas ★ 4.2

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, Mazuelo, ViuraFounded 1890Artacho familyCenicero

A long-established Cenicero house founded in 1890, recognisable by its castle-like facade, making the traditional Vina Albina and Monte Real Reserva and Gran Reserva.

Tasting: Tours of the castellated Cenicero winery with tastings of the Vina Albina and Monte Real ranges; by reservation.

Calle Estacion 1, 26350 Cenicero, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Muriel ★ 4.2

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Graciano, ViuraFounded 1986Muriel Vinedos y Bodegas (Murua family)Rioja Alavesa

A family group based at Elciego in Rioja Alavesa, founded in 1986, producing the Muriel range and the more traditional Conde de los Andes Reserva and Gran Reserva.

Tasting: Tours and tastings at Elciego of the Muriel and Conde de los Andes ranges; by reservation.

Barrio El Campillar 13, 01300 Elciego, Alava, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Ontanon ★ 4.3

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Graciano, Maturana Tinta, Tempranillo BlancoFounded 1985Perez Marin familyLogrono

A family winery linking a Logrono cellar with old-vine vineyards at Quel in Rioja Oriental, known for art-themed tours and a focus on Garnacha and heritage grapes such as Maturana.

Tasting: Tours combining wine and art at the Logrono winery, with vineyards in Quel in Rioja Oriental; tastings of the Ontanon and Vetiver wines by reservation.

Avenida de Aragon 3, 26006 Logrono, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Valdemar ★ 4.3

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Maturana Blanca, Tempranillo BlancoFounded 1889Martinez Bujanda familyRioja Alavesa

A Martinez Bujanda family bodega at Oyon dating to 1889, noted for reviving rare Rioja varieties such as Maturana Blanca and for its Inspiracion Valdemar range.

Tasting: Tours and tastings at Oyon, including pioneering work with recovered varieties such as Maturana Blanca; by reservation.

Camino Viejo de Logrono 24, 01320 Oyon, Alava, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Lecea ★ 4.2

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, ViuraFounded 1962Lecea familyRioja Alta

A grower bodega in Rioja Alta at San Asensio keeping the tradition of underground rock-cut calado cellars and foot-treading, offering hands-on harvest experiences alongside its reds.

Tasting: Visits to traditional rock-cut calado cellar-caves at San Asensio with tastings and the annual grape foot-treading experience; by reservation.

Calle Las Cuevas 246, Barrio de las Bodegas, 26340 San Asensio, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Altanza ★ 4.2

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, GracianoFounded 1998Bodegas Altanza S.A.Rioja Alta

A Fuenmayor bodega founded in 1998 working mainly with estate Tempranillo, best known for the artist-label Lealtanza range of Reserva and Gran Reserva.

Tasting: Tours of the Fuenmayor winery with tastings of the Altanza and art-label Lealtanza Reserva and Gran Reserva; by reservation.

Carretera Nacional 232 km 419, 26360 Fuenmayor, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Olarra ★ 4.2

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, Mazuelo, GracianoFounded 1973Grupo OlarraRioja Alta

A Logrono winery founded in 1973, famous for its Y-shaped modernist building called the cathedral of Rioja, producing the Cerro Anon and Anares Reserva and Gran Reserva.

Tasting: Tours of the distinctive Y-shaped winery near Logrono, nicknamed the cathedral of Rioja, with tastings of the Anares and Cerro Anon ranges; by reservation.

Poligono de Cantabria, 26006 Logrono, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Bodegas Tritium ★ 4.1

DOCa RiojaTempranillo, Garnacha, GracianoFounded 2004Tritium familyRioja Alta

A small Rioja Alta bodega at Badaran in the Najerilla valley, producing estate Tempranillo-led reds and offering visits and tastings in a quieter corner of the region.

Tasting: Tours and tastings at Badaran in the Najerilla valley of Rioja Alta, including barrel-aged Tempranillo; by reservation.

Carretera de Cordovin, 26310 Badaran, La Rioja, Spain → Book →

Still wine in Sicily

Benanti Viticoltori ★ 4.8

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, Carricante, CatarrattoFounded 1734Etna DOC

One of Etna's pioneering estates, Benanti has been producing wine on the volcanic slopes since 1734. Their single-contrada whites from Milo are benchmarks for Etna Bianco Superiore, and their Nerello reds helped launch the modern Etna renaissance.

Tasting: Tuesday through Sunday by reservation. Three tasting programmes including Get to Know Etna, Contrade Selection and Library Vintages. Tours of the historic palmento and vineyard.

Signature wines: benanti-pietramarina, benanti-rovittello, benanti-serra-della-contessa

Via Giuseppe Garibaldi 361, 95029 Viagrande (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Tenuta delle Terre Nere ★ 4.8

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, Carricante, CatarrattoFounded 2002Etna DOC

Founded in 2002 by Marc de Grazia on Etna's north slope at Randazzo, Tenuta delle Terre Nere was instrumental in establishing the single-contrada bottlings that made Etna internationally famous. Old-vine Nerello from plots like Calderara Sottana and Guardiola set the benchmark.

Tasting: By appointment, Monday to Friday. Contact via visite@tenutaterrenere.com.

Signature wines: terre-nere-calderara-sottana, terre-nere-guardiola, terre-nere-feudo-di-mezzo

Contrada Calderara sn, 95036 Randazzo (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Passopisciaro (Vini Franchetti) ★ 4.8

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, ChardonnayFounded 2000Etna DOC

Founded in 2000 by the late Andrea Franchetti at approximately 1,000 metres on Etna's north slope, Passopisciaro produces site-expressive single-contrada Nerello reds including the rare Franchetti red from the high-altitude Solicchiata plots.

Tasting: Visits by appointment at the Passopisciaro estate. Contact via the Vini Franchetti website.

Signature wines: passopisciaro-franchetti, passopisciaro-passorosso, passopisciaro-passobianco

Via Canonico Zumbo 1, Passopisciaro, 95012 Castiglione di Sicilia (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Graci ★ 4.7

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, Carricante, CatarrattoFounded 2004Etna DOC

Alberto Aiello Graci has been farming old-vine Nerello Mascalese and Carricante on Etna's north slope since 2004, producing single-contrada wines including the benchmark Arcuria Etna Rosso from 52 acres of ungrafted vines.

Tasting: Visits by appointment at the Passopisciaro estate. Contact via info@graci.eu.

Signature wines: graci-arcuria, graci-barbabecchi, graci-etna-bianco

Contrada Feudo di Mezzo, SP7iii, Passopisciaro, 95012 Castiglione di Sicilia (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Pietradolce ★ 4.7

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, Carricante, CatarrattoFounded 2005Etna DOC

Founded in 2005 by Michele Faro at Solicchiata on Etna's north slope, Pietradolce works with old-vine Nerello Mascalese and Carricante across several contrade, producing one of the island's most age-worthy single-vineyard Biancos in the Archineri.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at the Solicchiata winery on Etna's north slope. Contact via the Pietradolce website.

Signature wines: pietradolce-archineri, pietradolce-etna-rosso, pietradolce-barbagalli

SS120 174, Contrada Rampante, Solicchiata, 95012 Castiglione di Sicilia (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Girolamo Russo ★ 4.8

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Carricante, Catarratto, Inzolia, GrecanicoFounded 2005ORGANIC CERTIFIEDEtna DOC

Giuseppe Russo took over the family's old Nerello Mascalese vineyards in 2005 after his father Girolamo died. Fifteen hectares of organically farmed vines between 650 and 780 metres on Etna's north slope produce sought-after single-vineyard reds including San Lorenzo and a Rina.

Tasting: Vineyard visits and tastings with local cold cuts, cheeses and homemade bread. Contact info@girolamorusso.it or call 0942 983142.

Signature wines: russo-san-lorenzo, russo-a-rina, russo-feudo

Via Regina Margherita 78, 95012 Passopisciaro (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Azienda Vinicola Calabretta ★ 4.7

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, CatarrattoFounded 1900Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalEtna DOC

Founded in 1900, Calabretta is one of Etna's oldest family wineries. The fourth generation farms 12.5 hectares of ungrafted old vines with copper and sulphur only, producing low-intervention wines from Nerello Mascalese that rank among the most authentic expressions of the north slope.

Tasting: Visits by appointment at the Randazzo estate. Contact via calabretta.net.

Signature wines: calabretta-gaio-gaio, calabretta-etna-rosso, calabretta-vigne-vecchie

Contrada Calderara, 95036 Randazzo (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Cottanera ★ 4.5

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, Carricante, Merlot, Syrah, MondeuseFounded 1994Etna DOC

Cottanera is a family estate on Etna's north slope producing both native Nerello-based Etna DOC wines and international varieties grown at high altitude. The boutique agriturismo Dimora Cottanera makes it a wine-tourism anchor on the Alcantara Valley rim.

Tasting: Monday to Saturday 9:00 to 17:00, booking essential. Guided vineyard, cellar and tasting experiences.

Signature wines: cottanera-barbazzale-bianco, cottanera-fatagione, cottanera-calderara

Strada Provinciale 89, Contrada Iannazzo, 95030 Castiglione di Sicilia (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Murgo ★ 4.5

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, Carricante, CatarrattoFounded 1860Etna DOC

A historic estate on Etna's eastern slopes, Murgo introduced Sicily's most acclaimed traditional-method sparkling wine in 1990 using Nerello Mascalese. The 30-hectare property has been in the Scammacca del Murgo family since 1860.

Tasting: Wine tastings available Monday to Saturday 11:30 to 14:00. Restaurant and accommodation on site.

Signature wines: murgo-brut-metodo-classico, murgo-etna-rosso, murgo-etna-bianco

Via Zafferana 13, 95010 Santa Venerina (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Frank Cornelissen ★ 4.9

Etna DOC, IGP Terre SicilianeNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, Catarratto, Minella, CarricanteFounded 2001Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalEtna DOC

Belgian natural-wine pioneer Frank Cornelissen established his estate on Etna's north slope in 2001, working without sulphur at all. His Magma cuvee from ungrafted pre-phylloxera Nerello vines above 1,000 metres is among Sicily's most sought-after cult wines.

Tasting: Five tasting experiences from the Classic (5 wines, EU40) to the Magma private (6 crus including Grand Vin Magma, EU1,400 for up to 6 people). E-bike vineyard tours available. Book via pr@frankcornelissen.it.

Signature wines: cornelissen-magma, cornelissen-munjebel-rosso, cornelissen-munjebel-bianco

Via Canonico Zumbo 1, Passopisciaro, 95012 Castiglione di Sicilia (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Tenuta di Fessina ★ 4.6

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, CarricanteORGANIC CERTIFIEDEtna DOC

Silvia Maestrelli's Tenuta di Fessina in Rovittello at 670 metres on Etna's north slope offers one of the island's most accessible high-quality tasting experiences. The organic estate produces refined Nerello Mascalese and Carricante across its Etna DOC range.

Tasting: Open every day including holidays. Tasting reception at 10:30, 13:30 and 15:30. Contact fessina@tenutadifessina.com or call +39 345 7230176.

Signature wines: fessina-erse, fessina-musmeci, fessina-a-puddara

Contrada Rovittello, Via Nazionale SS120 22, 95012 Castiglione di Sicilia (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Palmento Costanzo ★ 4.6

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, Carricante, CatarrattoFounded 2011ORGANIC CERTIFIEDEtna DOC

Founded in 2011 by Mimmo and Valeria Costanzo, Palmento Costanzo farms approximately ten hectares organically on Etna's north slope in Contrada Santo Spirito. They produce contrada-specific wines with manual cultivation and a fully organic approach.

Tasting: Visite e Degustazioni bookable via the estate website. Contact info@palmentocostanzo.com or call +39 0942 983239.

Signature wines: palmento-costanzo-mofete-bianco, palmento-costanzo-mofete-rosso, palmento-costanzo-di-me

Contrada Santo Spirito, Passopisciaro, 95012 Castiglione di Sicilia (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Tornatore ★ 4.4

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, Carricante, CatarrattoFounded 1865Etna DOC

The Tornatore family has farmed on Etna since 1865, with a large single-vineyard block in the Castiglione di Sicilia hills. Their estate is often cited as Etna DOC's largest single property, producing a full range of DOC wines from Nerello and Carricante.

Tasting: Guided tours and tastings at the estate, described as the largest single vineyard in Etna DOC. Contact +39 337 1087964.

Signature wines: tornatore-etna-bianco, tornatore-etna-rosso, tornatore-trimarchisa

Via Pietramarina 8, 95012 Castiglione di Sicilia (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Tenute Nicosia ★ 4.4

Etna DOC, Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG, Sicilia DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, Carricante, Nero d'Avola, Frappato, CatarrattoORGANIC CERTIFIEDEtna DOC

Tenute Nicosia operates vineyards on Etna and in Vittoria, producing organic wines across a broad range including Etna DOC and Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG. Their Trecastagni winery on the south-east slope of Etna is a key wine-tourism destination.

Tasting: Guided tours and tastings by reservation at the Trecastagni winery. Multiple tasting packages including Contrade dell'Etna. Contact info@cantinenicosia.it.

Signature wines: nicosia-etna-rosso-fondo-filara, nicosia-etna-bianco, nicosia-monte-gorna

Via Luigi Capuana 65, 95039 Trecastagni (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Tenuta Tascante (Tasca d'Almerita on Etna) ★ 4.6

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, CarricanteFounded 2010Etna DOC

Tasca d'Almerita's Etna estate in Castiglione di Sicilia, established around 2010, produces contrada-expressive Nerello Mascalese reds and Carricante whites within the Etna DOC. Ghiaia Nera is the benchmark cuvee of the estate.

Tasting: Visits bookable via the Tasca d'Almerita website. Estate is located in Contrada Rampante on Etna's north slope.

Signature wines: tascante-ghiaia-nera, tascante-il-picciolo

Contrada Rampante, 95012 Castiglione di Sicilia (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Planeta Sciaranuova (Etna) ★ 4.5

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, CarricanteFounded 2008Etna DOC

Planeta's Etna estate in Solicchiata, established in 2008, produces Nerello Mascalese and Carricante within the Etna DOC. The Sciaranuova winery is one of the island's most architecturally striking cellar doors.

Tasting: Tasting experience at the Sciaranuova winery. Bookable via the Planeta estate website.

Signature wines: planeta-nerello-etna, planeta-carricante-etna

Contrada Sciaranuova, Solicchiata, 95012 Castiglione di Sicilia (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Alta Mora by Cusumano (Etna) ★ 4.4

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, CarricanteFounded 2013Etna DOC

Cusumano's Etna label Alta Mora is produced from the Verzella contrada on the north slope of Etna. The range focuses on Nerello Mascalese for reds and Carricante for whites, with contrada bottlings among the island's most accessible Etna expressions.

Tasting: Etna estate tastings bookable via the Cusumano website.

Signature wines: alta-mora-etna-rosso, alta-mora-etna-bianco

Contrada Verzella, 95012 Castiglione di Sicilia (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Firriato Cavanera Etnea ★ 4.4

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio, CarricanteEtna DOC

Firriato's Etna estate in the Verzella contrada includes the Cavanera resort, one of the most complete wine-tourism experiences on Etna, with accommodation, restaurant, cellar visits and the Gaudensius traditional-method sparkling wine from Nerello Mascalese.

Tasting: Wine resort and restaurant on Etna's north slope. Visits and stays bookable via Firriato.

Signature wines: firriato-gaudensius-etna-brut, firriato-cavanera-ripa-di-moraci

Contrada Verzella, Castiglione di Sicilia (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Benanti Pietrarizzo Contrada ★ 4.6

Etna DOCNerello Mascalese, Nerello CappuccioFounded 1734Etna DOC

The Pietrarizzo contrada on the north slope of Etna at 800 metres produces Benanti's most mineral and savoury Nerello Mascalese. The Benanti family has farmed this plot since the 18th century, making it one of the oldest continuously cultivated contrade on the volcano.

Tasting: Visits available as part of the Benanti estate experience. Book via benanti.it.

Signature wines: benanti-pietrarizzo

Via Giuseppe Garibaldi 361, 95029 Viagrande (CT), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Cantine Milazzo ★ 4.1

Mamertino DOC, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Nocera, Nerello Mascalese, Grillo, CatarrattoMamertino DOC

Cantine Milazzo is one of the few producers working seriously within the rare Mamertino DOC, an appellation near Milazzo in the Messina province with roots in ancient Roman viniculture. Their Nocera-blended reds reflect this underexplored north-east terroir.

Tasting: Winery visits and tastings at the Milazzo estate near the Tyrrhenian coast. Contact via the winery.

Signature wines: milazzo-mamertino-rosso, milazzo-nero-davola

Contrada Feudo Soprano, 98057 Milazzo (ME), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Marco De Bartoli ★ 4.8

Marsala DOC, Pantelleria DOCGrillo, ZibibboFounded 1978NaturalMarsala DOC

Marco De Bartoli founded this artisan Marsala house in 1978, working primarily with Grillo to revive traditional un-fortified and minimally fortified styles. The estate at Samperi is the spiritual home of authentic Marsala craft.

Tasting: Guided tours and tastings at the Samperi cellar near Marsala. Weekday visits by appointment. Contact by phone (+39 0923 962093) or email.

Signature wines: de-bartoli-vecchio-samperi, de-bartoli-marsala-superiore-oro, de-bartoli-bukkuram-sole-d-agosto

Contrada Samperi 292, 91025 Marsala (TP), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Donnafugata Pantelleria ★ 4.8

Passito di Pantelleria DOC, Pantelleria DOCZibibboFounded 1990Pantelleria DOC

Donnafugata's Pantelleria estate produces the celebrated Ben Rye Passito di Pantelleria DOC from Zibibbo (Moscato d'Alessandria) vines trained in the alberello pantesco system on volcanic soils. Ben Rye is consistently rated among Italy's finest sweet wines.

Tasting: Estate visits on Pantelleria. Bookable via the Donnafugata visit site.

Signature wines: donnafugata-ben-rye

Via S. Michele, 91017 Pantelleria (TP), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Marco De Bartoli - Bukkuram (Pantelleria) ★ 4.8

Passito di Pantelleria DOC, Moscato di Pantelleria DOCZibibboFounded 1978NaturalPantelleria DOC

Marco De Bartoli's Pantelleria estate at Bukkuram produces the Sole d'Agosto passito from Zibibbo vines trained in the alberello pantesco system. The wine is a benchmark of minimal-intervention Pantelleria passito style.

Tasting: Visits by appointment on Pantelleria. Contact via the Marco De Bartoli website.

Signature wines: de-bartoli-bukkuram-sole-d-agosto

Via S. Michele 41, 91017 Pantelleria (TP), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Serragghia (Pantelleria) ★ 4.7

Passito di Pantelleria DOCZibibboBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalPantelleria DOC

Gabrio Bini's Serragghia on Pantelleria is a cult natural-wine producer farming Zibibbo in the alberello pantesco system without sulphur. The Serragghia passito and natural Zibibbo whites are among Italy's most sought-after natural sweet and aromatic wines.

Tasting: Visits by appointment on Pantelleria. Contact via Gabrio Bini's Serragghia farm.

Signature wines: serragghia-passito-di-pantelleria

Contrada Khamma 132, 91017 Pantelleria (TP), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Hauner (Malvasia delle Lipari) ★ 4.6

Malvasia delle Lipari DOCMalvasia di Lipari, Corinto NeroFounded 1963Malvasia delle Lipari DOC

Founded by Carlo Hauner in 1963 on Salina in the Aeolian Islands, Hauner is the defining producer of Malvasia delle Lipari DOC. Their passito and naturale styles from Malvasia di Lipari vines are the benchmark for this rare island appellation.

Tasting: Tasting room at the estate on Salina. Open to visitors in the Aeolian Island season (April to October). Contact via the Hauner website.

Signature wines: hauner-malvasia-delle-lipari, hauner-malvasia-delle-lipari-passito

Via Umberto I 83, 98050 Santa Marina di Salina (ME), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Caravaglio (Salina / Malvasia) ★ 4.5

Malvasia delle Lipari DOCMalvasia di Lipari, Nero d'Avola, Corinto NeroORGANIC CERTIFIEDMalvasia delle Lipari DOC

A second-generation family estate on Salina producing organic Malvasia delle Lipari DOC wines alongside a Nero d'Avola-based red. Caravaglio's minimal-intervention passito is a benchmark expression of the Aeolian Islands' volcanic terroir.

Tasting: Seasonal tastings on Salina. Contact via the Caravaglio website.

Signature wines: caravaglio-malvasia-passito, caravaglio-salina-bianco

Via Nazionale 12, 98050 Malfa, Salina (ME), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Cantina Rallo ★ 4.1

Marsala DOC, Alcamo DOC, Pantelleria DOCGrillo, Catarratto, Inzolia, Nero d'Avola, ZibibboFounded 1860Marsala DOC

Founded in 1860 by Diego Rallo, one of Marsala's oldest historic families. The Vesco family acquired the estate in 1996, integrating vineyards in Alcamo and Pantelleria with the historic Marsala cellars. Today the estate operates across western Sicily's key appellations.

Tasting: Historic Marsala cellar visits and tastings. Contact via the Rallo website.

Signature wines: rallo-marsala-superiore, rallo-alcamo-bianco

Via Vincenzo Florio 2, 91025 Marsala (TP), Sicily, Italy → Book →

COS ★ 4.9

Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG, Vittoria DOCNero d'Avola, Frappato, Grecanico, ZibibboFounded 1980Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalCerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG

Founded in 1980 by Giambattista Cilia, Cirino Strano and Giusto Occhipinti, COS pioneered biodynamic farming and amphora fermentation in Sicily. Their Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG from Nero d'Avola and Frappato is the defining expression of the appellation.

Tasting: Winery visits by reservation only. Contact via the COS website or telephone (+39 0932 876145).

Signature wines: cos-cerasuolo-di-vittoria, cos-pithos-rosso, cos-rami

SP3 Acate-Chiaramonte Km 14,300, 97019 Vittoria (RG), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Arianna Occhipinti ★ 4.9

Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG, Vittoria DOC, IGP Terre SicilianeFrappato, Nero d'AvolaFounded 2004Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalCerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG

Arianna Occhipinti started with one hectare along the SP68 road in 2004 and has grown to 40 hectares farmed organically and biodynamically. Her SP68 Rosso blending Frappato and Nero d'Avola is among Italy's most celebrated natural wines.

Tasting: Visits by reservation year-round, mornings or afternoons. Vineyard walk, cellar tour and wine and oil tasting with olives and bruschetta. Languages: Italian, English, French. Contact +39 0932 1865519.

Signature wines: occhipinti-sp68-rosso, occhipinti-sp68-bianco, occhipinti-siccagno

SP68 Vittoria-Pedalino Km 3,3, 97019 Vittoria (RG), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Valle dell'Acate ★ 4.6
