Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG, Vittoria DOC, Sicilia DOCFrappato, Nero d'Avola, Grillo, VermentinoORGANIC CERTIFIEDCerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG

Six generations of the Jacono family have farmed these 70 hectares in the Ragusa province. Gaetana Jacono runs the estate, producing organic Frappato and Nero d'Avola that anchor their acclaimed Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG blend.

Tasting: Classic wine tastings, chef-led pairings, cooking courses and vineyard walks at the Acate estate.

Signature wines: valle-acate-cerasuolo-di-vittoria, valle-acate-il-frappato, valle-acate-tane

Contrada Biddini Soprano, 97011 Acate (RG), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Gulfi ★ 4.7

Eloro DOC, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Nerello Cappuccio, Albanello, CarricanteORGANIC CERTIFIEDEloro DOC

Gulfi owns some of the oldest Nero d'Avola parcels in the Pachino area, including pre-phylloxera crus at Maccaraj, Bufaleffj, Baronj and Sanlorenzo. The estate's four single-vineyard bottlings set the benchmark for terroir-expressive Nero d'Avola.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at the Noto estate. Contact info@gulfi.it.

Signature wines: gulfi-nerojbleo, gulfi-nerobuffaleffj, gulfi-nerobaronj

Contrada Maccari, Strada Prov. Noto-Pachino Km 13.5, 96017 Noto (SR), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Feudo Montoni ★ 4.7

Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Catarratto, GrilloORGANIC CERTIFIEDSicilia DOC

Fabio Sireci's Feudo Montoni sits in the hills of central Sicily at Cammarata, farming pre-phylloxera Nero d'Avola vines. The Vrucara single-vineyard bottling from ungrafted vines is one of Sicily's most distinctive and sought-after Nero d'Avola expressions.

Tasting: Visits by appointment at the Cammarata estate in central Sicily. Contact info@feudomontoni.it.

Signature wines: feudo-montoni-vrucara, feudo-montoni-lagnusa, feudo-montoni-grillo-della-timpa

Contrada Montoni Vecchi, 92022 Cammarata (AG), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Morgante ★ 4.4

Sicilia DOCNero d'AvolaSicilia DOC

The Morgante family at Grotte in the Agrigento province focuses almost exclusively on Nero d'Avola, producing a standard and a single-vineyard Don Antonio from vineyards in the hot hilly interior of Sicily.

Tasting: Winery visits by appointment. Contact info@morgantevini.it or call +39 0922 945579.

Signature wines: morgante-nero-davola, morgante-don-antonio

Contrada Racalmare, 92020 Grotte (AG), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Zisola (Mazzei) ★ 4.5

Noto DOC, Eloro DOC, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Syrah, Frappato, GrilloNoto DOC

The Mazzei family of Tuscany established Zisola near Noto in 2003, planting 50 hectares with Nero d'Avola and Syrah. The estate overlooks the Baroque town of Noto and produces wines that blend Tuscan winemaking precision with Sicilian terroir.

Tasting: Wine tours and tastings at the Noto estate. Reserve via enoteca@zisola.it or call +39 0931 839288.

Signature wines: zisola-doppiozeta, zisola-zisola, zisola-mofete

Contrada Zisola, 96017 Noto (SR), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Planeta Dorilli (Vittoria) ★ 4.5

Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Frappato, SyrahFounded 1997Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG

Planeta's Vittoria estate in the Dorilli contrada produces Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG, the only DOCG in Sicily, blending Nero d'Avola and Frappato. The estate has been instrumental in raising the profile of the Vittoria appellation.

Tasting: Tastings at the Vittoria estate bookable via the Planeta estate site.

Signature wines: planeta-cerasuolo-di-vittoria

Contrada Dorilli, 97019 Vittoria (RG), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Maggio Vini ★ 4.2

Noto DOC, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Moscato BiancoNoto DOC

Maggio Vini produces Noto DOC Nero d'Avola and Moscato di Noto from the Maccari contrada in the Syracuse province, one of the warmest and most sun-drenched zones in Sicily.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at the Noto estate. Contact via the winery.

Signature wines: maggio-vini-nero-davola, maggio-vini-moscato-di-noto

Contrada Maccari, 96017 Noto (SR), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Donnafugata ★ 4.7

Contessa Entellina DOC, Marsala DOC, Pantelleria DOC, Etna DOC, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Syrah, Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, Grillo, Catarratto, Inzolia, Zibibbo, Grecanico, FrappatoFounded 1983Marsala DOC

Donnafugata, founded by the Rallo family in Marsala, has grown into a multi-estate Sicilian producer with vineyards in Contessa Entellina, Pantelleria and Etna, crafting wines from native varieties including the iconic Ben Rye passito.

Tasting: Daily guided tours and tastings at the historic Marsala cantina (10:00-18:00) and at the Contessa Entellina estate. Reservations required.

Signature wines: donnafugata-ben-rye, donnafugata-mille-e-una-notte, donnafugata-tancredi

Via Sebastiano Lipari 18, 91025 Marsala (TP), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Planeta ★ 4.7

Menfi DOC, Etna DOC, Noto DOC, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Syrah, Merlot, Cabernet Franc, Grecanico, Chardonnay, Grillo, Carricante, Nocera, Nerello MascaleseFounded 1994Menfi DOC

One of Sicily's most influential estates, Planeta operates six wineries across the island from Menfi to Etna, Noto and Milazzo, showcasing the diversity of Sicilian terroir through native and international varieties.

Tasting: Three estate cellar doors: Ulmo at Menfi, Buonivini at Noto, and Sciaranuova on Etna. Tasting experiences bookable on the Planeta estate website.

Signature wines: planeta-santa-cecilia, planeta-burdese, planeta-cometa

Contrada Dispensa, 92013 Menfi (AG), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Duca di Salaparuta (Corvo) ★ 4.3

Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Catarratto, Grillo, Inzolia, PerriconeFounded 1824Sicilia DOC

Founded in 1824 by the Duke of Salaparuta, Duca di Salaparuta (Corvo) is one of Sicily's oldest commercial wine estates. Their Duca Enrico is a landmark 100% Nero d'Avola and the Bianca di Valguarnera a prestigious Inzolia white. Now owned by Illva Saronno.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at the historic Casteldaccia winery. Contact via the Duca di Salaparuta website.

Signature wines: duca-di-salaparuta-bianca-di-valguarnera, duca-enrico

Via Nazionale SS 113, 90040 Casteldaccia (PA), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Baglio del Cristo di Campobello ★ 4.4

Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, Nerello Mascalese, Grillo, Inzolia, CatarrattoFounded 2000ORGANIC CERTIFIEDSicilia DOC

Founded in 2000 by the Bonetta family near Agrigento, Baglio del Cristo di Campobello farms 50 hectares on calcareous chalky soils 8 kilometres from the sea. Their Adennzia white and Lu Patri red have earned international recognition for quality Sicilian DOC wines.

Tasting: Winery visits and tastings at the Campobello di Licata estate. Contact via the estate website.

Signature wines: baglio-cristo-adenzia, baglio-cristo-lu-patri

Contrada Favarotta SS123 Km 19+200, 92023 Campobello di Licata (AG), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Feudi del Pisciotto ★ 4.5

Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Frappato, Grillo, SyrahCerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG

Feudi del Pisciotto is an 18th-century restored manor on the edge of the Sughereta di Niscemi Nature Reserve producing wines in the Val di Noto. It is also a Wine Relais hotel with restaurant, combining Sicilian wine culture with upscale rural hospitality.

Tasting: Wine Relais with boutique hotel, restaurant and wine experiences at the restored 18th-century manor in the Val di Noto.

Signature wines: feudi-pisciotto-cerasuolo-di-vittoria, feudi-pisciotto-versace-nero-davola

Contrada del Pisciotto, 93015 Niscemi (CL), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Tasca d'Almerita ★ 4.7

Contea di Sclafani DOC, Etna DOC, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Nerello Mascalese, Perricone, Inzolia, Catarratto, Grillo, GrecanicoFounded 1830Contea di Sclafani DOC

One of Sicily's oldest and most storied family estates, Tasca d'Almerita has farmed the Regaleali plateau since 1830. Their Rosso del Conte, a Nero d'Avola-led flagship, is among the island's most age-worthy reds.

Tasting: Estate visits and tastings at the historic Regaleali property in the heart of Sicily, bookable via the Tasca d'Almerita website.

Signature wines: tasca-dalmerita-rosso-del-conte, tasca-dalmerita-chardonnay, tasca-dalmerita-leone-dalmerita

Contrada Regaleali, 90020 Sclafani Bagni (PA), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Cusumano ★ 4.4

Sicilia DOC, Etna DOCNero d'Avola, Syrah, Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, Nerello Mascalese, Inzolia, Grillo, Chardonnay, FianoFounded 2001Sicilia DOC

Brothers Alberto and Diego Cusumano founded their estate in 2001 at Partinico with five estate vineyards and more than 500 hectares. The Sagana single-vineyard Nero d'Avola from Riesi and Alta Mora Etna range mark their quality poles.

Tasting: Wine tastings and direct sales at the Partinico winery. Contact info@cusumano.it or call +39 091 8908713.

Signature wines: cusumano-sagana, cusumano-benuara, alta-mora-etna-rosso

SS113 Km 307, Contrada San Carlo, 90047 Partinico (PA), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Settesoli Mandrarossa ★ 4.2

Menfi DOC, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Grillo, Catarratto, Inzolia, Chardonnay, Grecanico, FianoFounded 1958Menfi DOC

Cantine Settesoli is the cooperative giant of Menfi DOC, founded in 1958, with Mandrarossa as its premium terroir-selection brand launched in 1999. Over 2,000 grower-members supply grapes from more than 6,000 hectares, making this the largest wine operation in Sicily.

Tasting: Tuesday to Saturday 09:30 to 18:00. Guided cellar visits and wine tastings. Reservations required.

Signature wines: mandrarossa-terre-di-giumara, mandrarossa-timperosse, mandrarossa-nero-davola

Contrada Puccia, 92013 Menfi (AG), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Tenuta Rapitala ★ 4.3

Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Catarratto, Chardonnay, GrilloFounded 1968Sicilia DOC

Tenuta Rapitala at Camporeale in the Palermo province was established by a Franco-Sicilian partnership in 1968. The estate produces a broad range of Sicilian varietal and blended wines across 170 hectares, with the Grand Cru their prestigious top bottling.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at the Camporeale estate. Contact via the Rapitala website.

Signature wines: rapitala-grand-cru, rapitala-hugonis

Contrada Rapitala, 90043 Camporeale (PA), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Baglio di Pianetto ★ 4.5

Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Syrah, Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, Inzolia, Catarratto, ViognierFounded 1997ORGANIC CERTIFIEDSicilia DOC

Founded in 1997 by Count Paolo Marzotto, Baglio di Pianetto sits at 900 metres near Santa Cristina Gela, 25 kilometres from Palermo. The estate blends Sicilian and French winemaking traditions across 106 hectares, with an elegant agrirelais for overnight stays.

Tasting: Private visits and introductory wine courses at the 20-room agrirelais. Monday to Friday 09:00 to 17:00 by appointment.

Signature wines: baglio-pianetto-ramione, baglio-pianetto-ficiligno, baglio-pianetto-viafrancia

Via Francia, 90030 Santa Cristina Gela (PA), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Firriato ★ 4.3

Etna DOC, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Nerello Mascalese, Perricone, Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, Grillo, Catarratto, Chardonnay, InzoliaFounded 1978Sicilia DOC

Founded by Salvatore Di Gaetano in 1978, Firriato has grown into one of Sicily's largest and most diverse producers with approximately 490 hectares across seven estates. Their reach spans from the Trapani hills to Etna and the island of Favignana.

Tasting: Guided cellar tours, vineyard visits and tastings at the Baglio Soria resort in Paceco and at Cavanera Etnea on Etna. By appointment.

Signature wines: firriato-camelot, firriato-ribeca, firriato-chiaramonte-nero-davola

Via Trapani 4, 91027 Paceco (TP), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Abele Ajello ★ 4.1

Alcamo DOC, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Catarratto, Grillo, InzoliaORGANIC CERTIFIEDAlcamo DOC

Abele Ajello is a small organic producer in the Belice Valley near Castelvetrano, working with Catarratto and Nero d'Avola in the western Sicilian interior.

Tasting: Visits by appointment in the Belice Valley near Castelvetrano. Contact via the winery.

Signature wines: ajello-osa, ajello-tajuni-catarratto

Via dei Mille 33, 91022 Castelvetrano (TP), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Tenuta Gatti ★ 4.2

Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Nerello Mascalese, Nocera, Catarratto, Inzolia, Grillo, Pinot Nero, Cabernet Sauvignon, MerlotFounded 1825ORGANIC CERTIFIEDFaro DOC

A historic organic estate spanning 217 hectares founded in 1825 at Librizzi in the Messina province, set on the Nebrodi slopes with views to the Aeolian Islands. Tenuta Gatti cultivates an unusually diverse mix of native and international varieties.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at the historic Nebrodi estate with views toward the Aeolian Islands. Contact via winetourism.com.

Signature wines: tenuta-gatti-pinot-nero, tenuta-gatti-nero-davola

Via Vignola, 98064 Librizzi (ME), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Planeta Buonivini (Noto) ★ 4.5

Noto DOC, Sicilia DOCNero d'Avola, Syrah, Moscato BiancoFounded 2003Noto DOC

Planeta's Noto estate produces Nero d'Avola and Moscato di Noto from the sun-drenched south-east of Sicily. The Buonivini contrada is within the historic Noto DOC, home to some of Sicily's oldest Nero d'Avola vines.

Tasting: Tasting experience at the Buonivini winery near Noto. Bookable via the Planeta estate website.

Signature wines: planeta-noto-nero-davola

Contrada Buonivini, 96017 Noto (SR), Sicily, Italy → Book →

Still wine in Tokaj

Royal Tokaji ★ 4.8

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályFounded 1990Mád

Founded in 1990 by Hugh Johnson and partners, Royal Tokaji pioneered the post-1989 private revival of single-vineyard Tokaj Aszú from Mád's historic dűlők.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Mád winery; contact via website.

Signature wines: royal-tokaji-mezes-maly-aszu-6-puttonyos, royal-tokaji-nyulaszo-aszu-6-puttonyos, royal-tokaji-szt-tamas-aszu-6-puttonyos, royal-tokaji-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: The Mézes Mály single-vineyard Aszú is the estate's flagship sweet expression; book the cellar visit through the estate website.

Rákóczi utca 35, 3909 Mád, Hungary → Book →

Disznókő ★ 4.7

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga Muskotály, Zéta104 haFounded 1992AXA MillésimesMezőzombor

Acquired in 1992 by AXA Millésimes, Disznókő is the largest single contiguous vineyard estate in Tokaj, producing Aszú and the rare Eszencia from 104 hectares at Mezőzombor.

Tasting: Guided cellar tours and tastings by appointment; guesthouse on site for overnight stays.

Signature wines: disznoko-aszu-6-puttonyos, disznoko-aszu-5-puttonyos, disznoko-eszencia, disznoko-inspiracio-furmint

Tip: Book the cellar tour well in advance; the estate's guesthouse is an atmospheric overnight option in the region.

3931 Mezőzombor, Külterület hrsz.0202, Hungary → Book →

Tokaj Oremus ★ 4.7

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályFounded 1993Tempos Vega SiciliaTolcsva

Oremus was acquired by Tempos Vega Sicilia in 1993 and is based in Tolcsva, where ancient 13th-century cellars underpin a modern winery producing both dry Furmint and Tokaji Aszú.

Tasting: Cellar tours and tastings by appointment at Tolcsva.

Signature wines: oremus-mandolas-furmint, oremus-aszu-5-puttonyos, oremus-aszu-6-puttonyos

Tip: The Mandolás dry Furmint is the estate's flagship white and a key reference for the dry Furmint revival.

Bajcsy-Zsilinszky Endre utca 45, 3934 Tolcsva, Hungary → Book →

Szepsy ★ 4.9

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályMád

The Szepsy family estate in Mád is the original force behind the single-vineyard Tokaj revival; meticulous work in the historic dűlők of Mád defines the dry Furmint movement.

Tasting: Visits by appointment only.

Signature wines: szepsy-furmint, szepsy-uradja-furmint, szepsy-aszu-6-puttonyos

Tip: Visits are strictly by prior arrangement; plan months ahead for harvest-season appointments.

Batthyány utca 59, 3909 Mád, Hungary → Book →

Holdvölgy ★ 4.5

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályMád

Set in central Mád in a 500-year-old cellar running almost 2 km underground, Holdvölgy produces precise single-vineyard dry Furmint and Aszú wines from the premier dűlők of the Mád basin.

Tasting: Open Sun-Thu 10:00-15:00, Fri-Sat 10:00-17:00; online booking via website.

Signature wines: holdvolgy-notabene-furmint, holdvolgy-aszu-6-puttonyos

Tip: The underground cellar tour is atmospheric; book the tasting package in advance.

Árpád utca 13, 3909 Mád, Hungary → Book →

Patricius Winery ★ 4.3

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga Muskotály, Zéta, Kövérszőlő68 haFounded 2000Bodrogkisfalud

Founded in 2000 in Bodrogkisfalud, Patricius cultivates 68 hectares of traditional Tokaj varieties including the rare Kövérszőlő, producing the full range from dry Furmint to Aszú.

Tasting: Monday to Friday 08:00-16:00, year-round; advance booking recommended.

Signature wines: patricius-furmint-dry, patricius-aszu-6-puttonyos

Tip: One of the few estates that bottles Kövérszőlő as a varietal; a rare Tokaj style worth asking about during any visit.

Várhegy dűlő, 3917 Bodrogkisfalud, Hungary → Book →

Béres Vineyards and Winery ★ 4.4

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga Muskotály45 haErdőbénye

Béres Vineyards and Winery occupies 45 hectares in the Bénye Basin at the geographical centre of Tokaj, producing single-vineyard dry whites and Aszú from terraced volcanic soils.

Tasting: Winery visits and tastings by appointment.

Signature wines: beres-locse-furmint, beres-aszu-6-puttonyos

Tip: The Lőcse single-vineyard Furmint is the estate's flagship expression of the volcanic rhyolite tuff terroir.

Bénye-völgy, 3932 Erdőbénye, Hungary → Book →

Kikelet Pince ★ 4.4

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályFounded 2002Winemaker Stéphanie BereczTarcal

Founded in 2002 by Loire-trained winemaker Stéphanie Berecz and her husband Zsolt in Tarcal, Kikelet crafts mineral dry whites and late-harvest wines from volcanic-rock cellars carved into the village bedrock.

Tasting: Winery and cellar visits by appointment in Tarcal.

Signature wines: kikelet-furmint, kikelet-harslevelu, kikelet-late-harvest

Tip: Stéphanie Berecz was awarded Winemaker of Winemakers by her peers in 2014; the natural-rock cellar environment is a highlight of any visit.

Könyves Kálmán utca 62, 3915 Tarcal, Hungary → Book →

Barta Winery ★ 4.4

Tokaj PDOFurmint, HárslevelűMád

Barta Winery in Mád crafts single-vineyard Furmint from the historic Öreg Király dűlő and structured Aszú from meticulously tended volcanic hillside plots above the village.

Tasting: Wine shop and cellar tastings at Rákóczi utca 83, Mád; book via email or phone.

Signature wines: barta-oreg-kiraly-furmint, barta-aszu-6-puttonyos

Tip: The Rákóczi utca wine shop doubles as the tasting room; the Öreg Király (Old King) Furmint is the estate's signature white.

Rákóczi utca 83, 3909 Mád, Hungary → Book →

Sauska Tokaj ★ 4.3

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályRátka

The Sauska family's Tokaj winery, with a new Rátka facility opened in 2023, produces sparkling Furmint, dry whites and Aszú alongside the estate's Villány reds under the same family label.

Tasting: Winery tastings and visits at the Rátka facility, opened November 2023.

Signature wines: sauska-furmint, sauska-tokaj-aszu

Tip: The traditional-method sparkling Furmint is an unusual and rewarding expression of the grape's high natural acidity.

2722 hrsz, 3939 Rátka, Hungary → Book →

Tokaj Nobilis ★ 4.4

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű7 haFounded 2000Winemaker Sarolta BárdosBodrogkeresztúr

Tokaj Nobilis was founded in 2000 in Bodrogkeresztúr by winemaker Sarolta Bárdos and her husband Péter Molnár; the 7-hectare estate focuses on single-vineyard expressions and also offers guesthouse accommodation.

Tasting: Winery visits, tastings and accommodation in Bodrogkeresztúr; booking via website.

Signature wines: tokaj-nobilis-barakonyi-furmint, tokaj-nobilis-aszu

Tip: Sarolta Bárdos is one of the region's most compelling advocates for dry Furmint; the Barakonyi plot delivers notably saline minerality.

Bodrogkeresztúr, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Pendits ★ 4.4

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalAbaújszántó

Pendits in Abaújszántó became Hungary's first Demeter-certified biodynamic winery in 2011 and the region's first certified organic estate in 2008, producing low-intervention Furmint and rare Aszú Essencia.

Tasting: Winery visits by appointment in Abaújszántó.

Signature wines: pendits-furmint, pendits-aszu-essencia

Tip: The first Hungarian winery to achieve both Demeter biodynamic and organic certification; a reference point for natural-leaning Tokaj.

Béke utca 111, 3881 Abaújszántó, Hungary → Book →

Bott Pince ★ 4.3

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű6 haFounded 2005ORGANIC CERTIFIEDNaturalBodrogkisfalud

Bott Pince is a 6-hectare family estate founded in 2005, farming six single vineyards across Tokaj with no herbicides, no enzymes and spontaneous fermentation from native yeasts.

Tasting: Visits by appointment only; contact via email.

Signature wines: bott-csontos-furmint, bott-teleki-furmint

Tip: With only 6 hectares split among six named plots, each bottling is genuinely single-site; the Csontos from Erdőbénye-Olaszliszka slopes is the most characteristic.

Bodrogkisfalud, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Erzsébet Pince ★ 4.2

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályFounded 1989Winemaker Erzsébet PrácserTokaj

Founded in 1989 on Tokaj's historic wine merchants' street by Erzsébet Prácser, one of the region's first female winemakers, this family cellar produces traditional dry and sweet Tokaj styles.

Tasting: Cellar tastings in the heart of Tokaj town; booking via website.

Signature wines: erzsebet-furmint-dry, erzsebet-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: The cellar sits on Bem Street, the historic wine trading street of Tokaj town; the family's personal history spans the transition from state to private winemaking.

Bem József utca 16, 3910 Tokaj, Hungary → Book →

Hímesudvar ★ 4.0

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga Muskotály3 haFounded 1983Tokaj

Established in 1983 in a 500-year-old building on Bem Street in Tokaj town, Hímesudvar produces small-batch Furmint, Hárslevelű and Sárga Muskotály from 3.5 hectares of estate vines.

Tasting: Cellar visits in Tokaj town centre; five-minute walk from the main square.

Signature wines: himesudvar-furmint, himesudvar-harslevelu

Tip: The historic building once served as a hunting lodge for King János Szapolyai; the cellar is among the oldest continually operated in the region.

Bem József utca 2, 3910 Tokaj, Hungary → Book →

Gizella Pince ★ 4.3

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályTokaj

Gizella Pince produces wines from 11 different Tokaj vineyards; Furmint dominates at 62% of plantings, including the historically documented Szil-völgy reconstruction project dating back to 1612.

Tasting: Winery visits by appointment in Tokaj town.

Signature wines: gizella-furmint-harslevelu, gizella-szil-volgy-furmint

Tip: The reconstruction of the historically documented Szil-völgy vineyard site produces very limited quantities worth seeking out at the cellar.

Dienes Pál utca 78, 3910 Tokaj, Hungary → Book →

Grand Tokaj ★ 3.9

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga Muskotály, ZétaFounded 1948Hungarian StateTolcsva

Grand Tokaj, formerly Tokaj Kereskedőház, is the state-owned large-format producer, rebranded in 2016, producing the full range of Tokaj styles at commercial scale and widely distributed internationally.

Tasting: Winery visits and group tastings; contact via website.

Signature wines: grand-tokaj-aszu-5-puttonyos, grand-tokaj-furmint

Tip: Grand Tokaj's wines are widely available internationally and a practical entry point for exploring the region's principal styles.

Tolcsva, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Füleky Pince ★ 4.0

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályBodrogkeresztúr

Füleky Pince is a family estate in Bodrogkeresztúr at the Bodrog-Tisza confluence, a village whose morning mists reliably stimulate noble rot; the estate produces Furmint and Aszú from these fog-prone slopes.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings in Bodrogkeresztúr by appointment.

Signature wines: fuleky-furmint, fuleky-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: Bodrogkeresztúr's autumn fogs produce some of the region's most consistently botrytised grapes; the village is a good base for understanding the geographical origins of Tokaji Aszú.

Bodrogkeresztúr, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Demeter Zoltán ★ 4.3

Tokaj PDOFurmint, HárslevelűWinemaker Zoltán DemeterMád

Zoltán Demeter is a boutique Mád producer making concentrated single-vineyard Furmint and precise Aszú from tiny parcels on the volcanic slopes above the village.

Tasting: Visits by prior appointment only.

Signature wines: demeter-zoltan-furmint, demeter-zoltan-aszu-6-puttonyos

Tip: Production is very small; wines are primarily available through specialist importers and local Mád tasting rooms.

Mád, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Zsirai Pince ★ 4.1

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályTarcal

Zsirai Pince in Tarcal grows vines on the southern slopes of Kopasz-hegy including plots adjacent to the historic Mézes Mály dűlő, one of Tokaj's first-class classified vineyards, producing full-bodied dry Furmint and Aszú.

Tasting: Winery visits by appointment in Tarcal.

Signature wines: zsirai-mezes-maly-furmint, zsirai-aszu-6-puttonyos

Tip: Tarcal is the southernmost major wine village in Tokaj; its maximum sun exposure produces noticeably richer fruit than the cooler northern villages.

Tarcal, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Balassa Pince ★ 4.0

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályTolcsva

Balassa Pince is based in Tolcsva, home village of Oremus, producing dry Furmint and textured Szamorodni from the rolling volcanic hillsides and ancient cellar tunnels of the Tolcsva valley.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings in Tolcsva by appointment.

Signature wines: balassa-furmint, balassa-szamorodni

Tip: Tolcsva's deep historic cellar network runs for hundreds of metres through volcanic tuff; an atmospheric location for any tasting.

Tolcsva, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Kovács Nimród Winery ★ 4.1

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga Muskotály, KabarWinemaker Nimród KovácsMezőzombor

Kovács Nimród Winery in Mezőzombor produces a range of Tokaj styles including the rare Kabar variety alongside Furmint and Hárslevelű under the winemaker's own label.

Tasting: Winery visits and tastings in Mezőzombor by appointment.

Signature wines: kovacs-nimrod-furmint, kovacs-nimrod-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: One of the few estates that bottles Kabar (Hárslevelű × Bouvier crossing) as a varietal wine; a rare Tokaj style worth seeking out.

Mezőzombor, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Tokaj Hétszőlő ★ 4.1

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályMád

Tokaj Hétszőlő, now part of the Grand Tokaj family, takes its name from one of Mád's historically documented vineyard sites, producing Aszú from clay-volcanic soils documented since the 17th century.

Tasting: Part of the Grand Tokaj group; visits by arrangement via Grand Tokaj.

Signature wines: tokaj-hetszolos-furmint, tokaj-hetszolos-aszu

Tip: The Hétszőlő name (seven vineyards) appears in historical Tokaj documents from the early 1600s; the estate carries the weight of the region's long documented viticultural history.

Mád, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Szepsy Jr Pince ★ 4.7

Tokaj PDOFurmint, HárslevelűWinemaker István Szepsy Jr.Mád

István Szepsy Jr. continues the Szepsy family tradition at the Mád estate, producing single-vineyard Furmint and Aszú with the same rigorous approach to dűlő selection that defined the original Szepsy revival.

Tasting: Visits by appointment only in Mád.

Signature wines: szepsy-jr-furmint-nyulaszo, szepsy-jr-aszu-6-puttonyos

Tip: The father-and-son succession at Szepsy is one of the clearest examples of generational knowledge transfer in Tokaj winemaking.

Mád, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Malatinszky Tokaj ★ 4.0

Tokaj PDOFurmint, HárslevelűMád

Malatinszky Tokaj produces mineral dry Furmint from Mád-area plots, complementing the same estate family known for reds from Villány; a rare dual-appellation Hungarian producer.

Tasting: Visits by appointment; estate also produces at Villány.

Signature wines: malatinszky-tokaj-furmint, malatinszky-tokaj-aszu

Tip: A useful comparative tasting when combined with Villány reds to understand the span of Hungarian wine from volcanic north to limestone south.

Mád, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Almási Pince ★ 3.9

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályHercegkút

Almási Pince is a family estate in Hercegkút, a Swabian settlement whose famous cellar street with barrel-roofed cellars built into the hillside creates one of Tokaj's most unusual and photogenic village streetscapes.

Tasting: Family cellar in Hercegkút's Swabian cellar street; visits by appointment.

Signature wines: almasi-pince-furmint-dry, almasi-pince-harslevelu

Tip: Hercegkút's Swabian cellar street is a short walk from the main road; the uniform barrel-vaulted cellar doors built into the hill are a memorable architectural sight unlike anything else in Tokaj.

Hercegkút, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Vincze Pince ★ 3.9

Tokaj PDOFurmint, HárslevelűErdőbénye

Vincze Pince is a family estate in the elevated forest-village of Erdőbénye, producing racy dry Furmint and late-harvest wine from cool-climate volcanic plots at higher elevations within the appellation.

Tasting: Family estate; visits by appointment.

Signature wines: vincze-pince-furmint-dry, vincze-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: Erdőbénye's altitude and forest cover produce Furmint with unusually high natural acidity; compare with a Mád or Tarcal bottling to appreciate the contrasting terroir.

Erdőbénye, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Fekete Pince ★ 3.9

Tokaj PDOFurmint, HárslevelűSárospatak

Fekete Pince in Sárospatak produces Furmint and Aszú from plots in the northern sector of the Tokaj appellation, where cooler temperatures extend the growing season and produce wines of heightened natural acidity.

Tasting: Cellar tastings in Sárospatak; visits by arrangement.

Signature wines: fekete-pince-furmint-dry, fekete-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: Sárospatak's 16th-century Renaissance castle complex is within walking distance of the cellar; combine the tasting with a visit to the historic castle and Calvinist college.

Sárospatak, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Harsányi Pince ★ 3.8

Tokaj PDOFurmint, HárslevelűSátoraljaújhely

Harsányi Pince is a family producer in Sátoraljaújhely, the northernmost town of the Tokaj appellation bordering Slovakia, growing Furmint and Hárslevelű on sheltered south-facing terraces in the appellation's coolest zone.

Tasting: Family winery in Sátoraljaújhely; visits by prior arrangement.

Signature wines: harsanyi-pince-furmint-dry, harsanyi-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: Sátoraljaújhely is the most northerly village of the appellation; wines from here show a more restrained, higher-acid profile than those from the sunnier Mád basin.

Sátoraljaújhely, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Katona Pince ★ 3.8

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályAbaújszántó

Katona Pince is a family producer in Abaújszántó, the southernmost wine village of Tokaj-Hegyalja, producing Furmint, Sárga Muskotály and Aszú from sheltered south-facing volcanic slopes.

Tasting: Cellar visits in Abaújszántó by appointment.

Signature wines: katona-pince-furmint-dry, katona-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: Abaújszántó sits at the southern gateway to the appellation; its wines show warmer, riper fruit character than the central Mád villages.

Abaújszántó, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Bognár Pince ★ 3.9

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályTokaj

Bognár Pince is a traditional family estate in Tokaj town producing dry Furmint and both styles of Szamorodni alongside Aszú, representing the full range of styles historically associated with the eponymous wine capital.

Tasting: Family cellar in Tokaj town; visits by prior arrangement.

Signature wines: bognar-pince-furmint-dry, bognar-pince-szamorodni-edes

Tip: Dry Szamorodni (száraz) is one of Tokaj's most underrated styles; aged examples develop sherry-like oxidative complexity and pair well with aged cheese.

Tokaj, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Papp Pince ★ 3.9

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályTarcal

Papp Pince is a family producer in Tarcal growing Furmint, Hárslevelű and Sárga Muskotály on south-facing volcanic terraces that define this village's fuller, more concentrated style of wine.

Tasting: Family winery; visits by prior arrangement in Tarcal.

Signature wines: papp-pince-furmint-dry, papp-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: Tarcal's Mézes Mály (Honey Meadow) is among the historically classified first-class dűlők; ask which plots the family tends during any visit.

Tarcal, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Jäger Pince ★ 4.0

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályMád

Jäger Pince is a boutique family estate in Mád focusing on single-parcel Furmint and small-volume Aszú from the village's historic volcanic clay and rhyolite tuff soils.

Tasting: Cellar visits by appointment in Mád.

Signature wines: jager-pince-furmint-dry, jager-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: In a village of many cellars, Jäger Pince offers an unhurried, personal tasting experience.

Mád, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Hétényi Pince ★ 3.9

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályTokaj

Hétényi Pince is a small family estate in Tokaj town producing dry Furmint, Sárga Muskotály and late-harvest wine from plots across the PDO, continuing a multigenerational family tradition in the wine capital.

Tasting: Family cellar tastings in Tokaj town; contact in advance.

Signature wines: hetenyi-pince-furmint-dry, hetenyi-pince-late-harvest

Tip: Tokaj town has several family cellars along its historic wine streets; combining two or three tasting visits in a single afternoon on foot is entirely feasible.

Tokaj, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Erdei Pince ★ 3.8

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályErdőbénye

Erdei Pince is a boutique estate in the hillside village of Erdőbénye set among forested ridges, producing mineral Furmint and fragrant Sárga Muskotály from sheltered volcanic plots at altitude.

Tasting: Family winery; visits by appointment.

Signature wines: erdei-pince-furmint-dry, erdei-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: Erdőbénye's elevated, forested position gives its wines a distinctive cool-climate lift compared to the valley-floor villages.

Erdőbénye, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Dorógi Pince ★ 3.8

Tokaj PDOFurmint, HárslevelűBodrogkisfalud

Dorógi Pince is a small family winery in Bodrogkisfalud producing dry Furmint and Aszú from clay-volcanic slopes bordering the Bodrog river, where autumn fog reliably creates botrytis conditions.

Tasting: Family winery; visits by appointment.

Signature wines: dorogi-pince-furmint-dry, dorogi-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: A classic small-producer experience in a village that receives far fewer visitors than Mád or Tokaj town.

Bodrogkisfalud, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Molnár Pince ★ 3.8

Tokaj PDOFurmint, HárslevelűOlaszliszka

Molnár Pince in Bodrogolaszi on the Bodrog riverbank maximises autumn fog exposure for reliable botrytis; the family makes dry Furmint and traditional sweet styles from Bodrog-riverside plots.

Tasting: Cellar visits by appointment in Bodrogolaszi.

Signature wines: molnar-pince-furmint-dry, molnar-pince-szamorodni-edes

Tip: The Bodrog riverbanks near Olaszliszka and Bodrogolaszi produce some of the most consistently botrytised grapes in the whole appellation.

Bodrogolaszi, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Lapis Pince ★ 3.9

Tokaj PDOFurmint, HárslevelűOlaszliszka

Lapis Pince is a small family producer in Olaszliszka, a Bodrog-valley village whose riverine fog creates ideal botrytis conditions; the estate produces Furmint and Aszú in a traditional style.

Tasting: Family cellar visits by appointment in Olaszliszka.

Signature wines: lapis-pince-furmint-dry, lapis-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: Olaszliszka sits directly on the Bodrog and experiences heavy morning mist in autumn; harvest-time visits are particularly atmospheric.

Olaszliszka, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Várhelyi Pince ★ 3.9

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályTokaj

Várhelyi Pince is a family cellar in central Tokaj town producing dry Furmint and sweet Aszú from volcanic plots across the region, with multigenerational roots in the historic wine-street quarter.

Tasting: Cellar tastings in Tokaj town; contact in advance.

Signature wines: varhelyi-pince-furmint-dry, varhelyi-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: Located in the historic Bem Street wine quarter of Tokaj; an atmospheric traditional cellar tasting.

Tokaj, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Winesor Pince ★ 3.9

Tokaj PDOFurmint, HárslevelűSárospatak

Winesor Pince is based in the historic town of Sárospatak, associated with Hungarian Calvinist culture and winemaking, producing dry Furmint and Aszú from the northern sector of the appellation.

Tasting: Winery visits and tastings by appointment in Sárospatak.

Signature wines: winesor-pince-furmint-dry, winesor-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: Sárospatak combines Renaissance castle, Calvinist college and wine cellars within a walkable town centre.

Sárospatak, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Tokajer Pince ★ 4.0

Tokaj PDOFurmint, Hárslevelű, Sárga MuskotályTállya

Tokajer Pince is a family producer in Tállya whose wines from the Király dűlő represent the northernmost concentration of first-class classified vineyard sites in the appellation.

Tasting: Family winery in Tállya; tastings by prior arrangement.

Signature wines: tokajer-pince-furmint-dry, tokajer-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: Tállya's Király dűlő has been classified as first class since the 18th-century royal decree; wines from here show structured, mineral character.

Tállya, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Karácsonyi Pince ★ 3.9

Tokaj PDOFurmint, HárslevelűTállya

Karácsonyi Pince is a small family winery in Tállya, producing traditional dry Furmint and Aszú from the northern Zemplén foothills terraces including historic first-class classified Király parcels.

Tasting: Family winery; visits by appointment in Tállya.

Signature wines: karacsony-pince-furmint-dry, karacsony-pince-aszu-5-puttonyos

Tip: Tállya is less visited than Mád but has among the oldest vine stock in the region; the Király dűlő is classified since the 1700s royal decree.

Tállya, Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Hungary → Book →

Still wine in Tuscany

Marchesi Antinori ★ 4.9

Chianti Classico DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot270 haFounded 1385Antinori family (Marchese Piero Antinori, chairman emeritus; Albiera Antinori, president)Winemaker Renzo CotarellaChianti Classico DOCG

The Antinori family has produced wine in Tuscany since 1385. Anchored by the Tignanello estate in Chianti Classico, the parent house also owns Bolgheri's Guado al Tasso under a separate producer slug.

Tasting: Antinori nel Chianti Classico cellar at Bargino offers visits, tastings and the Rinuccio 1180 restaurant; multi-tier tasting programmes by reservation.

Signature wines: tignanello, solaia, marchese-antinori-chianti-classico-riserva, villa-antinori-chianti-classico-riserva

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2019), Vinous 96 (2019)

Piazza degli Antinori 3, 50123 Firenze, Italy → Book →

Castello di Ama ★ 4.8

Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Malvasia Nera, Merlot, Chardonnay, Pinot Nero90 haFounded 1972Lorenza Sebasti and Marco PallantiWinemaker Marco PallantiChianti Classico DOCG

Founded in 1972 in the hamlet of Ama at Gaiole in Chianti, the estate produces single-vineyard Gran Selezione bottlings from Bellavista and La Casuccia and pairs the wine programme with a contemporary art collection.

Tasting: Cellar tour, hamlet walk, art collection and tasting flight bookable through the estate; the property hosts a contemporary art programme with installations by Anish Kapoor and Louise Bourgeois.

Signature wines: castello-di-ama-vigneto-bellavista, castello-di-ama-vigneto-la-casuccia, castello-di-ama-haiku, castello-di-ama-l-apparita

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2019), Vinous 95 (2018)

Localita Ama, 53013 Gaiole in Chianti, Siena, Italy → Book →

Felsina ★ 4.7

Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Chardonnay95 haFounded 1966Domenico Poggiali and familyWinemaker Giovanni PoggialiChianti Classico DOCG

Castelnuovo Berardenga estate established 1966 by Domenico Poggiali. Sangiovese-focused with the Rancia Gran Selezione and the IGT Fontalloro forming the core of the range.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tasting flights by appointment; the Rancia single-vineyard Gran Selezione is the estate's flagship.

Signature wines: felsina-rancia-chianti-classico-riserva, felsina-fontalloro, felsina-berardenga-chianti-classico

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2019), Vinous 95 (2019)

Via del Chianti 101, 53019 Castelnuovo Berardenga, Siena, Italy → Book →

Fontodi ★ 4.9

Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, Pinot Nero90 haFounded 1968Manetti familyWinemaker Giovanni ManettiICEAChianti Classico DOCG

Panzano estate in the Conca d'Oro amphitheatre, owned by the Manetti family since 1968 and ICEA-organic certified since 1990. Flaccianello della Pieve is the flagship Sangiovese IGT.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment in the Conca d'Oro amphitheatre of Panzano; organic since 1990 and certified by ICEA.

Signature wines: fontodi-flaccianello-della-pieve, fontodi-vigna-del-sorbo, fontodi-chianti-classico

Scores: Wine Advocate 97 (2019), Vinous 96 (2019)

Localita San Leolino 89, 50022 Panzano in Chianti, Firenze, Italy → Book →

Isole e Olena ★ 4.8

Chianti Classico DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, Chardonnay56 haFounded 1956EPI Group (Christopher Descours), acquired from the De Marchi family in 2022Winemaker Emanuele ReolonChianti Classico DOCG

Founded in 1956 by the De Marchi family; the EPI Group acquired the estate in 2022. The Cepparello pure Sangiovese IGT remains the flagship alongside the Chianti Classico.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment; Cepparello (100% Sangiovese IGT) is the long-running flagship from the De Marchi era.

Signature wines: isole-e-olena-cepparello, isole-e-olena-chianti-classico, isole-e-olena-collezione-de-marchi-syrah

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2019), Vinous 95 (2018)

Localita Isole 1, 50021 Barberino Tavarnelle, Firenze, Italy → Book →

San Felice ★ 4.6

Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Pugnitello, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot200 haFounded 1968Allianz Group (insurance investor); historic property of the Grisaldi del Taja familyWinemaker Leonardo BellacciniChianti Classico DOCG

Castelnuovo Berardenga estate combining a Relais & Chateaux resort with a Chianti Classico programme that recovered the indigenous Pugnitello grape from near-extinction.

Tasting: The Borgo San Felice resort hosts cellar tours, tasting flights and a vineyard experience programme; the property includes a Relais & Chateaux hotel.

Signature wines: san-felice-il-grigio-gran-selezione, san-felice-vigorello, san-felice-pugnitello

Scores: Wine Advocate 95 (2019), Vinous 94 (2018)

Localita San Felice, 53019 Castelnuovo Berardenga, Siena, Italy → Book →

Castello di Volpaia ★ 4.7

Chianti Classico DOCG Gran SelezioneSangiovese, Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon49 haFounded 1172Mascheroni Stianti familyWinemaker Giancarlo CecchiICEAChianti Classico DOCG

ICEA-organic certified estate on a fortified hamlet at 600 metres in Radda in Chianti, with the cellar dispersed through medieval buildings owned by the Mascheroni Stianti family.

Tasting: The fortified medieval hamlet of Volpaia houses the cellar across the village buildings; tastings, cellar walks and Bottega lunches by booking.

Signature wines: castello-di-volpaia-coltassala, castello-di-volpaia-il-puro-gran-selezione, castello-di-volpaia-chianti-classico

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2019), Vinous 94 (2018)

Localita Volpaia, 53017 Radda in Chianti, Siena, Italy → Book →

Querciabella ★ 4.7

Chianti Classico DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Pinot Bianco, Chardonnay81 haFounded 1974Castiglioni familyWinemaker Manfred IngDemeter CertifiedECOCERTChianti Classico DOCG

Greve in Chianti estate founded 1974 by the Castiglioni family. Demeter-certified biodynamic with vegan vinification practices; Camartina is the long-running Sangiovese-Cabernet IGT.

Tasting: Tastings and estate visits by appointment; the entire farming operation is Demeter biodynamic and the cellar uses no animal products in vinification.

Signature wines: querciabella-camartina, querciabella-batar, querciabella-chianti-classico

Scores: Wine Advocate 95 (2018), Vinous 94 (2018)

Via di Barbiano 17, 50022 Greve in Chianti, Firenze, Italy → Book →

Castell'in Villa ★ 4.6

Chianti Classico DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Malvasia Nera55 haFounded 1968Principessa Coralia Pignatelli della LeonessaWinemaker Coralia Pignatelli della Leonessa with Vittorio Fiore consultingChianti Classico DOCG

Castelnuovo Berardenga property owned since 1968 by Principessa Coralia Pignatelli, producing some of Chianti Classico's longest-lived traditionally-styled Riservas.

Tasting: Estate visits and tasting flights by appointment; lunch available in the borgo with prior reservation.

Signature wines: castell-in-villa-chianti-classico-riserva, castell-in-villa-poggio-delle-rose-riserva, castell-in-villa-santacroce

Scores: Vinous 95 (2016), Wine Advocate 94 (2016)

Localita Castell'in Villa, 53019 Castelnuovo Berardenga, Siena, Italy → Book →

Riecine ★ 4.7

Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Canaiolo Nero13 haFounded 1971Lana FrankWinemaker Alessandro CampatelliBiodynamic PracticingICEAChianti Classico DOCG

Tiny Gaiole estate of 13 hectares founded in 1971 by Englishman John Dunkley. Organic since 2014, vegan vinification, and now producing 100% Sangiovese under the La Gioia Toscana IGT flagship.

Tasting: Small-group estate tastings by reservation; vegan-vinified wines.

Signature wines: riecine-la-gioia, riecine-chianti-classico-riserva, riecine-chianti-classico

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2018), Vinous 95 (2019)

Localita Riecine, 53013 Gaiole in Chianti, Siena, Italy → Book →

Castello di Querceto ★ 4.4

Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Mammolo60 haFounded 1897Francois familyWinemaker Alessandro FrancoisChianti Classico DOCG

Francois family estate in Greve in Chianti dating to 1897, set around a medieval castle. Il Picchio Gran Selezione anchors a Sangiovese-led range alongside Super Tuscan IGT bottlings.

Tasting: Cellar tours and tasting flights from the medieval castle property; on-site agriturismo accommodation.

Signature wines: castello-di-querceto-il-picchio, castello-di-querceto-la-corte, castello-di-querceto-chianti-classico-riserva

Scores: Wine Advocate 93 (2019), Vinous 92 (2018)

Via Alessandro Francois 2, 50022 Greve in Chianti, Firenze, Italy → Book →

Biondi-Santi ★ 4.9

Brunello di Montalcino DOCGSangiovese Grosso25 haFounded 1888EPI Group (Christopher Descours), acquired from the Biondi Santi family in 2017Winemaker Federico RadiBrunello di Montalcino DOCG

The founding estate of Brunello di Montalcino; Ferruccio Biondi Santi bottled the first 1888 vintage. Now owned by EPI Group with the historic Greppo Sangiovese Grosso vines as the core.

Tasting: Cellar tours of the historic Greppo property by appointment; rare vintages tasting on request.

Signature wines: biondi-santi-brunello-di-montalcino, biondi-santi-brunello-di-montalcino-riserva, biondi-santi-rosso-di-montalcino

Scores: Wine Advocate 97 (2016), Vinous 97 (2016)

Villa Greppo 183, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy → Book →

Case Basse di Gianfranco Soldera ★ 4.9

Toscana IGT (estate withdrew from Brunello DOCG in 2013)Sangiovese8 haFounded 1972Soldera family (children of Gianfranco Soldera, who passed in 2019)Winemaker Mauro Fastelli (cellar master under Gianfranco Soldera)Biodynamic PracticingBrunello di Montalcino DOCG (now bottled as Toscana IGT)

Cult Montalcino estate founded 1972 by Gianfranco Soldera. Withdrew from Brunello DOCG in 2013 after a cellar sabotage incident; bottles continue as Toscana IGT from the original Intistieti and Case Basse vineyards.

Tasting: Not currently open to the public; allocation-only sales via mailing list.

Signature wines: soldera-case-basse-toscana-igt, soldera-case-basse-intistieti-vineyard

Localita Case Basse, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy → Book →

Casanova di Neri ★ 4.9

Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese Grosso, Cabernet Sauvignon63 haFounded 1971Neri family (Giacomo Neri, owner and winemaker)Winemaker Giacomo NeriBrunello di Montalcino DOCG

Family estate founded 1971, with the single-vineyard Cerretalto and the Tenuta Nuova selection driving the reputation. Wine Spectator named the 2001 Tenuta Nuova its 2006 Wine of the Year.

Tasting: Cellar visits, vineyard walks and vertical tastings of Tenuta Nuova and Cerretalto by appointment.

Signature wines: casanova-di-neri-cerretalto, casanova-di-neri-tenuta-nuova, casanova-di-neri-brunello-di-montalcino, casanova-di-neri-pietradonice

Scores: Wine Spectator 97 (2001), Wine Advocate 98 (2016)

Podere Fiesole, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy → Book →

Castello Banfi ★ 4.6

Brunello di Montalcino DOCG, Sant'Antimo DOC and Toscana IGTSangiovese Grosso, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Syrah, Chardonnay850 haFounded 1978Mariani family (Castello Banfi srl)Winemaker Rudy BurattiBrunello di Montalcino DOCG

Single largest Brunello estate at 850 hectares of vineyards inside Montalcino. Mariani family-owned since 1978 and home to the Castello di Poggio alle Mura medieval fortress and Relais hotel.

Tasting: Castello Banfi houses a Relais & Chateaux hotel, restaurants, an enoteca and the Banfi glass-and-wine museum; cellar tours and tasting flights run daily.

Signature wines: banfi-poggio-alle-mura-brunello, banfi-brunello-di-montalcino, banfi-summus, banfi-poggio-all-oro-riserva

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2016), Vinous 94 (2016)

Castello di Poggio alle Mura, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy → Book →

Tenuta Caparzo ★ 4.5

Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese Grosso, Sauvignon Blanc, Chardonnay90 haFounded 1970Elisabetta Gnudi AngeliniWinemaker Massimo BracalenteICEABrunello di Montalcino DOCG

Founded 1970 in the cooler northern Montalcino. The Vigna La Casa single-vineyard Brunello has been bottled since 1977; estate has been organic since 2014.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by reservation; the Vigna La Casa cru is the historical flagship.

Signature wines: caparzo-la-casa-brunello, caparzo-brunello-di-montalcino, caparzo-le-grance

Scores: Wine Advocate 95 (2016), Vinous 93 (2016)

Localita Caparzo 6, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy → Book →

Poggio Antico ★ 4.6

Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese Grosso, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot32 haFounded 1984Marcel Van Poecke (Belgian; via AtlasInvest, acquired 2017)Winemaker Paolo SalviICEABrunello di Montalcino DOCG

High-elevation estate at 450-500 metres in the southern Montalcino. Certified organic with ICEA, producing structured age-worthy Brunello and the Altero IGT Sangiovese.

Tasting: Estate visits, tastings and on-site restaurant overlooking the southern Montalcino vineyards.

Signature wines: poggio-antico-brunello-di-montalcino, poggio-antico-brunello-riserva, poggio-antico-altero

Scores: Wine Advocate 95 (2016), Vinous 94 (2016)

Localita Poggio Antico, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy → Book →

Il Poggione ★ 4.8

Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese Grosso, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot125 haFounded 1890Franceschi familyWinemaker Alessandro BindocciBrunello di Montalcino DOCG

Franceschi-family estate founded 1890 in Sant'Angelo in Colle, southern Montalcino. Vigna Paganelli Riserva uses the estate's oldest Sangiovese Grosso vines planted in 1964.

Tasting: Estate tours, cellar visits and tasting flights at Sant'Angelo in Colle by appointment.

Signature wines: il-poggione-brunello-di-montalcino, il-poggione-vigna-paganelli-riserva, il-poggione-rosso-di-montalcino

Scores: Wine Advocate 97 (2016), Vinous 96 (2016)

Localita Monteano, 53024 Sant'Angelo in Colle, Montalcino, Siena, Italy → Book →

Mastrojanni ★ 4.8

Brunello di Montalcino DOCGSangiovese Grosso, Cabernet Sauvignon, Sangiovese24 haFounded 1975Illy family (Illycaffe), since 2008Winemaker Andrea MachettiBrunello di Montalcino DOCG

Castelnuovo dell'Abate estate founded in 1975 and acquired by the Illy family in 2008. The Vigna Loreto and Schiena d'Asino crus are among Montalcino's most idiosyncratic Sangiovese expressions.

Tasting: Estate tours and cellar visits by appointment; the southeast-facing Vigna Loreto cru is the property's calling card.

Signature wines: mastrojanni-vigna-loreto, mastrojanni-brunello-di-montalcino, mastrojanni-schiena-d-asino

Scores: Wine Advocate 97 (2016), Vinous 95 (2016)

Podere Loreto e San Pio, 53024 Castelnuovo dell'Abate, Montalcino, Siena, Italy → Book →

Argiano ★ 4.9

Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese Grosso, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Petit Verdot125 haFounded 1580Andre Esteves (Brazilian financier; acquired from Countess Noemi Marone Cinzano in 2013)Winemaker Bernardino SaniBrunello di Montalcino DOCG

Renaissance villa estate at Sant'Angelo in Colle dating to 1580. Wine Spectator named the 2018 Brunello its Wine of the Year for 2023 (95 points), the first Italian wine to claim that ranking in two decades.

Tasting: Cellar tours, tasting flights and the Casa di Argiano dining experience at the Renaissance villa.

Signature wines: argiano-brunello-di-montalcino, argiano-vigna-del-suolo, argiano-solengo

Scores: Wine Spectator 95 (2018), Wine Advocate 93 (2018)

Sant'Angelo in Colle, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy → Book →

Col d'Orcia ★ 4.7

Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese Grosso, Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, Petit Verdot145 haFounded 1890Conte Francesco Marone CinzanoWinemaker Pablo HarriICEABrunello di Montalcino DOCG

Cinzano family estate at Sant'Angelo in Colle, 145 hectares of vines under one of Italy's largest organic conversions (certified ICEA 2013). Poggio al Vento Riserva is the historic cru.

Tasting: Estate tours, cellar visits and tastings by reservation; one of the largest organic-certified estates in Italy.

Signature wines: col-dorcia-poggio-al-vento-riserva, col-dorcia-brunello-di-montalcino, col-dorcia-olmaia

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2015), Vinous 95 (2016)

Localita Sant'Angelo in Colle, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy → Book →

Avignonesi ★ 4.8

Vino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG, Vin Santo di Montepulciano DOC and Toscana IGTSangiovese (Prugnolo Gentile), Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Trebbiano, Malvasia Bianca Lunga200 haFounded 1974Virginie SaverysWinemaker Matteo GiustinianiDemeter CertifiedECOCERTVino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG

Montepulciano estate acquired by Belgian shipowner Virginie Saverys in 2009 and converted to Demeter-certified biodynamics. The Occhio di Pernice Vin Santo from Sangiovese remains a benchmark of the style.

Tasting: Cellar tours, Vin Santo vinsantaia tastings and estate lunches by reservation; certified biodynamic by Demeter.

Signature wines: avignonesi-vino-nobile, avignonesi-grandi-annate, avignonesi-occhio-di-pernice, avignonesi-50-50

Scores: Vinous 95 (2017)

Via Colonica 1, 53045 Valiano di Montepulciano, Siena, Italy → Book →

Poderi Boscarelli ★ 4.7

Vino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese (Prugnolo Gentile), Canaiolo Nero, Mammolo, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot16 haFounded 1962De Ferrari Corradi familyWinemaker Maurizio Castelli (consulting) with Luca De FerrariVino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG

De Ferrari Corradi family estate at Cervognano, 16 hectares of high-density Prugnolo Gentile. The Vigna del Nocio Vino Nobile, from a single 1968-planted vineyard, is the flagship.

Tasting: Boutique cellar visits and tastings by appointment; the Vigna del Nocio single-vineyard is the estate's calling card.

Signature wines: boscarelli-vigna-del-nocio, boscarelli-vino-nobile, boscarelli-boscarelli-igt

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2019), Vinous 95 (2019)

Via di Montenero 28, 53045 Cervognano, Montepulciano, Siena, Italy → Book →

Salcheto ★ 4.6

Vino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese (Prugnolo Gentile), Canaiolo Nero, Mammolo, Merlot60 haFounded 1984Michele ManelliWinemaker Michele Manelli with Federico Curtaz consultingBiodynamic PracticingCCPBVino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG

Off-grid solar-powered cellar at Montepulciano, certified organic by CCPB. Salco Evoluzione is the Riserva flagship; the estate publishes a carbon-footprint declaration on every label.

Tasting: Tours of the off-grid energy-independent cellar, tastings and a restaurant overlooking Montepulciano; certified organic.

Signature wines: salcheto-salco-evoluzione-vino-nobile-riserva, salcheto-vino-nobile, salcheto-rosso-di-montepulciano

Scores: Wine Advocate 94 (2017), Vinous 93 (2018)

Via di Villa Bianca 15, 53045 Montepulciano, Siena, Italy → Book →

Poliziano ★ 4.7

Vino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese (Prugnolo Gentile), Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Canaiolo Nero130 haFounded 1961Carletti family (Federico Carletti, owner)Winemaker Federico CarlettiVino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG

Carletti family estate founded 1961 at the foot of Montepulciano. The Asinone single-vineyard Vino Nobile, named after the donkey-shaped hillside, is among the most consistently reviewed wines of the DOCG.

Tasting: Cellar tours and tasting flights by appointment; the Asinone single-vineyard Vino Nobile is the flagship.

Signature wines: poliziano-asinone, poliziano-vino-nobile, poliziano-le-stanze

Scores: Wine Advocate 94 (2019), Vinous 94 (2018)

Via Fontago 1, 53045 Montepulciano Stazione, Siena, Italy → Book →

Tenuta San Guido ★ 5.0

Bolgheri Sassicaia DOC (single-estate DOC, established 1994)Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc90 haFounded 1944Incisa della Rocchetta family (Marchese Nicolo Incisa della Rocchetta, president)Winemaker Carlo Paoli (estate manager) with Graziana Grassini consultingBolgheri Sassicaia DOC

The Bolgheri estate where Mario Incisa della Rocchetta planted Bordeaux cuttings in 1944. First released as Sassicaia 1968; awarded its own Bolgheri Sassicaia DOC in 1994.

Tasting: Strictly limited visits; the estate prioritises trade and allocation customers. Public access is rare.

Signature wines: sassicaia, guidalberto, le-difese

Scores: Wine Advocate 100 (1985)

Localita Le Capanne 27, 57020 Bolgheri, Livorno, Italy → Book →

Tenuta dell'Ornellaia ★ 4.9

Bolgheri Superiore DOC and Toscana IGTCabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot, Sauvignon Blanc115 haFounded 1981Frescobaldi family (acquired full ownership 2005)Winemaker Marco Balsimelli (estate director); Olga Fusari, head winemakerICEABolgheri DOC

Bolgheri estate founded by Lodovico Antinori in 1981 and fully owned by Frescobaldi since 2005. Bordeaux-cloned reds with Masseto and Ornellaia as flagships; the estate is ICEA-organic certified.

Tasting: Cellar tours, vineyard walks and tutored tastings by appointment; the Vendemmia d'Artista label programme is on display at the cellar.

Signature wines: ornellaia, le-volte-dell-ornellaia, ornellaia-bianco, le-serre-nuove-dell-ornellaia

Scores: Wine Spectator 96 (1998), Wine Advocate 98 (2015)

Localita Ornellaia 191, 57022 Bolgheri, Livorno, Italy → Book →

Masseto ★ 5.0

Toscana IGTMerlot7 haFounded 1986Frescobaldi familyWinemaker Eleonora MarconiICEABolgheri DOC

A 7-hectare blue-clay Merlot parcel inside the Ornellaia estate. First released as a separate brand in 1986. Now operates with its own dedicated cellar opened in 2020; widely considered Italy's most prestigious Merlot.

Tasting: Visits to the dedicated Masseto cellar by very limited appointment; one of Italy's hardest-to-secure cellar tours.

Signature wines: masseto, massetino

Scores: Wine Advocate 98 (2001), Wine Advocate 100 (2015)

Localita Ornellaia 191, 57022 Bolgheri, Livorno, Italy → Book →

Tenuta Guado al Tasso ★ 4.8

Bolgheri Superiore DOC and Toscana IGTCabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot, Vermentino, Syrah320 haFounded 1990Marchesi Antinori (the Antinori family's Bolgheri estate, separate producer slug from Marchesi Antinori in Chianti Classico)Winemaker Renzo Cotarella (Antinori chief winemaker); Pierluigi Talamo, estate directorBolgheri DOC

Antinori's Bolgheri estate, established as a standalone vineyard in 1990 from the family's 1932 land purchase. 320 hectares; Guado al Tasso Superiore is flagship.

Tasting: Cellar visits, vineyard walks and the Osteria Magona village kitchen at Bolgheri; bookings via the Antinori hospitality desk.

Signature wines: guado-al-tasso, il-bruciato, scalabrone, matarocchio

Scores: Wine Advocate 97 (2015), Vinous 97 (2018)

Localita Belvedere 116, 57020 Bolgheri, Livorno, Italy → Book →

Le Macchiole ★ 4.8

Bolgheri DOC and Toscana IGTCabernet Franc, Merlot, Syrah, Sangiovese22 haFounded 1983Cinzia Merli and family (widow of co-founder Eugenio Campolmi)Winemaker Cinzia Merli with Luca d'Attoma consultingCCPBBolgheri DOC

Family estate founded 1983 by Eugenio Campolmi; Cinzia Merli has led the property since his death in 2002. Single-varietal Cabernet Franc (Paleo), Merlot (Messorio) and Syrah (Scrio) bottlings; CCPB-organic certified.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tutored tastings by appointment; vegan vinification practices used since 2014.

Signature wines: le-macchiole-paleo-rosso, le-macchiole-messorio, le-macchiole-scrio, le-macchiole-bolgheri-rosso

Scores: Wine Advocate 97 (2015), Vinous 96 (2019)

Via Bolgherese 189A, 57022 Bolgheri, Livorno, Italy → Book →

Grattamacco ★ 4.7

Bolgheri Superiore DOC and Toscana IGTCabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Sangiovese, Vermentino30 haFounded 1977Claudio Tipa (ColleMassari Group)Winemaker Luca Marrone with Maurizio Castelli consultingCCPBBolgheri DOC

The second estate to release a Bolgheri red after Sassicaia, founded 1977. Acquired by Claudio Tipa's ColleMassari group in 2002; CCPB-organic certified since 2014.

Tasting: Cellar visits, vineyard walks and tastings by appointment; certified organic since 2014.

Signature wines: grattamacco-bolgheri-superiore, grattamacco-bolgheri-rosso, grattamacco-grattamacco-bianco

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2019), Vinous 95 (2018)

Localita Lungagnano 129, 57022 Castagneto Carducci, Livorno, Italy → Book →

Michele Satta ★ 4.6

Bolgheri DOC and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, Teroldego, Vermentino, Viognier27 haFounded 1983Michele Satta and familyWinemaker Michele SattaBolgheri DOC

Bolgheri's enduring artisan: Michele Satta arrived in 1974 and bottled his first wine in 1983. Notable for keeping a 100% Sangiovese (Cavaliere) on the Bordeaux-dominated coast.

Tasting: Cellar tours and tasting flights by appointment; Michele Satta personally hosts on advance booking.

Signature wines: michele-satta-piastraia, michele-satta-i-castagni, michele-satta-cavaliere

Scores: Wine Advocate 95 (2018), Vinous 93 (2018)

Localita Casone Ugolino 23, 57022 Castagneto Carducci, Livorno, Italy → Book →

Tenuta di Capezzana ★ 4.7

Carmignano DOCG and Vin Santo di Carmignano DOCSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Canaiolo, Trebbiano Toscano, San Colombano96 haFounded 1920Contini Bonacossi familyWinemaker Benedetta Contini BonacossiICEACarmignano DOCG

Contini Bonacossi family estate at Carmignano with vineyard records back to 804 AD on the property. Wine production formalised in 1920; certified organic and a Carmignano DOCG benchmark.

Tasting: Cellar tours, Vin Santo vinsantaia visits, vineyard walks and on-site agriturismo accommodation; certified organic.

Signature wines: capezzana-villa-di-capezzana, capezzana-trefiano-riserva, capezzana-vin-santo-di-carmignano-riserva

Scores: Wine Advocate 93 (2018), Vinous 93 (2018)

Via Capezzana 100, 59015 Carmignano, Prato, Italy → Book →

Fattoria Le Pupille ★ 4.8

Morellino di Scansano DOCG, Maremma Toscana DOC and Toscana IGTSangiovese (Morellino), Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Petit Verdot, Alicante, Vermentino80 haFounded 1985Elisabetta Geppetti and familyWinemaker Luca d'Attoma (consulting) with Cosimo Cossutti, estate enologistMorellino di Scansano DOCG

Maremma estate built by Elisabetta Geppetti from her 1985 inheritance into the region's reference producer. Saffredi (Cabernet-Merlot-Alicante IGT) and Morellino anchor the range.

Tasting: Cellar tours, vineyard walks and tasting flights by reservation; Saffredi is the long-running Super Tuscan flagship.

Signature wines: le-pupille-saffredi, le-pupille-poggio-valente, le-pupille-morellino-di-scansano

Scores: Wine Advocate 97 (2019), Vinous 95 (2018)

Localita Piagge del Maiano 92A, 58051 Magliano in Toscana, Grosseto, Italy → Book →

Rocca di Frassinello ★ 4.6

Maremma Toscana DOC and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Vermentino90 haFounded 2005Castellare di Castellina (Paolo Panerai) in joint venture with Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite)Winemaker Carlo FerriniMaremma Toscana DOC

Joint venture between Castellare di Castellina and Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite), housed in a Renzo Piano-designed cellar at Gavorrano. Baffonero, a 100% Merlot, is the prestige bottling.

Tasting: Tours of the Renzo Piano-designed cellar, vineyard walks and tasting flights by reservation; the cellar's underground barrel hall is a destination in itself.

Signature wines: rocca-di-frassinello, rocca-di-frassinello-baffonero, rocca-di-frassinello-le-sughere

Scores: Wine Advocate 95 (2018), Vinous 94 (2018)

Localita Giuncarico, 58023 Gavorrano, Grosseto, Italy → Book →

Panizzi ★ 4.5

Vernaccia di San Gimignano DOCG, Chianti Colli Senesi DOCG and Toscana IGTVernaccia, Sangiovese, Trebbiano Toscano32 haFounded 1979Simone Niccolai (acquired from the Panizzi family in 2010)Winemaker Davide MazzolaVernaccia di San Gimignano DOCG

San Gimignano's leading Vernaccia specialist, founded 1979 by Giovanni Panizzi and acquired by Simone Niccolai in 2010. The Riserva ages in a mix of stainless steel, tonneau and acacia wood.

Tasting: Cellar tours, tasting flights and an on-site museum dedicated to Vernaccia; guided visits at the Santa Margherita property.

Signature wines: panizzi-vernaccia-di-san-gimignano, panizzi-riserva-vernaccia-di-san-gimignano, panizzi-vertigo-chianti-colli-senesi

Scores: Wine Advocate 93 (2022), Vinous 92 (2021)

Localita Santa Margherita 34, 53037 San Gimignano, Siena, Italy → Book →

Castello di Nipozzano ★ 4.7

Chianti Rufina DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Malvasia Nera250 haFounded 1000Frescobaldi family (Marchesi Frescobaldi)Winemaker Niccolo d'AfflittoChianti Rufina DOCG

Frescobaldi's Rufina estate, in the family since 1000 AD and on its current 250-hectare scale since the mid-19th century. Montesodi single-vineyard Sangiovese is the long-running flagship.

Tasting: Castle tours, cellar visits, vineyard tastings and lunch in the medieval estate by reservation; one of the Frescobaldi estate-visit anchors.

Signature wines: nipozzano-vecchie-viti, nipozzano-montesodi, nipozzano-chianti-rufina-riserva

Scores: Wine Advocate 95 (2019), Vinous 94 (2018)

Via di Nipozzano 22, 50060 Pelago, Firenze, Italy → Book →

Frescobaldi CastelGiocondo ★ 4.7

Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese Grosso, Merlot235 haFounded 1989Frescobaldi family (Marchesi Frescobaldi)Winemaker Niccolo d'AfflittoBrunello di Montalcino DOCG

Frescobaldi's Montalcino estate, acquired in 1989. 235 hectares of Sangiovese Grosso in the western reaches of the appellation; Ripe al Convento is the Riserva from oldest vines.

Tasting: Estate tours, Brunello cellar visits and tasting flights by reservation; Luce della Vite is bottled separately on a neighbouring property.

Signature wines: castelgiocondo-brunello-di-montalcino, castelgiocondo-ripe-al-convento-riserva, castelgiocondo-lamaione

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2016), Vinous 94 (2016)

Localita CastelGiocondo, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy → Book →

Frescobaldi Tenuta di Castiglioni ★ 4.5

Chianti Colli Fiorentini DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot110 haFounded 1100Frescobaldi family (Marchesi Frescobaldi)Winemaker Niccolo d'AfflittoChianti Colli Fiorentini DOCG

The Frescobaldi family's oldest property, in continuous family ownership since the 11th century. 110 hectares of vines in Montespertoli; Giramonte is the Bordeaux-blended IGT flagship.

Tasting: Cellar visits, vineyard walks and tasting flights at the Castiglioni manor; Tenuta di Castiglioni is the historical Frescobaldi family property.

Signature wines: castiglioni-giramonte, castiglioni-chianti, castiglioni-tenuta-di-castiglioni

Scores: Wine Advocate 95 (2019), Vinous 93 (2018)

Via di Castiglioni 4, 50025 Montespertoli, Firenze, Italy → Book →

Frescobaldi Tenuta di Pomino ★ 4.5

Pomino DOC and Toscana IGTPinot Nero, Chardonnay, Pinot Bianco, Sangiovese90 haFounded 1855Frescobaldi family (Marchesi Frescobaldi)Winemaker Niccolo d'AfflittoPomino DOC

High-elevation Frescobaldi estate at Pomino (550 to 700 metres) bordering Chianti Rufina. Pinot Nero and Chardonnay vineyards date to 1855; Benefizio Riserva is one of Tuscany's pioneering barrel-aged Chardonnays.

Tasting: Estate visits and tastings by reservation at the high-altitude Pomino property; the cellar specialises in white-grape work unusual for the region.

Signature wines: pomino-benefizio-riserva, pomino-bianco, pomino-pinot-nero

Scores: Wine Advocate 94 (2021), Vinous 93 (2020)

Via di Petrognano 19, 50060 Pomino, Pelago, Firenze, Italy → Book →

Fattoria Selvapiana ★ 4.8

Chianti Rufina DOCG and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Malvasia Nera60 haFounded 1827Federico Giuntini MassetiWinemaker Federico Giuntini Masseti with Franco Bernabei consultingICEAChianti Rufina DOCG

Family-owned Chianti Rufina estate since 1827, certified organic by ICEA. The Bucerchiale single-vineyard Riserva is among Tuscany's most consistently age-worthy Sangiovese under €40.

Tasting: Cellar visits, vineyard walks and tastings by appointment; the Bucerchiale single-vineyard is the long-running flagship.

Signature wines: selvapiana-bucerchiale, selvapiana-chianti-rufina, selvapiana-fornace

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2019), Vinous 95 (2019)

Via Selvapiana 43, 50068 Rufina, Firenze, Italy → Book →

Tua Rita ★ 4.8

Toscana IGT, Val di Cornia DOC and Suvereto DOCGMerlot, Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, Sangiovese32 haFounded 1984Stefano Frascolla and Simena BistiWinemaker Stefano Frascolla with Luca d'Attoma consultingSuvereto DOCG (Val di Cornia coastal area)

Suvereto estate founded 1984; Redigaffi, a 100% Merlot, scored 100 points from Wine Advocate for the 2000 vintage and put the Val di Cornia on the Super Tuscan map.

Tasting: Cellar tours, vineyard walks and tasting flights by appointment at Suvereto, just south of Bolgheri.

Signature wines: tua-rita-redigaffi, tua-rita-giusto-di-notri, tua-rita-perlato-del-bosco

Scores: Wine Advocate 100 (2000), Vinous 97 (2019)

Localita Notri 81, 57028 Suvereto, Livorno, Italy → Book →

Fattoria Le Fonti ★ 4.4

Chianti Classico DOCG and Vin Santo del Chianti Classico DOCSangiovese, Canaiolo Nero, Colorino, Malvasia Bianca Lunga, Trebbiano Toscano22 haFounded 1999Vicky Schmitt-VitaliWinemaker Vicky Schmitt-Vitali with Stefano Porcinai consultingICEAChianti Classico DOCG

Small Chianti Classico estate at Poggibonsi run by Vicky Schmitt-Vitali since 1999. Certified ICEA-organic; the Vin Santo del Chianti Classico is aged 10 years in caratelli.

Tasting: Small-group estate tastings and cellar visits by appointment; agriturismo rooms available; certified organic.

Signature wines: le-fonti-chianti-classico-riserva, le-fonti-vito-arturo, le-fonti-vin-santo

Scores: Vinous 92 (2019), Wine Advocate 91 (2019)

Localita Le Fonti 41, 53036 Poggibonsi, Siena, Italy → Book →

Barone Ricasoli Castello di Brolio ★ 4.8

Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione, Chianti Classico DOCG, and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Abrusco240 haFounded 1141Francesco Ricasoli (32nd generation of the Ricasoli family)Winemaker Massimiliano BiagiChianti Classico DOCG

1,200-hectare estate at Gaiole in Chianti with 240 hectares under vine. Bettino Ricasoli codified the modern Chianti formula here in 1872; the family has owned the castle since 1141 and produces Gran Selezione plus the Casalferro IGT.

Tasting: Cellar visits, guided tastings, wine shop, agriturismo, and the Osteria del Castello restaurant at the Madonna a Brolio property. Open daily 10:00 to 19:00.

Signature wines: castello-di-brolio-gran-selezione, colledila-gran-selezione, casalferro, brolio-bettino

Scores: Wine Advocate 96 (2019), James Suckling 96 (2019)

Localita Madonna a Brolio, 53013 Gaiole in Chianti, Siena, Italy → Book →

Montevertine ★ 4.9

Toscana IGT (estate withdrew from Chianti Classico DOCG consortium in 1981)Sangiovese, Canaiolo, Colorino18 haFounded 1967Manetti family (Martino Manetti, son of founder Sergio Manetti)Winemaker Martino Manetti with consulting from Giulio Gambelli historically and currently Paolo SalviToscana IGT (Radda in Chianti)

Sergio Manetti founded Montevertine at Radda in 1967 and walked out of the Chianti Classico consortium in 1981 to bottle pure Sangiovese as Toscana IGT. Le Pergole Torte (1977 first vintage) is the canonical pure-Sangiovese IGT.

Tasting: Cellar tour and tasting by appointment only; small-group visits hosted at the estate cellar at Radda in Chianti.

Signature wines: le-pergole-torte, montevertine-rosso, pian-del-ciampolo

Scores: Wine Advocate 97 (2019), Vinous 96 (2019)

Localita Montevertine, 53017 Radda in Chianti, Siena, Italy → Book →

Poggio di Sotto ★ 4.9

Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Rosso di Montalcino DOCSangiovese Grosso17 haFounded 1989ColleMassari Group (Maria Iris Bertarelli and Claudio Tipa), acquired 2011Winemaker Luca Marrone with Maurizio Castelli consultingICEABrunello di Montalcino DOCG (Castelnuovo dell'Abate)

Castelnuovo dell'Abate estate founded by Piero Palmucci in 1989, now part of the ColleMassari Group (acquired 2011). Long Slavonian-oak ageing and southeastern Montalcino fruit anchor a top-tier modern Brunello.

Tasting: Cellar visits and Brunello tastings by appointment only at the Castelnuovo dell'Abate estate.

Signature wines: poggio-di-sotto-brunello, poggio-di-sotto-brunello-riserva, poggio-di-sotto-rosso-di-montalcino

Scores: Wine Advocate 97 (2016), Vinous 96 (2016)

Localita Poggio di Sotto 1, Castelnuovo dell'Abate, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy → Book →

Marchesi Mazzei Castello di Fonterutoli ★ 4.8

Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione, Chianti Classico DOCG, and Toscana IGTSangiovese, Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, Malvasia Nera, Colorino117 haFounded 1435Mazzei family (brothers Filippo and Francesco Mazzei; Filippo's son Giovanni represents the 25th generation)Winemaker Carlo Ferrini consulting with the Mazzei familyChianti Classico DOCG (Castellina in Chianti)

Mazzei has owned Castello di Fonterutoli at Castellina since 1435. Today 117 hectares of vineyards across 120 parcels, with the Castello Fonterutoli Gran Selezione, the Sangiovese-Merlot Siepi IGT, and the 1981-launched Concerto.

Tasting: Estate tour and tasting at the Fonterutoli hamlet, plus the Osteria di Fonterutoli restaurant; booking via mazzei.it. The cellar at Castellina is open by appointment.

Signature wines: castello-fonterutoli-gran-selezione, siepi, concerto-di-fonterutoli, poggio-badiola

Scores: Wine Advocate 97 (2019), James Suckling 97 (2019)

Via Ottone di Sassonia III 5, Localita Fonterutoli, 53011 Castellina in Chianti, Siena, Italy → Book →

Petrolo ★ 4.8

Val d'Arno di Sopra DOC and Toscana IGTMerlot, Sangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, Trebbiano31 haFounded 1947Sanjust family (Lucia Bazzocchi Sanjust and Luca Sanjust)Winemaker Luca SanjustBiodynamic PracticingICEAVal d'Arno di Sopra DOC (Mercatale Valdarno)

31-hectare estate in upper Val d'Arno owned by the Sanjust family since 1947. The Galatrona vineyard produces a 100 percent Merlot widely cited among Italy's top Merlots; Torrione and Boggina cover Sangiovese under the Val d'Arno di Sopra DOC.

Tasting: Estate tasting and cellar visits by appointment; multiple agriturismo properties (La Villa, Boggina, La Vigna, La Costa, Feriale) on the estate.

Signature wines: galatrona, boggina-c, torrione

Scores: Wine Advocate 97 (2019), James Suckling 97 (2019)

Localita Petrolo 30, Mercatale Valdarno, 52021 Bucine, Arezzo, Italy → Book →

Azienda Agricola Cesani ★ 4.5

Vernaccia di San Gimignano DOCG, Chianti Colli Senesi DOCG, San Gimignano Rosso DOC, and Toscana IGTVernaccia, Sangiovese, Ciliegiolo, Merlot22 haFounded 1951Cesani family (Letizia, Marialuisa, and Sara Cesani)Winemaker Letizia CesaniICEAVernaccia di San Gimignano DOCG (Pancole)

Cesani family estate at Pancole near San Gimignano, founded 1951 when Guido and Annunziata Cesani acquired the property. Long-time organic producer of Vernaccia di San Gimignano DOCG plus Chianti Colli Senesi and Toscana IGT reds.

Tasting: Estate tastings, vineyard walks, and agriturismo at Pancole 6 km from San Gimignano; booking via the estate site.

Signature wines: cesani-vernaccia-di-san-gimignano, cesani-clamys-vernaccia, cesani-luenzo, cesani-chianti-colli-senesi

Scores: Wine Advocate 92 (2021), Vinous 91 (2021)

Localita Pancole 82/D, 53037 San Gimignano, Siena, Italy → Book →

Still wine in Veneto

Giuseppe Quintarelli ★ 5.0

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Corvinone, Rondinella, Molinara, Oseleta, Cabernet Sauvignon, NebbioloFounded 1924Quintarelli family (Fiorenza Quintarelli and family)Winemaker Quintarelli family teamValpolicella Classico

The legendary Negrar estate founded in 1924, now run by Giuseppe's daughter Fiorenza. Quintarelli's Amarone and Recioto are benchmark expressions of dried-grape Valpolicella winemaking, aged for years before release.

Tasting: By appointment only; tastings at the historic cellar in Negrar. Very limited availability.

Signature wines: quintarelli-amarone-classico, quintarelli-alzero, quintarelli-recioto-classico

Via Cerè 1, 37020 Negrar di Valpolicella, Verona, Italy → Book →

Dal Forno Romano ★ 5.0

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Corvinone, Oseleta, MolinaraFounded 1983Romano Dal Forno and familyWinemaker Romano Dal Forno and Marco Dal FornoValpolicella

Founded by Romano Dal Forno in 1983 in the Illasi valley, outside the Classico zone. Tiny production, extremely low yields and very long maceration produce arguably the most powerful Amarone in the region.

Tasting: By appointment only; private estate visit at Cellore d'Illasi.

Signature wines: dal-forno-amarone-valpolicella, dal-forno-valpolicella-superiore

Via Lodoletta 1, 37030 Cellore d'Illasi, Verona, Italy → Book →

Allegrini ★ 4.8

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Corvinone, Rondinella, OseletaFounded 1854Allegrini family (Marilisa Allegrini, president)Winemaker Allegrini winemaking teamValpolicella Classico

Multi-generation family estate at Fumane at the heart of Valpolicella Classico. Allegrini pioneered single-vineyard bottlings in the zone and is widely credited with modernising Amarone's international profile in the 1980s under Giovanni Allegrini.
