Tasting: Tasting visits at the Fumane estate and Villa della Torre; bookable via the estate website.

Signature wines: allegrini-amarone-classico, allegrini-la-poja, allegrini-palazzo-della-torre

Via Giare 9-11, 37022 Fumane, Verona, Italy → Book →

Masi Agricola ★ 4.6

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Rondinella, Molinara, OseletaFounded 1772Boscaini familyWinemaker Andrea Dal CinValpolicella Classico

One of Valpolicella's oldest and most recognisable names, founded in 1772. The Boscaini family pioneered the ripasso technique. The historic Serego Alighieri property owned by Dante Alighieri's descendants is part of the Masi portfolio.

Tasting: Estate visits and tastings at Serego Alighieri guest property and the Gargagnago winery.

Signature wines: masi-costasera-amarone, masi-campolongo-di-torbe, masi-campofiorin

Via Monteleone, 37015 Gargagnago di Sant'Ambrogio di Valpolicella, Verona, Italy → Book →

Bertani ★ 4.5

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Rondinella, MolinaraFounded 1857Tenimenti Angelini (Angelini Wines and Estates)Winemaker Bertani winemaking teamValpolicella Classico

A founding force in Valpolicella winemaking since 1857, Bertani codified the long-maceration Amarone style. Owned by Tenimenti Angelini since 2021 and still operating from the historic Novare cellars.

Tasting: Cellar tours and tastings bookable at the Novare estate; group and trade visits by appointment.

Signature wines: bertani-amarone-classico, bertani-valpolicella-ripasso

Loc. Novare, 37024 Negrar di Valpolicella, Verona, Italy → Book →

Tommasi Viticoltori ★ 4.5

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Corvinone, Rondinella, MolinaraFounded 1902Tommasi familyWinemaker Giancarlo TommasiValpolicella Classico

Family estate in San Pietro in Cariano producing Amarone, Valpolicella and Ripasso across multiple vineyard parcels in the Classico zone since 1902. Fourth-generation winemaker Giancarlo oversees production.

Tasting: Wine tasting and cellar visits at the San Pietro in Cariano estate; bookable online.

Signature wines: tommasi-amarone-classico, tommasi-valpolicella-ripasso, tommasi-amarone-ca-florian

Via Ronchetto 2, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano, Verona, Italy → Book →

Tedeschi Vini ★ 4.7

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Corvinone, RondinellaFounded 1824Tedeschi familyWinemaker Lorenzo TedeschiValpolicella Classico

Founded in 1824, Tedeschi is one of the oldest family producers in Valpolicella Classico. The single-vineyard Amarone Capitel Monte Olmi is its flagship; sixth-generation Lorenzo Tedeschi manages the estate.

Tasting: Visits and tastings by appointment; group tours of the historic cellar available.

Signature wines: tedeschi-amarone-capitel-monte-olmi, tedeschi-valpolicella-capitel-dei-nicalo

Via G. Verdi 4, 37020 San Pietro in Cariano, Verona, Italy → Book →

Tenuta Sant'Antonio ★ 4.6

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Corvinone, Rondinella, OseletaFounded 1989Castagnedi familyWinemaker Armando CastagnediValpolicella

The Castagnedi brothers established Tenuta Sant'Antonio in 1989 in the Mezzane valley east of the Classico zone. Their Campo dei Gigli Amarone from Oseleta and Corvina is a benchmark for the sub-zone style.

Tasting: Estate tasting visits by appointment at Mezzane di Sotto; full range of Valpolicella and Amarone poured.

Signature wines: tenuta-santantonio-amarone-campo-dei-gigli, tenuta-santantonio-valpolicella-superiore

Via Ceriani 23, 37030 Mezzane di Sotto, Verona, Italy → Book →

Zyme ★ 4.7

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCG and Veronese IGTCorvina, Corvinone, Oseleta, Rondinella, Cabernet SauvignonFounded 2001Celestino GaspariWinemaker Celestino GaspariValpolicella Classico

Founded in 2001 by former Quintarelli cellarmaster Celestino Gaspari. Zyme spans tradition and innovation: the Amarone Kairos uses classic drying methods, while the Harlequin IGT blends 15 native and international varieties.

Tasting: Private tastings by appointment at the San Pietro in Cariano cellar.

Signature wines: zyme-amarone-kairos, zyme-harlequin-veronese-igt

Via Ca' del Pipa 1, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano, Verona, Italy → Book →

Ca' La Bionda ★ 4.5

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Corvinone, Rondinella, MolinaraFounded 1902Castellani familyWinemaker Alessandro CastellaniValpolicella Classico

Family-run estate at the top of the Marano valley producing structured, traditional Amarone and Valpolicella from old vineyards on volcanic soils.

Tasting: Visits and tastings by appointment on the Marano hillside estate.

Signature wines: ca-la-bionda-amarone-classico, ca-la-bionda-valpolicella-classico

Via Bionda 4, 37020 Marano di Valpolicella, Verona, Italy → Book →

Corte Sant'Alda ★ 4.8

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Rondinella, MolinaraFounded 1986Marinella CameraniWinemaker Marinella CameraniDemeter CertifiedECOCERTNaturalValpolicella

Marinella Camerani's biodynamic estate in the Mezzane valley has been Demeter-certified since 2003. The Mithas Amarone and Soraigo Valpolicella are made with minimal intervention and long ageing in large oak.

Tasting: Estate visits and tastings by appointment at Mezzane di Sotto; biodynamic vineyard tours available.

Signature wines: corte-santalda-amarone-mithas, corte-santalda-soraigo

Via Capovilla 28, 37030 Mezzane di Sotto, Verona, Italy → Book →

Pieropan ★ 4.9

Soave Superiore DOCG and Soave Classico DOCGarganega, Trebbiano di SoaveFounded 1880Pieropan family (Andrea and Dario Pieropan)Winemaker Andrea and Dario PieropanORGANIC CERTIFIEDSoave Classico

The reference producer for Soave since 1880, Pieropan produces single-vineyard bottlings La Rocca (volcanic tufa) and Calvarino (basalt and limestone) that define what world-class Garganega can achieve. Certified organic by Suolo e Salute since 2012.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at the historic Soave cellar; bookable via the website.

Signature wines: pieropan-soave-classico, pieropan-la-rocca, pieropan-calvarino

Via Camuzzoni 3, 37038 Soave, Verona, Italy → Book →

Inama Azienda Agricola ★ 4.7

Soave Superiore DOCG and Colli Berici DOCGarganega, Sauvignon Blanc, CarmenereFounded 1991Inama family (Giuseppe Inama and sons)Winemaker Matteo InamaSoave Classico

Founded by Giuseppe Inama in 1991, the estate spans Soave Classico hill vineyards and pioneered quality Carmenere in the Colli Berici DOC. The Vulcaia Soave from basalt soils is one of the appellation's most mineral expressions.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at San Bonifacio; Soave Classico and Colli Berici Carmenere tasting programmes.

Signature wines: inama-soave-classico-foscarino, inama-oratorio-san-lorenzo, inama-bradisismo

Via IV Novembre 29, 37047 San Bonifacio, Verona, Italy → Book →

Gini ★ 4.7

Soave Superiore DOCGGarganegaFounded 1600Gini family (Sandro and Claudio Gini)Winemaker Sandro and Claudio GiniSoave Classico

The Gini family has farmed on the volcanic hillsides of Monteforte d'Alpone since the 17th century. La Frosca and Col Foscarin are their benchmark single-vineyard Soaves from ancient Garganega vines on basalt-rich soils.

Tasting: Estate visits and tasting flights at Monteforte d'Alpone; booking required.

Signature wines: gini-soave-classico-la-frosca, gini-col-foscarin

Via Matteotti 42, 37032 Monteforte d'Alpone, Verona, Italy → Book →

Suavia ★ 4.7

Soave Superiore DOCGGarganega, Trebbiano di SoaveFounded 1982Tessari family (Alessandra and Meri Tessari)Winemaker Alessandra TessariSoave Classico

Sisters Alessandra and Meri Tessari run this compact estate in the village of Soave producing Classico and the single-cru Monte Carbonare from centenarian Garganega vines on volcanic tufa soils.

Tasting: Tasting visits by appointment in the village of Soave.

Signature wines: suavia-soave-classico, suavia-monte-carbonare, suavia-amedeo

Via Centro 14, 37038 Soave, Verona, Italy → Book →

Pra ★ 4.7

Soave Superiore DOCGGarganegaFounded 1983Graziano PraWinemaker Graziano PraORGANIC CERTIFIEDSoave Classico

Graziano Pra converted this Monteforte d'Alpone estate to organic viticulture and produces deeply terroir-driven Soave Classico Monte Grande from volcanic basalt and garnet-clay soils alongside the richer Otto bottling.

Tasting: Cellar visit and tasting at Monteforte d'Alpone by appointment.

Signature wines: pra-soave-classico-monte-grande, pra-otto

Via della Fontana 31, 37032 Monteforte d'Alpone, Verona, Italy → Book →

Cantina di Soave ★ 4.2

Soave Classico DOCGarganega, Trebbiano di Soave, Corvina, RondinellaFounded 1898Cooperative of 2,600 member growersWinemaker Cantina di Soave winemaking teamSoave Classico

The largest Soave cooperative, founded in 1898, representing 2,600 member growers across 6,000 hectares. Its premium Rocca Sveva line is entry-level access to the appellation; the visitor centre in town is walk-in friendly.

Tasting: Public tasting room open daily in Soave town; walk-in welcome.

Signature wines: cantina-soave-rocca-sveva

Via Vittorio Emanuele II 94, 37038 Soave, Verona, Italy → Book →

Roberto Anselmi ★ 4.8

Veneto IGTGarganega, ChardonnayFounded 1948Roberto AnselmiWinemaker Roberto AnselmiSoave Classico

Roberto Anselmi famously resigned from the Soave Consorzio and declassified his wines to Veneto IGT as a protest against commercial dilution of the appellation. His San Vincenzo, Capitel Croce and I Capitelli (sweet) remain defining Garganega benchmarks.

Tasting: Tasting visits at Monteforte d'Alpone by appointment.

Signature wines: anselmi-san-vincenzo, anselmi-capitel-croce, anselmi-i-capitelli

Via San Carlo 46, 37032 Monteforte d'Alpone, Verona, Italy → Book →

Le Salette ★ 4.6

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Corvinone, RondinellaFounded 1986Franco ScamperleWinemaker Monica ScamperleValpolicella Classico

A high-altitude Fumane estate farmed by the Scamperle family since 1986 producing Amarone Pergole Vece from century-old Corvina vines at 450 metres elevation. Monica Scamperle is one of Valpolicella's few female winemakers.

Tasting: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment in Fumane.

Signature wines: le-salette-amarone-pergole-vece, le-salette-valpolicella-classico

Via Pio Brugnoli 11, 37022 Fumane, Verona, Italy → Book →

Valentina Cubi ★ 4.5

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Corvinone, Rondinella, OseletaFounded 1969Valentina CubiWinemaker Valentina CubiORGANIC CERTIFIEDValpolicella Classico

Valentina Cubi's certified organic estate on the Fumane hillsides produces small-production Amarone and Valpolicella with a focus on indigenous varieties and minimal intervention in both vineyard and cellar.

Tasting: Visits and tastings by appointment at the Fumane estate.

Signature wines: valentina-cubi-amarone-morar, valentina-cubi-valpolicella-classico

Via Casterne 60, 37022 Fumane, Verona, Italy → Book →

Viviani ★ 4.7

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Rondinella, MolinaraFounded 1993Viviani familyWinemaker Claudio VivianiValpolicella Classico

Claudio Viviani farms old Corvina and Rondinella vines on the Mazzano ridge in Negrar, producing a tiny-production Amarone Campo Morar from the estate's highest plots with notable freshness and longevity.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at the Mazzano estate by appointment.

Signature wines: viviani-amarone-campo-morar, viviani-valpolicella-classico

Via Mazzano 8, 37020 Mazzano di Negrar, Verona, Italy → Book →

Zenato ★ 4.6

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCG and Lugana DOCCorvina, Rondinella, Oseleta, TurbianaFounded 1960Zenato family (Alberto and Nadia Zenato)Winemaker Alberto ZenatoLugana

Founded by Sergio Zenato in 1960, this estate bridges two zones: the Valpolicella hillside estates east of Negrar produce Amarone, and the Lake Garda shoreline vineyards at Peschiera yield benchmark Lugana from Turbiana.

Tasting: Tasting visits at Peschiera del Garda; both Valpolicella and Lugana wines poured.

Signature wines: zenato-amarone-classico, zenato-valpolicella-ripassa

Via San Benedetto 8, 37019 Peschiera del Garda, Verona, Italy → Book →

Speri Viticoltori ★ 4.7

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Corvinone, Rondinella, MolinaraFounded 1874Speri familyWinemaker Luca SperiValpolicella Classico

Six-generation family estate on the Monte Masua and Sant'Urbano hillsides of Fumane. Among the most traditional producers in the Classico zone; Luca Speri is the current winemaker.

Tasting: Cellar visits and seated tastings by appointment at San Pietro in Cariano.

Signature wines: speri-amarone-classico, speri-amarone-monte-sant-urbano

Via Fontana 14, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano, Verona, Italy → Book →

Santa Sofia ★ 4.4

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Rondinella, CorvinoneFounded 1811Begnoni familyWinemaker Begnoni familyValpolicella Classico

Operating from the 16th-century Villa Santa Sofia in Pedemonte, this producer has been bottling Valpolicella wines since 1811. The Begnoni family combination of history and good-value Amarone attracts steady wine tourism.

Tasting: Villa Santa Sofia open for visits and tastings; the historic loggia attributed to Palladio is part of the tour.

Signature wines: santa-sofia-amarone-classico, santa-sofia-valpolicella-classico

Via Ca' Dede 61, 37020 Pedemonte di San Pietro in Cariano, Verona, Italy → Book →

Lorenzo Begali ★ 4.7

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Corvinone, RondinellaFounded 1986Begali family (Lorenzo and Giordana Begali)Winemaker Lorenzo BegaliValpolicella Classico

Small family estate at San Pietro in Cariano producing Amarone and Recioto from very old Corvina vines on the Ca'Bianca cru. Consistently high quality with a traditional approach to drying and long cellaring.

Tasting: Tastings and cellar tours by appointment; intimate family atmosphere.

Signature wines: begali-amarone-monte-ca-bianca, begali-recioto-classico

Via Cengia 10, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano, Verona, Italy → Book →

Nicolis Angelo e Figli ★ 4.5

Amarone della Valpolicella DOCGCorvina, Corvinone, Rondinella, MolinaraFounded 1951Nicolis familyWinemaker Nicolis familyValpolicella Classico

The Nicolis family has farmed the Villa Girardi estate in San Pietro in Cariano since 1951. The Ambrosan single-vineyard Amarone from the oldest family vines is their prestige bottling.

Tasting: Wine visits and tastings at San Pietro in Cariano by appointment.

Signature wines: nicolis-amarone-classico, nicolis-ambrosan-amarone

Via Villa Girardi 29, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano, Verona, Italy → Book →

Serafini and Vidotto ★ 4.6

Montello Rosso DOCGCabernet Franc, Merlot, Cabernet SauvignonFounded 1986Francesco Serafini and Antonello VidottoWinemaker Antonello VidottoORGANIC CERTIFIEDMontello Rosso

Francesco Serafini and Antonello Vidotto established this Treviso estate in 1986, pioneering quality Bordeaux-variety production on the Montello hills. Il Rosso dell'Abazia is their flagship DOCG red.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at Nervesa della Battaglia by appointment.

Signature wines: serafini-vidotto-il-rosso-dell-abazia, serafini-vidotto-phigaia

Via Ca' di Serra 2, 31044 Nervesa della Battaglia, Treviso, Italy → Book →

Guerrieri Rizzardi ★ 4.6

Bardolino Superiore DOCGCorvina, Rondinella, MolinaraFounded 1649Guerrieri Rizzardi familyWinemaker Guerrieri Rizzardi winemaking teamBardolino

One of the oldest estates in Bardolino, with records dating to 1649, Guerrieri Rizzardi operates vineyards on both Lake Garda shores and in Valpolicella. The on-site Museo del Vino is one of Italy's leading wine museums.

Tasting: Wine museum Museo del Vino di Bardolino on-site; open daily including tasting flight.

Signature wines: guerrieri-rizzardi-bardolino-classico, guerrieri-rizzardi-tacchetto-chiaretto

Via Verdi 4, 37011 Bardolino, Verona, Italy → Book →

Cavalchina ★ 4.4

Bianco di Custoza DOCTrebbiano Toscano, Garganega, Cortese, Bianca Fernanda, Riesling ItalicoFounded 1974Piona familyWinemaker Piona familyCustoza

The Piona family estate at Sommacampagna is one of the benchmarks for Bianco di Custoza DOC, producing a crisp blend of Trebbiano, Garganega and Cortese on the morainic hills south of Lake Garda.

Tasting: Tasting visits at the Sommacampagna estate by appointment.

Signature wines: cavalchina-bianco-di-custoza, cavalchina-amedeo

Loc. Cavalchina 7, 37066 Sommacampagna, Verona, Italy → Book →

Maculan ★ 4.8

Breganze DOCVespaiola, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, ChardonnayFounded 1947Maculan family (Fausto Maculan)Winemaker Fausto MaculanBreganze

The defining estate of Breganze DOC in Vicenza, founded by the Maculan family in 1947. Fausto Maculan's Torcolato dessert wine from dried Vespaiola grapes and the Bordeaux-blend Fratta are the zone's most celebrated bottles.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at the Breganze cellar; Torcolato dessert wine and Fratta flagship poured.

Signature wines: maculan-torcolato, maculan-fratta, maculan-acininobili

Via Castelletto 3, 36042 Breganze, Vicenza, Italy → Book →

Vignalta ★ 4.5

Colli Euganei Fior d'Arancio DOCGMoscato Giallo, Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Pinot BiancoFounded 1980Vignalta familyWinemaker Vignalta winemaking teamColli Euganei

Situated in the medieval village of Arqua Petrarca on the Euganean Hills, Vignalta is a benchmark producer for the Colli Euganei Fior d'Arancio DOCG passito from Moscato Giallo and the red Gemola IGT.

Tasting: Visits and tasting at Arqua Petrarca; the Fior d'Arancio DOCG and Colli Euganei reds are the focus.

Signature wines: vignalta-fior-darancio-passito, vignalta-gemola

Via dei Vescovi 5, 35030 Arqua Petrarca, Padova, Italy → Book →

Italo Cescon ★ 4.4

Piave DOCRaboso Piave, Merlot, Pinot Grigio, ChardonnayFounded 1960Cescon familyWinemaker Cescon familyPiave

A leading producer of Piave DOC wines including the rare Raboso Piave, the zone's autochthonous red grape. The Cescon family has championed Raboso's rehabilitation in the Venetian plains since the 1960s.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at Ormelle by appointment; Raboso Piave and varietal range poured.

Signature wines: italo-cescon-raboso-piave, italo-cescon-il-tralcetto-pinot-grigio

Piazza dei Caduti 3, 31024 Ormelle, Treviso, Italy → Book →

Gorgo ★ 4.4

Bianco di Custoza DOCGarganega, Trebbiano Toscano, Cortese, MerlotFounded 1966Custoza

A family estate on the glacial morainic amphitheater south of Lake Garda at Custoza. The estate specialises in crisp, food-friendly Bianco di Custoza DOC from Garganega, Trebbiano and Cortese.

Tasting: Tasting visits at the morainic estate in Custoza by appointment.

Signature wines: gorgo-bianco-di-custoza, gorgo-merlot-garda

Via Gorgo 17, 37067 Custoza di Valeggio sul Mincio, Verona, Italy → Book →

Ottella ★ 4.6

Lugana DOCTurbianaFounded 1980Montresor familyWinemaker Montresor familyLugana

Montresor family estate at Peschiera del Garda specialising exclusively in Lugana DOC from the Turbiana grape. The Molceo Riserva aged on lees for over a year is among the most complex Lugana bottlings.

Tasting: Tasting visits at the Peschiera del Garda estate; Lugana DOC range.

Signature wines: ottella-lugana, ottella-molceo-lugana-riserva

Loc. Ottella, 37019 Peschiera del Garda, Verona, Italy → Book →

Corte Gardoni ★ 4.4

Bianco di Custoza DOCGarganega, Trebbiano Toscano, Cortese, Cabernet Sauvignon, MerlotFounded 1980Piccoli familyWinemaker Piccoli familyCustoza

The Piccoli family estate at Valeggio sul Mincio on the morainic rim of Lake Garda produces a fresh, saline Bianco di Custoza DOC alongside an interesting Garda Cabernet from south-shore hillside vineyards.

Tasting: Tasting visits at the Valeggio sul Mincio estate; bookable online.

Signature wines: corte-gardoni-bianco-di-custoza, corte-gardoni-le-fontane-garda-cabernet

Loc. Gardoni 5, 37067 Valeggio sul Mincio, Verona, Italy → Book →

Cantina di Custoza ★ 4.1

Bianco di Custoza DOCGarganega, Trebbiano Toscano, CorteseFounded 1958Cooperative of local growersWinemaker Cooperative winemaking teamCustoza

The Custoza cooperative founded in 1958 is the largest producer in the Bianco di Custoza DOC appellation. High-volume accessible wines from morainic soils at good prices; the walk-in shop is a tourist draw on the Garda circuit.

Tasting: Public shop and tasting room at Custoza; walk-in welcome.

Signature wines: cantina-custoza-bianco-di-custoza

Via Sommacampagna 3, 37060 Custoza di Sommacampagna, Verona, Italy → Book →

Cantine Campagnola ★ 4.3

Bardolino Superiore DOCGCorvina, Rondinella, Molinara, GarganegaFounded 1889Campagnola familyWinemaker Campagnola familyBardolino

Founded in 1889 at the Lake Garda waterfront in Bardolino, Cantine Campagnola is one of the appellation's oldest estates producing light, food-friendly Bardolino Superiore DOCG and a range of Valpolicella.

Tasting: Visits and tastings at the Bardolino cellar; daily opening.

Signature wines: campagnola-bardolino-classico, campagnola-valpolicella-classico

Via Agnella 9, 37011 Bardolino, Verona, Italy → Book →

Monte Tondo ★ 4.4

Soave Superiore DOCGGarganega, Trebbiano di SoaveFounded 1969Magnabosco familyWinemaker Magnabosco familySoave Classico

The Magnabosco family's estate on the volcanic hillsides east of Soave Classico produces well-structured Soave Superiore DOCG from the Casette Foscarin vineyard on high-elevation basalt terraces.

Tasting: Tasting visits at the Soave estate by appointment.

Signature wines: monte-tondo-casette-foscarin

Via San Lorenzo 89, 37038 Soave, Verona, Italy → Book →

Fasoli Gino ★ 4.5

Soave Classico DOCGarganega, Trebbiano di Soave, CorvinaFounded 1924Fasoli familyWinemaker Fasoli familyICEASoave Classico

ICEA-certified organic estate at Colognola ai Colli producing Soave Classico and Valpolicella from certified-organic vineyards. A pioneer of organic viticulture in the Soave zone.

Tasting: Organic estate visits and tastings by appointment at Colognola ai Colli.

Signature wines: fasoli-gino-soave-classico-mael

Via C. Battisti 47, 37030 Colognola ai Colli, Verona, Italy → Book →

Still wine in Wachau

Weingut F.X. Pichler ★ 5.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Gelber Muskateller20 haFounded 1898Lucas Franz Pichler and Johanna Elisabeth PichlerWinemaker Lucas Franz PichlerBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDLoiben

Seven-generation Pichler estate in Oberloiben producing Smaragd Grüner Veltliner and Riesling from the Kellerberg and Loibenberg sites. 20 hectares planted half Grüner Veltliner, half Riesling.

Tasting: By appointment only; cellar visits and tastings for trade and press.

Signature wines: fx-pichler-kellerberg-riesling-smaragd, fx-pichler-loibenberg-gruner-smaragd, fx-pichler-unendlich

Tip: The Kellerberg and Unendlich Rieslings require several years of bottle age to fully open.

Oberloiben 27, 3601 Dürnstein, Austria → Book →

Weingut Emmerich Knoll ★ 4.9

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Gelber Muskateller19 haFounded 1825Unterloiben

Family estate since 1825 at Unterloiben, working Schütt, Loibenberg, Pfaffenberg and Kreutles sites. Celebrated for age-worthy Riesling and Grüner Veltliner Smaragd bottlings.

Tasting: Direct sales from the estate (Ab Hof); by appointment.

Signature wines: knoll-schütt-riesling-smaragd, knoll-loibenberg-gruner-smaragd, knoll-pfaffenberg-riesling-smaragd

Tip: Pfaffenberg Riesling Smaragd develops extraordinary mineral complexity with a decade of ageing.

Unterloiben 132, 3601 Dürnstein, Austria →

Nikolaihof Wachau ★ 4.9

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Neuburger18 haFounded 1971Familie SaahsDemeter CertifiedDEMETERNaturalMautern

Oldest documented winery in Austria (nearly 2,000 years of history) at Mautern on the south bank. Demeter biodynamic since 1971. Famous for the Vom Stein Riesling Smaragd and long-aged Hefeabzug Grüner Veltliner.

Tasting: Wine shop and tasting room at the estate; guided cellar tours available on request. Salon 77 restaurant on site.

Signature wines: nikolaihof-vom-stein-riesling-smaragd, nikolaihof-hefeabzug-gruner-smaragd, nikolaihof-vinothek-riesling

Tip: The Vinothek releases are barrel-aged wines held 10+ years before release. The estate restaurant is open seasonally.

Nikolaigasse 3, 3512 Mautern an der Donau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Alzinger ★ 4.8

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling9 haFounded 1975Unterloiben

Small-scale Unterloiben estate working the Steinertal, Loibenberg and Höhereck sites. Leo Alzinger pioneered a precision style in the 1980s; Katharina Alzinger-Kittel now continues the tradition.

Tasting: By appointment; direct sales from the estate.

Signature wines: alzinger-steinertal-riesling-smaragd, alzinger-loibenberg-gruner-smaragd, alzinger-hollerin-riesling-smaragd

Tip: Steinertal Riesling Smaragd is one of the Wachau's most reliable expressions of primary-rock minerality.

Unterloiben 11, 3601 Dürnstein, Austria → Book →

Weingut Tegernseerhof ★ 4.8

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling16 haFounded 1176Familie MittelbachWinemaker Martin MittelbachUnterloiben

Historic estate dating to 1176, when Holy Roman Emperor Henry II granted the Benedictine monastery of Tegernsee vineyard land in the Wachau. Fifth-generation Martin Mittelbach runs the estate from Unterloiben.

Tasting: Open Wednesdays from 13:00-18:00 (April onwards); tastings in the historic cellars.

Signature wines: tegernseerhof-bergdistel-loibenberg-gruner-smaragd, tegernseerhof-steinertal-riesling-smaragd, tegernseerhof-durnstein-federspiel

Tip: The Bergdistel Loibenberg Smaragd is the estate signature; the Dürnstein Federspiel is an accessible entry to the Tegernseerhof style.

Unterloiben 12, 3601 Dürnstein, Austria → Book →

Domäne Wachau ★ 4.6

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling440 haFounded 1938ORGANIC CERTIFIEDDürnstein

Wachau's largest estate, representing around 250 vintner families across 440 hectares. Founded 1938 as Winzergenossenschaft Wachau, renamed Domäne Wachau in 2008. Roman Horvath MW oversees the range alongside Heinz Frischengruber.

Tasting: Vinothek in Dürnstein open to visitors; tastings and direct sales. The Kellerschlössel Baroque cellar palace hosts tours.

Signature wines: domaene-wachau-achleiten-gruner-smaragd, domaene-wachau-kellerberg-riesling-smaragd, domaene-wachau-terrasen-federspiel-gruner

Tip: The Achleiten and Kellerberg Smaragd bottlings compete directly with the great single-estate wines at a more accessible price.

Dürnstein 107, 3601 Dürnstein, Austria → Book →

Weingut Prager ★ 4.9

Wachau DACRiesling, Grüner Veltliner18 haFounded 1715Familie BodensteinWinemaker Toni BodensteinWeissenkirchen

Bodenstein-run estate in Weissenkirchen with 18 hectares split across Achleiten, Klaus, Steinriegl, Hinter der Burg and Hollerin sites. The Wachstum Bodenstein series bottles selected Achleiten parcels as Smaragd.

Tasting: By appointment only; the cellar is their home (no regular hours).

Signature wines: prager-wachstum-bodenstein-riesling-smaragd, prager-wachstum-bodenstein-gruner-smaragd, prager-achleiten-riesling-smaragd

Tip: Wachstum Bodenstein Grüner Veltliner rewards patience; open 8-10 years from vintage for full complexity.

Wachaustrasse 48, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Schmelz ★ 4.4

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling10 haFounded 1870Joching

150-year-old family estate at Joching producing Höhereck Riesling Smaragd from the prized Dürnstein terraces. A well-regarded Vinea Wachau member with 10 hectares under vine.

Tasting: Direct sales; Buschenschank (seasonal wine tavern) at the estate.

Signature wines: schmelz-hoehereck-riesling-smaragd, schmelz-hochriesling-gruner-smaragd

Tip: Höhereck Riesling Smaragd is the estate's calling card; the Buschenschank offers seasonal tastings.

Weinbergstraße 14, 3610 Joching, Austria → Book →

Weingut Josef Jamek ★ 4.5

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling25 haFounded 1912Familie Jamek-MaletaJoching

Joching estate founded in 1912. Josef Jamek was a pioneer of dry, varietal Wachau wines in the 1960s. The estate restaurant remains open on the Danube. Julia Jamek and Herwig Maleta run it today.

Tasting: Restaurant at the estate serving regional cuisine; wine flights available.

Signature wines: jamek-ried-Klaus-riesling-smaragd, jamek-hochrain-gruner-smaragd, jamek-ried-steinvordern-riesling-smaragd

Tip: Try the Klaus Riesling Smaragd from steep Weissenkirchen terraces alongside the restaurant pairing lunch.

Josef-Jamek-Straße 45, 3610 Joching, Austria → Book →

Weingut Holzapfel Prandtauerhof ★ 4.5

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling14 haFounded 1702Familie HolzapfelJoching

Estate housed in the Prandtauerhof, a Baroque jewel designed by Jakob Prandtauer. 14 hectares with traditional viticulture methods; boutique hotel and acclaimed restaurant on site.

Tasting: Boutique hotel and restaurant at Prandtauerhof; wine tastings available.

Signature wines: holzapfel-liebenberg-riesling-smaragd, holzapfel-achleiten-gruner-smaragd, holzapfel-achleiten-riesling-smaragd, holzapfel-gruner-smaragd-joching

Tip: Stay overnight at the Prandtauerhof and dine in the arcade courtyard for the full Wachau experience.

Prandtauerplatz 36, 3610 Joching, Austria → Book →

Weingut Höllmüller ★ 4.2

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Sauvignon Blanc8 haFounded 1920Joching

Multi-generation Joching estate evolving from grape production to quality self-marketing. 8 hectares across terraced Wachau sites; the attached Heurigen is popular with locals.

Tasting: Wine tavern (Heurigen) attached to the estate; walk-ins welcome.

Signature wines: hoellmueller-ried-pichl-gruner-smaragd, hoellmueller-sauvignon-blanc-wachau

Tip: The Sauvignon Blanc is a rarity in the Wachau and worth seeking out.

Joching 42, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Höllmüller Joching ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling5 haFounded 1956Joching

Small estate in Joching committed to ecological viticulture. The family developed from grape-growing into quality wine production in recent years.

Tasting: Seasonal Heurigen; walk-ins during open season.

Signature wines: hoellmueller-gruner-federspiel-joching

Tip: Look for this estate in local Joching wine festivals where small producers shine.

Hinterberg 6, 3610 Joching, Austria → Book →

Weingut Hutter Silberbichlerhof ★ 4.2

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Grauburgunder14 haFounded 1748Familie HutterMautern

Seven-generation Mautern estate documented to 1748, with 14 hectares on both banks of the Danube. A Vinea Wachau member producing Steinfeder, Federspiel and Smaragd across Grüner Veltliner, Riesling and Grauburgunder.

Tasting: Event venue (Silberbichlerhof) for private events; direct sales available.

Signature wines: hutter-loibenberg-riesling-smaragd, hutter-gruner-federspiel-mautern

Tip: The south-bank Mautern terroir adds an extra warmth to the Grüner Veltliners compared with the steep north-bank sites.

St. Pöltenerstraße 385, 3512 Mautern an der Donau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Hofstätter ★ 4.3

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Neuburger, Gelber Muskateller, Weissburgunder9 haFounded 1930Petra and Wolfgang HofstätterWinemaker Wolfgang HofstätterSpitz

Third-generation family estate at Spitz foot working 8.5 hectares from St. Michael to Schwallenbach. Singerriedel, Hartberg and Tausendeimerberg are key sites. Gelber Muskateller and Frühroter Veltliner add variety to the portfolio.

Tasting: Seasonal Heurigen (wine tavern) on site; direct sales available.

Signature wines: hofstaetter-singerriedel-riesling-smaragd, hofstaetter-tausendeimerberg-gruner-smaragd

Tip: The Gelber Muskateller Federspiel is one of the few compelling Muscats in the Wachau.

Hofstrasse 6, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Stift Göttweig ★ 4.3

Kremstal DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Weissburgunder30 haFounded 1083Benedictine Monastery of GöttweigFurth bei Göttweig

Benedictine monastery at Göttweig with viticulture documented since 1083. The 30-hectare estate is in Kremstal DAC at the eastern edge of the Wachau. Named Falstaff Winery of the Year 2020.

Tasting: Wine shop and vinothek at the monastery; combined monastery-and-wine tours.

Signature wines: stift-goettweig-kremstal-gruner-smaragd, stift-goettweig-privat-riesling

Tip: Combine the monastery tour with a wine tasting at the Vinothek for one of Lower Austria's great heritage experiences.

Stift Göttweig 1, 3511 Furth bei Göttweig, Austria → Book →

Winzer Krems ★ 4.0

Kremstal DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Chardonnay, Weissburgunder1200 haFounded 1938Krems

Major Kremstal cooperative representing 700 growers over 1,200 hectares. One of Austria's largest estate-bottled wine producers and a leading exporter. Based at Sandgrube 13 in Krems, the gateway city to the Wachau.

Tasting: Visitors centre at Krems; large tastings and group tours available year-round.

Signature wines: winzer-krems-sandgrube-gruner-smaragd, winzer-krems-riesling-pfaffenberg

Tip: The Sandgrube Grüner Veltliner Smaragd is among Austria's best-value quality whites; the visitor centre offers comprehensive tastings.

Sandgrube 13, 3500 Krems an der Donau, Austria → Book →

Weinhofmeisterei Mathias Hirtzberger ★ 4.3

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling4 haFounded 2014Mathias HirtzbergerWinemaker Mathias HirtzbergerWösendorf

Mathias Hirtzberger set up his own estate in Wösendorf in 2014, independent from the Franz Hirtzberger domaine. The Hofmeisterei restaurant attached to the winery is a wine-tourism draw.

Tasting: Restaurant and wine shop on site (Hofmeisterei); tastings combined with dining.

Signature wines: weinhofmeisterei-hirtzberger-gruner-smaragd, weinhofmeisterei-hirtzberger-riesling-federspiel

Tip: Book the Hofmeisterei restaurant for a combination of regional Wachau cuisine and Mathias's wines on the terrace.

Hauptstraße 142, 3610 Wösendorf in der Wachau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Özelt ★ 4.1

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Frühroter Veltliner, Chardonnay8 haFounded 1865Familie ÖzeltSpitz

Family estate in central Spitz since 1865, producing Grüner Veltliner, Riesling and uncommon varieties including Frühroter Veltliner and Chardonnay. Heurigen and guest rooms make it a complete Spitz base.

Tasting: Heurigen (wine tavern) and guest rooms at the estate; walk-ins welcome during open hours.

Signature wines: ozelt-gruner-smaragd-spitz, ozelt-riesling-federspiel-spitz

Tip: The Frühroter Veltliner is a historic Wachau variety rarely bottled elsewhere; worth trying at the Heurigen.

Kirchenplatz 3, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Mayer Spitz ★ 4.1

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Weissburgunder8 haFounded 1940Familie MayerWinemaker Franz Mayer juniorSpitz

Third-generation Franz Mayer estate in the Spitzer Graben producing award-winning wines to Vinea Wachau quality standards. The Spitzer Graben sub-valley gives distinctive floral and mineral character.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales daily from 16:00 (Sundays from 14:30); walk-ins welcome.

Signature wines: mayer-gruner-smaragd-spitzer-graben, mayer-riesling-federspiel-spitz

Tip: One of the few Wachau estates open daily in the late afternoon for impromptu visits.

Gut am Steg 11, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Mayr Joching ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling6 haFounded 1948Joching

Joching estate producing Grüner Veltliner and Riesling from the terraced sites flanking the historic village. A Vinea Wachau member.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales; seasonal open hours.

Tip: Joching's wine taverns in autumn during harvest season offer some of the most genuine local Wachau atmosphere.

Joching 55, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria →

Weingut Miesbauer ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling6 haFounded 1958Joching

Joching estate listed in the Vinea Wachau directory; works the local terrace sites producing Grüner Veltliner and Riesling in all three quality tiers.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales from the Joching estate.

Tip: Joching is among the most photogenic of the Wachau villages; the Miesbauer family wines pair well with the local asparagus season in spring.

Joching 38, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria →

Weingut Grabner Wachau ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling5 haFounded 1935Rossatz

South-bank Rossatz estate on the opposite shore of the Danube from the main Wachau wine villages. Produces Grüner Veltliner and Riesling from south-bank terroir which is slightly warmer than north-bank sites.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales; seasonal open hours.

Tip: The south bank of the Wachau is often bypassed by visitors; the warmer south-facing terroir produces rounder styles.

Rossatz 44, 3602 Rossatz-Arnsdorf, Austria →

Weingut Eder Wachau ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Chardonnay7 haFounded 1928Mautern

Mautern estate on the south bank of the Danube, listed in the Vinea Wachau directory. Produces classic Grüner Veltliner and Riesling from south-bank parcels.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales at the Mautern estate.

Tip: The Mautern cluster including Nikolaihof is underexplored; combine a visit here with the Nikolaihof nearby.

Wielandgasse 4, 3512 Mautern an der Donau, Austria →

Weingut Konrad ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling7 haFounded 1930Dürnstein

Dürnstein estate below the famous blue Baroque church tower and castle ruins. Works Loibenberg and Hollerin parcels producing Grüner Veltliner and Riesling.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales; by appointment.

Tip: Buy a bottle and walk up to the castle ruins for a Wachau picnic with an exceptional Danube view.

Dürnstein 58, 3601 Dürnstein, Austria →

Bioweingut Schmidl ★ 4.1

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling5 haFounded 1942Biodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDUnterloiben

Certified organic estate in Unterloiben operating under the Bioweingut designation. Listed in the Vinea Wachau directory; produces Grüner Veltliner and Riesling across all three quality tiers.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales; by appointment.

Tip: One of the few certified organic/biodynamic estates in the Loiben cluster; worth seeking for natural Wachau expression.

Unterloiben 33, 3601 Dürnstein, Austria →

Loibnerhof (Weingut Knoll) ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling4 haFounded 1820Unterloiben

Historic Unterloiben tavern and small wine estate associated with the Knoll family. The Loibnerhof address (Unterloiben 7) appears in merchant records alongside the Knoll estate; a notable Wachau hospitality point.

Tasting: Restaurant and tavern adjacent to the Knoll estate; meals with estate wines.

Tip: The Loibnerhof is a historic address in Wachau wine history; visit for the traditional atmosphere.

Unterloiben 7, 3601 Dürnstein, Austria →

Weingut Franz Hirtzberger ★ 4.9

Wachau DACRiesling, Grüner Veltliner20 haFounded 1788Winemaker Franz HirtzbergerSpitz

Five-generation estate in Spitz known for the Singerriedel Riesling from gneiss-granite terraces above Spitz. Honivogl Grüner Veltliner Smaragd is a warm-year, full-bodied expression of Wachau Veltliner.

Tasting: By appointment; winery and cellar visits available.

Signature wines: hirtzberger-singerriedel-riesling-smaragd, hirtzberger-honivogl-gruner-smaragd, hirtzberger-hochrain-riesling-smaragd

Tip: Singerriedel Riesling thrives with 8-12 years of bottle age; cool granite terroir preserves acidity for decades.

Kremserstraße 8, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria →

Weingut Rudi Pichler ★ 4.8

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Weissburgunder15 haFounded 1731Wösendorf

A separate family from F.X. Pichler, based in Wösendorf since 1731. The 15-hectare estate is planted 65% Grüner Veltliner, 30% Riesling. Known for textured Smaragd from the Hochrain and Achleiten sites.

Tasting: Direct sales (Ab Hof) and wine shop on site.

Signature wines: rudi-pichler-hochrain-riesling-smaragd, rudi-pichler-achleiten-gruner-smaragd, rudi-pichler-bruck-gruner-smaragd

Tip: The modern winery building opened in 2004 is architecturally significant; Achleiten Smaragd is the flagship.

Marienfeldweg 122, 3610 Wösendorf in der Wachau, Austria → Book →

Winery Veyder-Malberg ★ 4.7

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling5 haFounded 2008Peter Veyder-MalbergWinemaker Peter Veyder-MalbergBiodynamic PracticingORGANIC CERTIFIEDSpitz

Peter Veyder-Malberg founded his estate in 2008, farming ancient terraces in the Wachau entirely by hand. Biodynamic practices since inception; 5 hectares across Brandstatt, Hochrain and Viessling sites.

Tasting: By appointment only; small-production cellar door visits for trade.

Signature wines: veyder-malberg-viessling-gruner-smaragd, veyder-malberg-schoner-riesling-smaragd, veyder-malberg-brandstatt-gruner-federspiel

Tip: Veyder-Malberg's wines reward patience; the Viessling Smaragd is among the most structured Grüner Veltliners in the Wachau.

Vorderarnsdorf 10, 3610 Spitz an der Donau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Lagler ★ 4.4

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Gelber Muskateller, Neuburger, Weissburgunder14 haFounded 1789Familie LaglerSpitz

Family estate in Spitz since 1789, operating 14 hectares across several varieties. The attached Hotel Weinberghof is a wine-tourism destination with views over the vine-covered slopes.

Tasting: Hotel Weinberghof on site; tastings combined with hotel stays available.

Signature wines: lagler-drei-weingarten-riesling-smaragd, lagler-tausendeimerberg-gruner-smaragd

Tip: Pair a stay at the Hotel Weinberghof with the Tausendeimerberg Smaragd for the full Spitz experience.

Am Hinterweg 17, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Johann Donabaum ★ 4.5

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Neuburger14 haFounded 1996Winemaker Johann DonabaumSpitz

Johann Donabaum junior took over in 1996 and tripled the vineyard area with a focus on quality. Working sites in the Spitzer Graben on gneiss and granite soils, producing expressive Smaragd from the Spitzer Punkt site.

Tasting: Direct sales and tastings from the estate; wine shop open.

Signature wines: donabaum-spitzer-punkt-riesling-smaragd, donabaum-mariental-gruner-smaragd

Tip: Spitzer Punkt Riesling Smaragd shows the granitic character of the western Wachau at its most precise.

Feldgasse 34, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria → Book →

Weingut FJ Gritsch Mauritiushof ★ 4.4

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling15 haFounded 1835Spitz

Historic estate in central Spitz with 15 hectares across terraced sites including Singerriedel and Tausendeimerberg. The Mauritiushof visitor complex blends winery tourism with premium production.

Tasting: Wine experience world at Mauritiushof: tasting rooms, show cellar, event facilities, holiday apartments.

Signature wines: gritsch-singerriedel-riesling-smaragd, gritsch-tausendeimerberg-gruner-smaragd

Tip: The wine experience complex at Mauritiushof makes this one of the most visitor-friendly estates in Spitz.

Hauptstraße 66, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Schrey ★ 4.2

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling6 haFounded 1920Winemaker Rudi SchreyWösendorf

Family estate in Wösendorf working steep sites at Höll, Kirchweg, Kollmütz and Hochrain. A Vinea Wachau member committed to site-specific farming with active green cover management.

Tasting: Direct cellar-door sales; vineyard walk tastings by arrangement.

Signature wines: schrey-hochrain-riesling-smaragd, schrey-holl-gruner-federspiel

Tip: Vineyard walks with tasting can be arranged for groups wanting an off-the-beaten-path Wachau experience.

Prof.-Gruber-Gasse 37, 3610 Wösendorf in der Wachau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Erich Machherndl ★ 4.2

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Weissburgunder8 haFounded 1786Winemaker Erich MachherndlWösendorf

"Honest viticulture without compromise" is the motto of this 1786-founded estate in Wösendorf. Picturesque 13th-century farmhouse cellar; the family has been active in the region for over 200 years.

Tasting: Direct sales at the 13th-century farmhouse; Buschenschank seasonal tavern.

Signature wines: machherndl-gruner-smaragd-wosendorf, machherndl-riesling-federspiel-wosendorf

Tip: The historic farmhouse and barrel cellar offer a genuine old-Wachau atmosphere that larger estates cannot replicate.

Hauptstraße 1, 3610 Wösendorf, Austria → Book →

Weingut Josef & Georg Högl ★ 4.4

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling10 haFounded 1948Josef Högl and Georg HöglWinemaker Georg HöglSpitz

Father-and-son estate at Vießling on the western edge of the Wachau, where the Spitzer Graben valley opens toward the Waldviertel. 10 hectares planted 60% Grüner Veltliner, 35% Riesling, all white wine.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales (Ab Hof); by appointment for groups.

Signature wines: hoegl-viessling-gruner-smaragd, hoegl-spitzer-graben-riesling-smaragd

Tip: The westernmost position in the Wachau imparts a distinctive cooler-climate intensity to the Rieslings.

Vießling 31, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Schneeweiß ★ 4.2

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling5 haFounded 1932Moritz SchneeweissWinemaker Moritz SchneeweissWeissenkirchen

Family estate in the historic Salzstadl of Weissenkirchen producing award-winning Steinfeder, Federspiel and Smaragd. The unique ceramics programme by Sabine Schneeweiß adds an artisan dimension to visits.

Tasting: Wine shop at the historic Salzstadl (salt warehouse); holiday apartments on site.

Signature wines: schneeweis-steinriegl-riesling-smaragd, schneeweis-gruner-federspiel-weissenkirchen

Tip: Buy a bottle of Steinriegl Smaragd and a hand-thrown wine jug from the estate ceramics studio together.

Wachaustraße 233 Salzstadl, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Bruch ★ 4.2

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling6 haFounded 1910Martin BruchWinemaker Martin BruchWeissenkirchen

Fourth-generation St. Michael estate working steep terrace sites since 1910. Martin Bruch produces vivid, exhilarating whites from a focused range; a Vinea Wachau member.

Tasting: Direct sales at the estate; visits by appointment.

Signature wines: bruch-riesling-smaragd-stmichael, bruch-gruner-federspiel-wachau

Tip: The St. Michael sub-village is often overlooked; Bruch's Riesling from these sandy-gneiss soils has a distinctive floral lift.

St. Michael 5, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Erich Bayer ★ 4.1

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling7 haFounded 1848Erich and Arnold BayerWeissenkirchen

Family-run since 1848, now managed by Erich and Arnold since 2005. Seven hectares in St. Michael and Weissenkirchen producing Grüner Veltliner and Riesling across all three Vinea Wachau tiers.

Tasting: Wine shop and direct sales; seasonal Heurigen.

Signature wines: bayer-gruner-smaragd-stmichael, bayer-riesling-federspiel-wachau

Tip: One of the longer-established estates in St. Michael; the Smaragd Grüner Veltliner from old-vine parcels is the standout.

St. Michael 71, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Bernhard ★ 4.1

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling7 haFounded 1928Familie BernhardWeissenkirchen

Three-generation Weissenkirchen estate with 7 hectares in prime Wachau locations including Achleiten parcels. Also runs a guest house (Gästehaus) adjacent to the winery.

Tasting: Guest house attached to winery; wine tasting available for guests.

Signature wines: bernhard-achleiten-gruner-smaragd, bernhard-riesling-federspiel-weissenkirchen

Tip: Combine a stay at the guest house with tastings of the Achleiten Smaragd directly from the family.

Laimgrubgasse 158, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria → Book →

Weingut Forstreiter ★ 4.2

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Weissburgunder22 haFounded 1868Familie ForstreiterWinemaker Meinhard ForstreiterHollenburg

Family estate in Hollenburg at the eastern edge of the Wachau, family-owned since 1868. 22 hectares with reliable opening hours make it one of the most accessible Wachau producers.

Tasting: Open Monday-Friday 09:30-18:00, Saturday 09:30-14:00; direct cellar sales.

Signature wines: forstreiter-gruner-smaragd-hollenburg, forstreiter-riesling-federspiel-wachau

Tip: The most reliably open estate for walk-in cellar visits in the Wachau; no appointment needed on weekdays.

Obere Hollenburger Hauptstraße 36, 3506 Krems-Hollenburg, Austria → Book →

Weingut Gruber Wachau ★ 4.1

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling6 haFounded 1940Familie GruberWinemaker Georg GruberWeissenkirchen

Modern and creative Weissenkirchen estate with a strong direct-sales focus. Georg and Beate Gruber work 6 hectares producing Grüner Veltliner and Riesling under the Vinea Wachau charter.

Tasting: Direct sales and online shop; tastings by prior arrangement.

Signature wines: gruber-wachau-riesling-smaragd, gruber-wachau-gruner-federspiel

Tip: One of the younger-generation Wachau estates embracing direct-to-consumer sales; wines available via their online shop.

Grubstraße 241, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria → Book →

Herbert Högl Vogelleithen ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling4 haFounded 1960Herbert HöglWinemaker Herbert HöglSpitz

Small family estate at the Vogelleithen hamlet above Spitz, producing Grüner Veltliner and Riesling from terrace sites with direct Danube views. Listed in the Vinea Wachau directory.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales; by appointment.

Tip: The Vogelleithen hamlet is rarely visited; a hidden high-altitude Wachau perspective above the main valley floor.

Vogelleithen 10, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria →

Winzerhof Leonhartsberger ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Gelber Muskateller6 haFounded 1920Unterloiben

Small Unterloiben estate listed in the Vinea Wachau directory working Loibenberg-area parcels. Produces across the three Steinfeder-Federspiel-Smaragd tiers.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales (Ab Hof); seasonal open hours.

Tip: A genuinely local estate whose wines are rarely seen beyond the Wachau itself.

Unterloiben 22, 3601 Dürnstein, Austria →

Weingut Familie Piewald ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling6 haFounded 1945Familie PiewaldSpitz

Small family estate at Aggsbach-Dorf near the western Wachau boundary, listed in the Vinea Wachau producer directory. Works Grüner Veltliner and Riesling terraces.

Tasting: Direct sales from the estate.

Tip: The western terminus of the Wachau near Melk; the Piewalds are known locally for their honest, traditional Federspiel.

Aggsbach-Dorf 27, 3642 Aggsbach, Austria →

Wein und Obstbau Muthenthaler ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling4 haFounded 1950Spitz

Small Schwallenbach estate at the foot of the Spitz hillside also producing apricots (Marillen) alongside wine, a classic Wachau dual-crop operation.

Tasting: Seasonal direct sales; Buschenschank.

Tip: Schwallenbach is a quiet hamlet popular with cyclists; pair a Federspiel white with apricot jam from the estate.

Schwallenbach 10, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria →

Weingut Jäger ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling6 haFounded 1940Weissenkirchen

Weissenkirchen estate working the Steinriegl and Achleiten parcels on gneiss-dominated terraces. Listed in the Vinea Wachau directory; a reliable mid-size producer.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales in Weissenkirchen.

Tip: Weissenkirchen is one of the most scenic Wachau wine villages; walk the vineyard trail after your tasting.

Wachaustraße 50, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria →

Weingut Gebetsberger ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling7 haFounded 1960Spitz

Spitz estate working classic Grüner Veltliner and Riesling sites above the Danube. A Vinea Wachau producer committed to the Steinfeder-Federspiel-Smaragd tier system.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales; seasonal open hours.

Tip: A quiet producer in Spitz whose Federspiel Grüner is an everyday Wachau pleasure at a reasonable price.

Nussdorfer Straße 15, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria →

Weingut Weixelbaum ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling5 haFounded 1930Weissenkirchen

Weissenkirchen family estate working steep terrace sites above the Danube. Listed in the Vinea Wachau directory and the Weissenkirchen wine guide.

Tasting: Seasonal direct sales.

Tip: Among the smaller estates in Weissenkirchen worth exploring off the main tourist circuit.

Wachaustraße 108, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria →

Weingut Zottl ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling5 haFounded 1948Weissenkirchen

Weissenkirchen estate working Klaus and Steinriegl parcels; a smaller producer listed in the village wine guide and Vinea Wachau directory.

Tasting: Direct sales; walk-ins during open season.

Tip: Klaus Riesling from Weissenkirchen is a recognised village wine; the Zottl version offers an affordable entry.

Wachaustraße 193, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria →

Weingut Nothnagl ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling, Neuburger5 haFounded 1940Spitz

Small Spitz estate listed in the Vinea Wachau directory producing traditional Wachau whites including the rare Neuburger variety alongside Grüner Veltliner and Riesling.

Tasting: Direct cellar sales in Spitz.

Tip: Neuburger is a historic Wachau variety; Nothnagl is one of the few estates still producing it in any quantity.

Ober-Spitz 14, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria →

Weingut Ferdinand Denk ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling7 haFounded 1950Familie DenkWeissenkirchen

Weissenkirchen family estate working classic Achleiten and Klaus parcels. Listed in the Vinea Wachau and the local wine guide.

Tasting: Direct sales from the winery.

Tip: Weissenkirchen is the undisputed heart of Wachau white wine; any of the local estates here offers genuine terroir character.

Burgstraße 12, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria →

Weingut PAX ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling5 haFounded 1962Wösendorf

Wösendorf estate in the central Wachau near Rudi Pichler, listed in the Vinea Wachau directory. Produces Grüner Veltliner and Riesling Federspiel and Smaragd.

Tasting: Direct sales and seasonal Heurigen in Wösendorf.

Tip: Wösendorf is one of the smallest Wachau villages with one of the highest concentrations of quality producers per capita.

Hauptstraße 71, 3610 Wösendorf in der Wachau, Austria →

Weingut Stalzer ★ 4.0

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling5 haFounded 1955Spitz

Spitz estate with attached Heurigen tavern listed in the Vinea Wachau directory and the Spitz municipal wine guide.

Tasting: Heurigen (Stallzer wine tavern) open seasonally.

Tip: An atmospheric Spitz Heurigen for classic Grüner Veltliner Federspiel and local Wachau snacks.

Kellergasse 5, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria →

Domäne Roland Chan ★ 4.2

Wachau DACGrüner Veltliner, Riesling5 haFounded 2012Roland ChanWinemaker Roland ChanORGANIC CERTIFIEDWeissenkirchen

Boutique St. Michael estate run by Roland Chan, focusing on organic viticulture and minimal-intervention winemaking. Listed on the Vinea Wachau producer directory.

Tasting: By appointment only; small-production estate.

Signature wines: chan-gruner-smaragd-stmichael

Tip: One of the few Wachau estates run by an owner from outside the traditional winemaking families; small-batch releases.

St. Michael 27, 3610 Weissenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria →
